data.summary: Consolidate marketplace into colton-claude-plugins
data.detail: Consolidate marketplace into colton-claude-plugins
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Marketplace consolidated to colton-claude-plugins; this repo ships no marketplace.json
data.detail: Two marketplace.json files were both named "coltondyck" (one in this repo, one in Haagndaazer/colton-claude-plugins). Claude Code keys marketplaces by name, so they collided — only one could register per machine. Chose colton-claude-plugins (Silvie-maintained) as the single source of truth and deleted the in-repo file rather than renaming it.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Never add a marketplace.json named "coltondyck" to the vibe-cognition repo
data.detail: Claude Code keys marketplaces by name. A second file named "coltondyck" collides with the canonical one in colton-claude-plugins — a machine can only register one marketplace per name. If a local-dev marketplace is ever needed here, it must use a distinct name (e.g. teammate-comms uses "colton-comms").
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Declare MCP server in plugin.json; drop per-project .mcp.json, v0.6.0
data.detail: Declare MCP server in plugin.json; drop per-project .mcp.json, v0.6.0
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Project-scope .mcp.json OUTRANKS plugin-provided MCP servers; CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR auto-injected into stdio servers
data.detail: Claude Code keys MCP servers by name and uses the highest-precedence source (local > project .mcp.json > user > plugin-provided), without merging. So a stale per-project vibe-cognition entry would keep winning over a plugin.json declaration — it must be removed. Separately, Claude Code auto-injects CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR into every spawned stdio server's env (read directly, no mapping needed), and SessionStart hooks CANNOT set env vars for an already-spawned MCP server.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: MCP server declared in plugin.json (not per-project .mcp.json); venv relocated to ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}
data.detail: Replaced the hook-written per-project .mcp.json with a plugin.json mcpServers declaration using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} + REPO_PATH=${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}. Moved the uv venv out of the version-pinned cache dir into ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/.venv so a running server stops locking the cache dir during Windows /plugin update (EPERM). Eliminates the restart dance and per-version config churn.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.6.0: Move MCP setup into plugin.json + relocate venv to fix Windows update lock
data.detail: Reworked how the vibe-cognition plugin sets up its MCP server per project. Previously hooks/session-start.sh wrote a per-project .mcp.json hard-coding the version-pinned plugin cache path and an absolute REPO_PATH, which forced a restart on install and on every update, churned config on version bumps, and caused a Windows EPERM lock during /plugin update (the running server held the .venv inside the versioned cache dir). Verified via claude-code-guide that: plugins can declare mcpServers in plugin.json with ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} interpolation; Claude Code auto-injects CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR into spawned stdio servers; hooks cannot set env for already-running servers; and project-scope .mcp.json OUTRANKS plugin-provided definitions (so a stale entry must be actively removed). Implementation: (1) plugin.json declares the server; (2) config.py defaults repo_path to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR then cwd; (3) session-start.sh relocates the venv to ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/.venv and now surgically removes any stale per-project vibe-cognition entry via the new vibe_cognition.migrate_mcp module (only that key touched; preserves all other servers/keys; no-ops on missing/malformed/absent; never deletes the file); (4) docs updated; (5) this repo's own .mcp.json untracked + gitignored. All 115 tests pass (8 new), ruff clean, pyright clean. Committed dfcd556, version bumped to 0.6.0, pushed to main. RELEASE GATED on a real Windows /plugin update test (no EPERM) and confirming ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} interpolation before Silvie's marketplace re-pin is treated as final.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Auto-curate after recording; fix stale skill name, v0.6.1
data.detail: Auto-curate after recording; fix stale skill name, v0.6.1
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: vibe-cognition skill auto-runs /vibe-curate as a Final Step after any recording turn
data.detail: Users kept having to remind the agent to curate after logging nodes. Added a Final Step to the vibe-cognition skill: if any nodes were recorded this turn (always after an episode), run /vibe-curate before finishing, unprompted. Chose end-of-turn cadence over per-record (too noisy) or episode-only (misses stray entities). Cheap because /vibe-curate only processes uncurated nodes. Also fixed a stale /curate-edges reference (skill is /vibe-curate).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Prove-the-safe-path-first: file-touching migrations get a dry-run/report-only mode, not just test-asserted safety
data.detail: Adopted from Ian's resume-safety gate (prove the undo path before restructuring a working system). vibe-cognition migrations that edit user files — e.g. migrate_mcp stripping the stale .mcp.json entry — should offer a dry-run/report-only path and surface "removed vibe-cognition, preserved <other servers>" at runtime, so the safety guarantee is demonstrable on the user's real artifact, not only asserted by the test suite. Concrete next step: add that mode to migrate_mcp + hook output.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Install-mechanics fixes can't be self-verified — no headless plugin install/update harness, so releases gate on a human's machine
data.detail: The v0.6.0 Windows EPERM fix (venv relocation + plugin-declared server) is install-layer behavior that can't be validated without a real Claude Code install→run→update cycle, so the release gated entirely on Colton's Windows box. A sandboxed "install plugin, start it, trigger /plugin update, report locks" harness would let the maintainer self-verify install-mechanics fixes. Flagged as a tool-gap in the 2026-05-29 team meeting — shared theme across projects: let agents self-verify what currently only the human can check.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Post-commit hook uses FULL commit SHAs but manual records use short SHAs → deterministic part_of matching misses, graph fragments
data.detail: The PostToolUse post-commit hook auto-creates episode nodes referencing the full 40-char commit SHA (e.g. commit:dfcd556c4ad...), while manual cognition_record calls and the skill examples use short SHAs (commit:dfcd556). Deterministic edge matching compares reference strings exactly, so the hook's episodes don't link to the hand-authored entities for the same commit — producing parallel short-SHA and full-SHA islands (e.g. two separate v0.6.0 episodes). Fix candidate: normalize commit refs (match on SHA prefix) in the deterministic matcher and/or have the hook record short SHAs.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fix SHA-mismatch graph fragmentation in the deterministic MATCHER (prefix-normalize refs), not the writer — witnessed in 3 projects
data.detail: The short-vs-full SHA fragmentation (discovery 55e963b39354) is confirmed across 3 graphs today: vibe-cognition (mine), TestSVN (Isla), teammate-comms (Silvie). The short side comes from BOTH the hook-vs-manual asymmetry AND agents hand-typing 7-char SHAs, so the fix must live in the deterministic edge matcher: canonicalize both reference sides before comparing (prefix-match short↔full, or rev-parse both to full at match time). Fixing only the hook's output would miss agent-entered short refs. Regression cases offered: Silvie's hand-wired v0.4.0→0.4.4 edges, Isla's short-ref TestSVN nodes (8f0b803, a5e8d0a, d6ed150, 122f135).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: FALSE ALARM: "SHA-mismatch graph fragmentation" does not exist — matcher already prefix-matches short↔full
data.detail: I asserted (and told Isla + Silvie) that the post-commit hook's full 40-char SHAs fail to part_of-match manual short SHAs, fragmenting the graph. WRONG. _normalize_refs (storage.py) already emits a 7-char prefix key commit:<first7> for any ref >=7 chars, so short and full collapse to a shared key. Verified three ways: reproduced both add-orders AND the cross-process case (all link), and inspected the REAL graph — all six short-ref nodes ARE part_of the full-ref hook episode 0849f99433f4. Root error: I read get_uncurated ("not skill-reviewed") as "edgeless" and never ran get_neighbors to check the edges existed. Silvie's "8 hand-wired led_to edges" were never deterministic anyway — the matcher only creates part_of, never led_to, so that was expected, not a symptom. Three people "corroborated" a bug nobody verified. Lesson: verify-don't-assert applies to bug REPORTS, not just code — check the edges before declaring a graph fragmented.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Corroboration off an unverified claim isn't verification — check the primary artifact, not the echo
data.detail: A verify-don't-assert miss can stack across a team into false confidence: one agent asserts from a proxy signal (here, reading get_uncurated as "edgeless" without running get_neighbors), teammates "confirm" off that claim instead of the primary, and a lead reports "≥2 graphs confirmed" as proof. Two+ agents agreeing can all be wrong if none checked the primary. For cognition-graph claims the primary is the actual edges (get_neighbors), not record-time counts or another agent's word. Each corroborator must independently hit the primary, or it's one assertion echoed N times and mistaken for evidence.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: migrate_mcp: dry-run mode + surface what was removed/preserved, v0.6.2
data.detail: migrate_mcp: dry-run mode + surface what was removed/preserved, v0.6.2
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.6.2: migrate_mcp --dry-run + surface removed/preserved at runtime
data.detail: Implemented the prove-safe-path-first follow-up (pattern 3a4534a5d2d3). The per-project .mcp.json migration removed its stale entry safely but invisibly — the 'only vibe-cognition removed, everything else preserved' guarantee lived only in tests. Made it demonstrable. Changes: (1) remove_server_entry now returns {status, removed, preserved, dry_run}; preserved snapshots surviving servers in file order, never our key; [] for missing/skip, other servers for absent. (2) New --dry-run: reports what would change, writes nothing (not even the .tmp). (3) Hook captures a one-line note on real removals and routes it through prime via VIBE_MIGRATION_NOTE, so it's surfaced ALONGSIDE context injection — prime now self-guards (emits when note OR .cognition dir present), so we no longer drop prime context on the migration session. Bash capture hardened for set -e. Peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent (approve-with-changes): the two required changes — don't drop prime context, and harden the capture — were adopted; skill-doc nudge deferred as separate scope. Smoke-testing caught a real bug: an em-dash in the note could raise UnicodeEncodeError on Windows stdout and silently swallow the note (hook's || ""), so the note is ASCII-only now. Tests: 16 migrate tests (added dry-run-writes-nothing incl. no .tmp, preserved ordering, per-status shape, CLI). Full suite 123 green; ruff/pyright clean on changed files (2 pre-existing prime.py UP017 left as out-of-scope tech debt). Committed 5c21212, pushed to main.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: migrate_mcp: never write bare {} (Claude Code rejects it); repair, don't delete, v0.6.3
data.detail: migrate_mcp: never write bare {} (Claude Code rejects it); repair, don't delete, v0.6.3
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: migrate_mcp wrote invalid {} .mcp.json when vibe-cognition was the only server → Claude Code parse error
data.detail: Reported by Colton: a project (TestSVN) showed "[Failed to parse] ... mcpServers: Invalid input: expected record, received undefined" and the removed server still listed. Confirmed the file was literally {}. Root cause: migrate_mcp popped the mcpServers key when it emptied (code present since v0.6.0), producing {}; Claude Code's schema requires mcpServers to be a record, so {} fails to parse and Claude Code falls back to a cached config still showing the old entry. Affected any project where vibe-cognition was the only MCP server. Fix (v0.6.3, 3201cca): never write bare {} — removal leaves {"mcpServers": {}}; absent-case contentless/invalid files ({} or {"mcpServers": null}) are repaired by REWRITING to {"mcpServers": {}} (not deleted), self-healing the fleet on next SessionStart; never-delete invariant kept. Peer review steered me off a deletion approach (symlink/locked-file/provenance hazards) to leave-empty-record. Hotfixed TestSVN by removing its {} vestige.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.6.3: fix migrate_mcp emitting invalid {} .mcp.json; leave valid empty record + self-repair
data.detail: Fixed the incident where migrate_mcp left a bare {} (invalid to Claude Code) when vibe-cognition was the only server. Changed remove_server_entry to keep mcpServers as a valid (possibly empty) record instead of popping it, so an emptied config is {"mcpServers": {}} not {}. Added self-repair: when our entry is absent but the file is a contentless invalid shape ({} or {"mcpServers": null}) with no other top-level keys, rewrite to {"mcpServers": {}} (new status "repaired") — heals already-damaged projects on next SessionStart. Files with other content/servers or already-valid empty records are left untouched; nothing is ever deleted (invariant preserved). Peer-reviewed: the reviewer returned "revise" and argued leave-empty-record over my initial delete-contentless approach (deletion risked symlinks, locked files on Windows, and deleting files we didn't author); adopted it. Notes surfaced via the existing prime path, ASCII-only. Tests: updated the two that assumed mcpServers gets dropped, added repair/no-op/whitespace cases; full suite 129 green; ruff/pyright clean on changed files. Hotfixed Colton's TestSVN by removing its {} vestige. Committed 3201cca, pushed; v0.6.3.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Converge concurrent sessions: catch up on the shared journal per-op, v0.7.0
data.detail: Converge concurrent sessions: catch up on the shared journal per-op, v0.7.0
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Multi-agent cognition diverges: each MCP server process hydrates its in-memory graph from journal.jsonl once at startup and never re-ingests another process's appends
data.detail: Two agents on one project = two stdio server processes sharing one .cognition/journal.jsonl. Each builds its NetworkX graph at its own startup; neither re-reads journal lines the other appends. Symptoms: get_status shows only your nodes, get_neighbors 404s the other author's id, add_edges_batch skips cross-author edges with a misleading "Missing to_id". cognition_search works across both ONLY because ChromaDB is a shared on-disk store while the graph is per-process memory. Confirmed in code at storage.py: __init__ hydrates once, no re-ingest path. Reported by Claudius from a two-agent run on rental-dispute.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Fix multi-agent divergence with lazy byte-offset journal catch-up under the lock (reentrancy-guarded _synced), not a background watcher or sqlite rewrite
data.detail: Chose lazy catch-up: at the start of every public storage op, replay journal lines appended since last read; advance the offset ONLY past complete newline-terminated lines so a concurrent writer's half-written final line is re-read next pass, not lost (peer review caught this data-loss bug in the first draft). Read binary so byte offset matches stat().st_size (CRLF on Windows). Replay is idempotent so re-applying our own writes is harmless. Rejected: background file-watcher thread (extra lifecycle, same result) and sqlite single-writer store (large architectural change — deferred). Also routed queries.get_history_for_context and dashboard get_graph through synced accessors, and added a cognition_reload diagnostic tool. Write-race on concurrent appends left out of scope (torn line tolerated; serialization is the future sqlite step).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Incremental journal tailing must advance its offset only to the last complete newline — reading to EOF can swallow a concurrent writer's half-written final line forever
data.detail: A startup one-shot replay can skip a torn trailing line harmlessly, but an INCREMENTAL catch-up that advances offset to EOF will seek past the middle of a not-yet-complete append next pass and lose that entry permanently. Mitigation: rfind(b"\n"), only consume through the last newline, re-read the tail once the writer completes it. General rule for append-log tailing across processes. Caught by peer review on the v0.7.0 catch-up fix.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Remove background LLM auto-curator; curation is now agent-driven, v0.7.0
data.detail: Remove background LLM auto-curator; curation is now agent-driven, v0.7.0
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Removed the background LLM auto-curator entirely; curation is now agent-driven — only deterministic part_of edges are automatic, all semantic edges come from the agent running /vibe-curate
data.detail: The opt-in CognitionCurator (Ollama/qwen, disabled by default) was unreliable, so it was deleted (class + tests + config fields + server wiring + get_status block). New operating model demarcated positively across tool docstrings, both skills, and README: deterministic part_of (shared refs) is the ONLY automatic edge; semantic curation (led_to/supersedes/contradicts/resolved_by) is the agent's responsibility via /vibe-curate after recording. Kept historical source="curator" provenance tags (commented) since they describe edge origin in existing journals. Rejected keeping-but-disabling the file (leaves dead code + a lingering mention).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.7.0: multi-agent journal convergence + removal of the background auto-curator (agent-driven curation)
data.detail: Two-part release triggered by Claudius's bug report from a two-agent run on rental-dispute. PART 1 (commit 9ad4d2f): fixed multi-agent cognition divergence — each MCP server process hydrated its in-memory graph from journal.jsonl once at startup and never re-ingested another process's appends, so concurrent agents forked in memory while search worked across both (shared ChromaDB). Fix: lazy byte-offset journal catch-up under the lock via a reentrancy-guarded _synced() context manager, run at the start of every public storage op. Peer review caught a data-loss bug in the first draft (advancing the offset to EOF would swallow a concurrent writer's half-written final line) — fixed by advancing only to the last complete newline. Read binary so byte offset matches stat().st_size (CRLF on Windows). Also routed queries.get_history_for_context and dashboard get_graph through synced accessors (snapshot()), and added a cognition_reload diagnostic tool. New TestJournalCatchUp covers convergence, cross-author edges, no double-count, truncation rehydrate, torn-line eventual ingestion, corrupt-line skip. PART 2 (commit 7395672): removed the unreliable background LLM auto-curator entirely (CognitionCurator/Ollama) per Colton; curation is now explicitly the agent's job via /vibe-curate, with only deterministic part_of edges automatic. Demarcated the new model positively across tool docstrings, both skills, and README; hardened the vibe-cognition skill to a mandatory MUST-run-/vibe-curate Final Step. Both parts ship as v0.7.0. Out of scope (flagged): concurrent-append write race and a single-writer/sqlite store. Verified headlessly (125 tests, two-live-instance convergence tests, import smoke); install-mechanics not self-verifiable per the standing constraint.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Bake standing cognition practices into the server as MCP instructions, v0.7.0
data.detail: Bake standing cognition practices into the server as MCP instructions, v0.7.0
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Baked the two standing cognition rules (check-history-before-task, record-as-you-work) into the server via the FastMCP instructions field, so they're plugin defaults for every project/user, not per-project CLAUDE.md edits
data.detail: Chose FastMCP `instructions=` (surfaced by Claude Code under "MCP Server Instructions", same mechanism teammate-comms uses) over SessionStart prime injection — server instructions fire even on a brand-new project with no .cognition/ dir, which is exactly when the rules need to be heard; prime self-suppresses without a graph. Text kept ASCII-only (Windows mojibake safety), scoped to "non-trivial work" to avoid nagging on trivial fixes, and consistent with the v0.7.0 agent-driven curation model. Peer review confirmed the mechanism (FastMCP 3.1.1 installed, instructions propagate to the MCP initialize result) and flagged the em-dash/ASCII hazard.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Re-inject standing cognition rules after a compact, v0.7.0
data.detail: Re-inject standing cognition rules after a compact, v0.7.0
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Re-inject server instructions after a compact via a SessionStart matcher:compact hook, since MCP instructions may not survive compaction (undocumented)
data.detail: MCP server instructions (FastMCP instructions field) are NOT in Claude Code's documented "survives compaction" list, so they may be dropped on compact. Added a second SessionStart hook gated by matcher:compact that runs python -m vibe_cognition.instructions and re-emits the rules as additionalContext. Chose this (Approach B) over editing session-start.sh to parse stdin source (Approach A) for declarative gating + separation of concerns. Moved SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS into a new stdlib-only instructions.py (single source of truth, import-light so the hook stays fast). The reinject script replicates session-start.sh's plugin-data venv resolution (UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT+cygpath) and uses capture-then-print for set -e safety. Benign failure mode if a plugin matcher were ignored (labeled block appears on every source). Post-compact injection only observable live (run /compact).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Claude Code SessionStart fires on compaction with source=compact (sources: startup/resume/clear/compact); MCP server instructions are NOT in the documented "survives compaction" set, so they may be dropped
data.detail: Verified via claude-code-guide against Claude Code docs: SessionStart supports a matcher of "compact" to fire only after /compact or auto-compaction; hooks inject post-compact context via stdout JSON {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"..."}}; PreCompact runs before and cannot add post-compact context. Crucially, MCP server instructions (FastMCP instructions field) do NOT appear in the docs' "what survives compaction" table (unlike project-root CLAUDE.md which is re-injected) — so a plugin that relies on them must re-inject after a compact. Reusable platform fact for any MCP/plugin work.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: TEST NODE
data.detail: Test node created for debugging the cognition graph write path.

data.summary: Add cognition_remove_node MCP tool (delete node + cascade edges), v0.7.1
data.detail: Add cognition_remove_node MCP tool (delete node + cascade edges), v0.7.1
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Exposed node deletion as cognition_remove_node MCP tool; extracted a shared delete_cognition_node helper used by both the tool and the dashboard endpoint
data.detail: Node deletion already existed end-to-end but only via the dashboard HTTP endpoint (storage.remove_node cascades incident edges via NetworkX + journals a remove_node tombstone that re-cascades on cross-process replay; delete_embedding purges the ChromaDB vector). The gap was agent reachability — no MCP tool. Chose to extract the deletion contract into a shared HTTP/MCP-agnostic helper (cognition/operations.py:delete_cognition_node) consumed by BOTH the dashboard endpoint and the new tool, rather than inline-mirroring 6 lines (peer review steered me here — single source for the contract, directly unit-testable). Named the tool cognition_remove_node for 1:1 symmetry with the existing cognition_remove_edge tool and the remove_node storage method (rejected cognition_delete_node despite the dashboard's 'Delete node' UI copy). Tool returns orphaned-edge detail (from/to/type list) not a bare count, and does NOT surface delete_embedding's boolean (it returns True even when nothing was deleted). Single-node only — destructive and not undoable; skill steers toward a supersedes edge for outdated-but-real nodes. Known accepted risk (mirrors dashboard): tombstone is journaled before the embedding purge, so a crash between them could leave a stale vector surfacing in search.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: v0.7.1: cognition_remove_node MCP tool — agent-facing node deletion with edge cascade
data.detail: Follow-up to v0.7.0 — Colton wanted node deletion in the toolset and it had been missed. Investigation found deletion was already fully implemented and tested, but only behind the dashboard HTTP DELETE endpoint: storage.remove_node (storage.py:163) cascades incident-edge removal in-memory via NetworkX, journals a single remove_node tombstone, and _replay_entry (storage.py:766) re-cascades that on cross-process catch-up; embeddings/storage.py:delete_embedding purges the ChromaDB vector with no embedding generator needed. The only missing piece was an MCP tool. Plan was peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent (verdict APPROVE with refinements, all adopted): (1) extract a shared delete_cognition_node helper in a new cognition/operations.py used by both the dashboard endpoint and the tool — single source for the deletion contract, directly unit-testable; (2) name the tool cognition_remove_node for symmetry with cognition_remove_edge / the remove_node storage method (not cognition_delete_node); (3) return orphaned-edge detail {from,to,type} not a bare count; (4) do not surface delete_embedding's unreliable boolean. The tool is single-node and destructive; the vibe-cognition skill now warns it cascades all attached edges and steers toward a supersedes edge for outdated-but-real nodes. Refactored the dashboard delete_node endpoint to call the helper. Tests: TestDeleteCognitionNode (cascade of in+out edges, missing-node -> None, non-fatal embedding failure) and a TestJournalCatchUp case proving a delete converges across two live processes (node + incident edge gone in the other instance after catch-up). Verified: 129 pytest green, ruff clean on changed files (only pre-existing baseline UP017/E741 remain, not introduced here), tool registration confirmed live (15 tools, cognition_remove_node present). README 14->15, skill note added, version 0.7.0->0.7.1 in plugin.json + pyproject.toml. Committed bd741bb, pushed to main. Pending: ping Silvie to re-pin the marketplace at bd741bb.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Release procedure: marketplace pinning is now Loki's, not Silvie's
data.detail: Release procedure: marketplace pinning is now Loki's, not Silvie's
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Journal appends are not cross-process atomic: buffered TextIOWrapper can interleave large lines from two processes; _catch_up then skips both as malformed — silent data loss
data.detail: storage.py:607 uses a buffered text append; lines over ~8KiB (episodes) flush as multiple OS writes, so two concurrent writers can interleave chunks. No fcntl/msvcrt lock exists anywhere. Fix inside the settled catch-up design: single os.write() on an O_APPEND fd, optionally a byte-range lock. Found by 2026-06-10 audit; no prior graph node ever considered append atomicity.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Node↔embedding drift is structural: post-commit hook and cross-process replay never embed; only server restart repairs — observed live as 37 nodes vs 35 embeddings
data.detail: Embeddings are written only at record time and backfilled once at startup (server.py:128-135); journal replay has no embedding path, post-commit.py appends raw journal lines, and cognition_reload doesn't resync. v0.7.0 deliberately scoped convergence to the graph only (ChromaDB staleness was named in discovery 55b6740e42f8) — this is the known-deferred half. Cheapest levers: resync in cognition_reload or embed-on-replay.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: ChromaDB telemetry is enabled by default (no Settings(anonymized_telemetry=False)) and documents are embedded with the QUERY prefix — both unexamined since initial build
data.detail: embeddings/storage.py:28 creates PersistentClient with no settings, so PostHog telemetry phones home from every user's project. cognition_tools.py:67 and server.py:80 call generate_query_embedding for documents, discarding nomic's asymmetric search_document:/search_query: training; self-consistent so search works, but ranking is degraded and fixing requires a one-time re-embed (vector spaces incompatible).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Distribution blind spots: post-commit hook runs on bare `python` (silent failure where absent), vibe-curate skill uses repo-relative subagent paths that break installed, no LICENSE/CI, httpx is a dead dep
data.detail: hooks.json:31 invokes bare python (only uv is guaranteed); vibe-curate SKILL.md points at skills/vibe-curate/edge-analyzer.md which doesn't resolve from a user project cwd; MIT declared in both manifests but no LICENSE file ships; no .github/ so 129 passing tests never run on push; httpx has zero imports repo-wide. All pre-date the graph — genuine oversights, not decided trade-offs.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Full toolset audit of vibe-cognition v0.7.1 → docs/AUDIT-2026-06-10.md (identify-only, ~70 findings)
data.detail: Colton asked for a complete take-stock of the toolset: improvements, dead code, gaps, issues — identify only, no implementation. Method: plan peer-reviewed by a general-purpose subagent (which merged the embeddings area into core, added verbatim-snippet evidence rules, a cross-cutting Windows/error-handling/stdout checklist, and verify-deps-by-grep), then 5 parallel read-only review agents (core+embeddings, MCP tools/server, dashboard, distribution/lifecycle, docs/skills/tests) plus a fresh ruff (23) / pyright (31) / pytest (129 pass) baseline. Per Colton's mid-task instruction, every major finding got a provenance pass through the cognition graph before its recommendation, separating deliberate-decision residue (catch-up scoped to graph only; curator-removal journal compat) from genuine oversights (append atomicity, telemetry, query-prefix embedding, post-commit hook wiring — all pre-graph history). Top findings: non-atomic cross-process journal appends (high), vibe-curate skill subagent paths break when installed (high), post-commit hook on bare python (high), ChromaDB telemetry on, structural node↔embedding drift (the live 37v35), agents/plan.md self-contradiction + namespace mismatch, docs embedded with query prefix, journal same-size replacement defeats offset check, zero tool-layer tests, no LICENSE/CI. Also catalogued dead code (code-search heritage in embeddings/storage.py, redirect_edges/DUPLICATE_OF curator-merge residue, generate_batch, httpx) and verified-accurate docs (tool count 15, migrate_mcp exemplary, curator removal propagated cleanly). Output: docs/AUDIT-2026-06-10.md with severity-sorted priorities, per-area findings with file:line evidence, provenance notes, coverage map, and a suggested (not started) fix ordering.

data.summary: Added standing rule 3 VALIDATE SUGGESTIONS AGAINST HISTORY to the injected server instructions (session start + post-compact), as a separate rule rather than folded into CHECK HISTORY FIRST, v0.7.2
data.detail: Colton wanted every improvement/fix suggestion validated against the graph to understand how the current state came to be — the provenance discipline used in the 2026-06-10 audit, made a plugin default. Kept as a third rule (not merged into rule 1) because it fires at a different moment: rule 1 triggers at task start, rule 3 whenever a suggestion is proposed mid-work, and its verb is interpret-before-recommending (deliberate decision vs oversight), not search-before-acting. One edit in instructions.py covers both injection points (FastMCP instructions field + compact-matched SessionStart hook).

Peer review added the journal rule: .cognition/journal.jsonl is git-committed now, so WP branches must never include it (avoids merge conflicts and the C-3 replay hazard); vince owns journal commits on main. Vorpid records his own cognition nodes; vince spot-checks during review.

data.summary: Journal rule reversed (Colton): implementer commits journal updates on WP branches in dedicated commits; merge safety via .gitattributes merge=union + cognition_reload after merges until C-3 lands
data.detail: The original no-journal-on-branches rule protected against merge conflicts and the C-3 stale-offset hazard, but left node appends permanently uncommitted — one stash/reset/discarded worktree and the implementer's recorded history is silently destroyed (worse: a journal shrink triggers full rebuild, erasing the nodes everywhere). Reversal keeps the data safe and moves the safety to the merge: union driver is correct for append-only JSONL with unique node IDs; dedicated journal: commits keep code review clean; C-3 promoted into WP-4 since branch merges make the hazard live.

data.summary: Shared-worktree journal protocol: NOBODY commits .cognition/journal.jsonl on a WP branch; manager flushes live journal to main via a temp git worktree at each checkpoint. Hard destructive-op ban near the journal.
data.detail: vince and Vorpid share ONE working tree. Committing the journal on a WP branch means a later branch switch rewrites the live journal under running servers — reproduces C-3 (stale-offset replay) with no merge involved. Rule: record nodes freely (they persist to disk), but never stage the journal on a WP branch. Manager flushes via `git worktree add` → cp journal → commit on main → remove, at checkpoints (plan/diff/merge). BAN: no git reset --hard, checkout -- ., stash, or clean that could touch the journal without pinging the manager to flush first. .gitattributes merge=union stays as defense-in-depth.

data.summary: Merge-pin voiding clause cuts BOTH ways: implementer has the duty (not just option) to HOLD a merge and void the manager's SHA approval on any material post-sign-off info — flaky re-run, later-branch composition effect, etc. Hold first, root-cause, then report; no permission needed.
data.detail: vince's standing amendment (lesson from Silvie's pipeline, where it stopped a bad merge). An approval is a snapshot of what was known, not a command to merge. Complements the existing rule that any post-approval commit voids the approval — same principle, implementer's side.

data.summary: WP-4 (concurrency/journal-atomicity) acceptance criteria: (1) N=5 consecutive green full-suite runs before pin — single green proves nothing about timing-dependent code; (2) any test where a worker thread can die mid-run must collect worker exceptions and assert the collection empty as its own labeled assertion.
data.detail: vince's pre-WP-4 standing rules. Rationale: timing bugs pass intermittently, so one green run is not evidence; and a silently-dead worker thread otherwise surfaces as a baffling count mismatch two assertions later instead of a clear failure. Write both into WP-4 acceptance criteria (the cross-process append test for C-1/C-3).

data.summary: C-3's category is wider than git pull/merge: ANY clean-filtered rewrite (e.g. `git checkout -- journal` under core.autocrlf) can byte-rewrite a content-identical journal under the servers' byte offsets — our tree dodges it only because autocrlf=true smudge coincides with Python's CRLF writes
data.detail: Verified live: journal stored i/lf, working tree w/crlf, autocrlf=true, live file uniformly CRLF — today's alignment checkout was byte-identical by configuration luck. On autocrlf=input/false or Linux, a content-no-op checkout rewrites every line one byte shorter under a byte-based replay offset. Fix adopted (via Silvie): `.cognition/journal.jsonl -text` in .gitattributes (Vorpid's WP-2), making content-equal byte-equal; blob renormalizes LF→CRLF at next manager flush, safe for our binary splitlines replay. Until merged: no per-file journal checkouts without byte verification.

data.summary: uv ignores [tool.uv.sources] for purely-transitive deps — to pin torch (transitive via sentence-transformers) to the pytorch-cpu index, torch MUST be declared a direct [project.dependencies] entry. Otherwise uv resolves it from PyPI regardless.
data.detail: Verified live: with torch only transitive, `[tool.uv.sources] torch={index="pytorch-cpu"}` was silently ignored (uv pulled torch from pypi.org with the CUDA stack). Declaring `torch==2.11.0` direct made the source apply and dropped 18 GPU-only packages. Index source pinning only reaches declared deps.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: uv's --torch-backend/UV_TORCH_BACKEND is a `uv pip` feature ONLY — `uv sync` rejects it. And uv.lock pinned the CUDA stack (42 nvidia/cuda pkgs, sys_platform==linux), so `uv sync` installs CUDA on Linux regardless of any env. CPU torch needs a lock-level index source, not a sync env flag.
data.detail: Peer review caught this; reproduced: `uv sync --torch-backend cpu` errors, `uv sync --help` has no such option. The env-on-sync approach is a silent no-op. Invisible locally because Windows resolves the CPU wheel anyway; only Linux pulls CUDA. Don't assert a slim-install mechanism — verify it in the CI run.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: CI pyright baseline-count ratchet: commit a baseline file (=current error count); CI fails only if pyright's count EXCEEDS it, warns when it drops (lower it in the same PR). Monotonic to zero, then delete the file → strict. Near-zero machinery; avoids identity-level diffing that breaks on line shifts.
data.detail: Implemented in .github/scripts/pyright_ratchet.py (parses `pyright --outputjson` summary.errorCount vs .github/pyright-baseline.txt). Chosen over continue-on-error (non-blocking steps rot — new errors slide in unnoticed) and full baseline-diff (false-positive machine). Tested fail/pass/warn paths.
data.author: Vorpid

Solved torch: this CPU-inference tool was pulling the multi-GB CUDA stack on Linux (B-4). Mechanism A — [[tool.uv.index]] pytorch-cpu + torch declared DIRECT and exact-pinned ==2.11.0 (uv sources don't reach transitives; exact pin = zero drift, fail-loud at future ST bumps). Lock 162→144 (removed 18 GPU-only pkgs), all 3 platforms served, no other drift. Commits: noqa UP042 (ruff gating, f3107f6); .gitattributes -text journal byte-determinism (fdea857); CPU torch (29c4e02); CI workflow (3a0d227); windows matrix + action bumps (0451854); CHANGELOG. data.summary: "The defense is also a write to the defended thing": adding -text to an LF-stored journal blob without a cut-over commit creates the exact C-3 rewrite it defends against (phantom-modified file, LF-overwrite window, union-merge journal duplication across the boundary)

Resolution choreography: manager merges the attribute change then IMMEDIATELY flushes — with -text in effect the flush re-commits the live CRLF bytes verbatim, renormalizing the blob; implementer frozen on git ops from approval until the cut-over-done ping. Check any protective change against the question: does applying the protection itself touch the protected artifact? Caught by the WP-2 adversarial review (green CI hid it); traded to Silvie whose eol=lf repo had it worse.

data.summary: WP-3: post-commit hook (H-1 + utf-8 decode) + S-1 skill paths — PR #3
data.detail: Three distribution-surface fixes. (a) H-1: hooks.json ran post-commit.py on bare `python` (silent fail where absent). Added hooks/post-commit.sh wrapper running it via `uv run --no-sync` (mirrors session-start.sh venv/env resolution: PLUGIN_DATA fallback, cygpath, REPO_PATH, stdin passthrough, printf for byte-exact {} ); hooks.json now invokes bash wrapper. Measured per-call overhead (fires on every Bash call): uv run ~184ms vs old bare-python ~221ms (net faster here) vs direct-venv ~112ms (flagged as faster-but-divergent option). (b) post-commit.py decoded git output with subprocess text=True + no encoding → locale codepage mangled non-ASCII ('§'→'Â§'); added encoding='utf-8' to both git calls + regression test (temp repo, non-ASCII commit, codepoint-exact; red-before on Windows verified, passes on utf-8 CI). (c) S-1: vibe-curate SKILL.md referenced analyzer prompts by repo-relative paths (broken installed); now referenced from the skill's own dir. Baselines: ruff clean, pyright 31 (new test's 1 error fixed by reordering a None-narrow assert), pytest 130→131. Peer review caught: wrapper must set REPO_PATH (reinject template omits it), printf not echo, test needs git config + codepoint compare, venv-missing→uv creates empty venv. Flagged mangled journal node 0a23fe8200e4 for vince. v0.7.3 release after merge.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Python subprocess.run(text=True) decodes child output with the locale codepage (Windows cp1252), NOT utf-8 — git emits utf-8, so non-ASCII gets mangled ('§'→'Â§') on Windows. Pass encoding="utf-8" explicitly when reading git output.
data.detail: Root cause of the journal's 'Â§8.1' node. text=True uses locale.getpreferredencoding(); on Windows that's cp1252, on utf-8-locale Linux/CI it happens to be utf-8 (so the bug is invisible on CI — only manifests on non-utf-8 locales). Always set encoding='utf-8' for subprocess reads of known-utf-8 output. Verify fails-before by codepoint (==), not terminal eyeballing (the terminal re-renders the mangle identically).
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Plugin hooks route through `uv run --no-sync` (+ a bash wrapper resolving the CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA venv) rather than bare `python` — uv is a guaranteed dependency, python is not. But for HIGH-frequency hooks (post-commit fires on every Bash call), the ~180ms uv-run spawn matters; the venv interpreter direct is ~40% faster.
data.detail: Wrapper pattern (post-commit.sh, mirrors session-start.sh/reinject): PLUGIN_DATA=${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA:-${PLUGIN_ROOT%/*}}, cygpath -m, VENV_DIR=$PLUGIN_DATA/.venv, UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT + REPO_PATH env, uv run --no-sync --project $PLUGIN_ROOT. Measured: uv run ~184ms vs direct $VENV_DIR/Scripts(or bin)/python ~112ms vs bare python ~221ms (Windows). Tradeoff: direct-interpreter is faster but needs an OS branch and breaks the uv-run idiom. --no-sync against a missing venv CREATES an empty one (fine for stdlib-only hooks).
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: FEATURE ACCEPTED (deferred, Colton): first-class document storage — store actual documents (client docs etc.) as first-class episode nodes, with attached entity nodes describing the document's content
data.detail: Requested 2026-06-10 during the audit-fix pipeline; logged to return to after the audit tiers (or when Colton prioritizes). Shape: a document IS an episode (full artifact, verbose detail is already the episode contract), and recording one spawns/attaches descriptive entity nodes (decisions, constraints, discoveries the doc contains) linked to it. Design questions for pickup: where the bytes live (.cognition/docs/ blob dir vs journal-embedded vs file references), size/binary handling, whether doc text gets chunked into ChromaDB for search, dedup/versioning of re-stored docs, and how auto-description (the attached nodes) gets generated — agent-driven like curation, per the no-background-LLM decision.

data.summary: Bash `${var%/*}` strips nothing on a Windows backslash path (no `/` to match) — the B-3 venv-misplacement bug. Use `${var%[/\\]*}` to strip the last segment on either separator. Verify in a real script file, not via a tool's command line (backslash escaping mangles the test).
data.detail: The CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA fallback `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA:-${PLUGIN_ROOT%/*}}` left a backslash PLUGIN_ROOT untouched, landing the venv inside the version-pinned cache dir. `[/\\]` inside `${%...}` is a glob bracket matching / or \ (the `\\` is one literal backslash); it works when the bytes are exact (Write tool / script file) but command-line layers can collapse `\\`→`\` and break it. Was in all 3 hooks (post-commit.sh, session-start.sh, reinject-instructions.sh).
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Version bumps must re-lock uv.lock: it pins the project's OWN version (editable self-package, source={editable="."}), so bumping pyproject without `uv lock` makes CI's `uv sync --locked` fail. The lock diff is exactly the one version line (no hash, editable self-package).
data.detail: uv.lock had `name="vibe-cognition"` / `version="0.7.2"`. A release that bumps pyproject+plugin.json to 0.7.3 must also `uv lock` → uv.lock:2682 to 0.7.3, else --locked drift fails the pipeline. The release procedure (CLAUDE.md) names pyproject+plugin.json as the two version sources but predates CI/uv.lock — so the real release surface is now THREE files + the CHANGELOG.
data.author: Vorpid

Kept the WP-grouped sub-sections under [0.7.3] (repeated ### headings, cosmetically non-canonical) per "cut, nothing else" — flagged not fixed. CI green. Post-merge: Loki pins SHA+version in colton-claude-plugins marketplace; Colton runs the human release-test for the H-1/S-1/B-3 install mechanics (b8ec24fe9107, can't self-verify installs).

data.summary: Document-storage design complete (docs/DESIGN-document-storage.md): content-addressed blobs + text sidecars, DOCUMENT node type (episode-like in the matcher), agent-extracts-text (zero new deps), chunked ChromaDB search, deletion ships with store — pending 3 operator calls
data.detail: Peer review falsified two original claims against code: the deterministic matcher requires literal EPISODE type (descriptor auto-link needs a one-line matcher change), and startup re-sync is id-based so teammate machines would never get chunk vectors (WP-D2 must re-chunk from sidecars). Other review-driven choices: full text in sidecar not journal detail (C-1 line-size hazard), dedup-by-sha returns existing node, versioning via supersedes, refcounted blob unlink + git-history privacy caveat on delete, .cognition/documents/** -text (sha-named content must be byte-stable), extension sanitization, agent text ceiling stated (~tens of KB/call). 2 new tools (store/get; list folds into get_history), 4 WPs, v0.8.0. Open: blob git policy default, 10MB cap, doc-episode auto-linking.

data.summary: Document-storage §8 resolved (Colton): blobs default-commit with local_only opt-out; NO size cap (tool reports size); documents DO auto-link to episodes — matcher gains document↔episode relates_to rule alongside entity↔document part_of
data.detail: (c) reverses the design's episode-like-means-no-episode-links simplification: Colton expects documents to be relevant to many connections, especially episodes that pertain to them. relates_to (not part_of) for doc↔episode since neither contains the other; document↔document stays manual (supersedes). (b) no-cap interacts with (a) default-commit — GitHub's hard 100MB file limit is an external constraint the WP-D1 brief must handle (warn/require local_only above it), flagged for the seam gate. Design doc updated same day.

data.summary: §8(d) Colton: document storage defaults to REFERENCE mode — node stores path + metadata + sha256, bytes stay in place; blob copy is opt-in (store_copy). Text sidecar kept in both modes for search
data.detail: "Avoid storing too much in the graph; the nodes for the information inside the document will be in the attached nodes anyways." Staleness handled by re-hash on access (unchanged|modified|missing, reported honestly). Known limitation documented: paths are machine-specific — teammates get node+descriptors+text, not the file. This dissolves most of the no-cap×git-commit seam (S1) and the dedup×local_only seam (S3) for the default path; they survive only in the opt-in copy mode.

data.summary: v0.7.3 upgrade corrupts the shared plugin venv: torch PyPI→CPU-index swap during self-sync fails under running servers holding DLLs (Windows) — half-uninstalled torch (missing RECORD), all new server starts die on ImportError torch.Tensor
data.detail: Caught by Colton's human release test (constraint b8ec24fe9107 doing its job). The venv relocation fixed update-time cache locks, but the venv is still SHARED with live servers — any release that swaps a loaded heavy dep (this one: torch re-sourced for the CPU-install win) hits Windows file locks mid-swap and bricks the env for new sessions while old sessions keep running on mapped DLLs. One-time per dep-swap, but the CLASS recurs. Repair: stop all vibe-cognition sessions, then uv sync --reinstall-package torch (or delete the .venv and let session-start rebuild). Mitigation discussion queued: detect-and-fail-loud on partial sync? sync staging? release notes flag for dep-swap releases?

data.summary: WP-5: session-start venv-health probe — detect a half-swapped venv, guide self-heal (PR #5)
data.detail: Response to the v0.7.3 upgrade incident: torch PyPI→CPU-index swap fails mid-uninstall under running servers holding DLLs (Windows) → half-installed torch → new MCP server starts die on ImportError, shown only as a cryptic connection failure; self-heals on a clean start. Per ledger 19 (an upgrade is also a write to the running copy — ship detection + a clear message, not a code fix). session-start.sh Step 2: guard the sync with `|| true` (the set -e death on a failed sync was the cryptic-failure root), then probe `import torch, chromadb` ONLY after a sync; stamp written only for a verified-importable venv so a broken one re-warns every start until healed; on failure emit a clear SessionStart message (close ALL sessions, start ONE). Steady-state happy path skips the probe (zero added cost); post-sync probe ~7.5s (dwarfed by sync). Seam check (ledger 18): exit is python's own no-pipe (17), probe read-only on the venv (5), valid JSON on failure. Verified under failure condition (ledger 3) on throwaway venvs (torch-absent AND present-but-broken) without mutating the real 1.1GB venv. CHANGELOG: 0.7.3 known-upgrade note + the detection fix under Unreleased. Flagged (not edited): CLAUDE.md release procedure should gain a close-sessions-before-dep-swap-update line. Install-mechanics → gated on human release test.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Upgrade-resilience probe pattern: gate the venv health check to run ONLY after a sync (not every session start), and write the sync-stamp only for a verified-importable venv. Healthy steady state pays nothing; a broken venv leaves the stamp unwritten so it re-detects + re-warns every start until it self-heals.
data.detail: From WP-5. The stamp semantics shift from "synced" to "synced AND importable" — that one change makes detection self-healing without a separate retry loop. Probe reads the program's own exit (no pipe, ledger 17) and is read-only on the venv it checks (ledger 5). Cost: zero on the happy path (stamp match skips the block); ~7.5s on the post-sync path (import torch+chromadb), dwarfed by the sync. Embodies ledger 19: ship detection + a clear message for a self-healing upgrade collision, not a code fix that adds its own seams.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WP-4: journal append atomicity (C-1) + replacement detection (C-3) + H-2 — PR #6
data.detail: The data-loss WP. New stdlib-only cognition/journal_io.py: append_journal_line writes each record to an O_APPEND fd under an exclusive byte-range lock (fcntl.flock POSIX / msvcrt sentinel-byte Windows) + loop-to-completion (interleave-safe only under the lock — a short write would otherwise reintroduce C-2, the ledger-18 catch peer review made). Lock-contention fallback: single un-looped os.write + loud log (documented residual). Byte format preserved via O_BINARY (CRLF on Windows) so -text/offset-math unaffected. Both writers routed through it: storage._append_journal + the post-commit hook (path-loads it, stays stdlib-only) — closes H-2. _catch_up gains C-3: first-line sha256 + st_mtime_ns identity; same-or-larger replacement re-hydrates instead of stale-offset replay; mtime catches equal-size swap; from-0 read with non-empty graph re-hydrates (the offset==0 gap the composition test found, ledger 11). Rebuild read deliberately NOT under the lock — safety via torn-tail+idempotency+identity (invariant in docstring). Verification: fails-before run repeatedly (old append 4/4 interleaved 145-192/240; C-3 and offset-0 branches reverted → their tests fail, ledger 12); N=5 consecutive green (135 passed); pyright 31, ruff clean. Build snags fixed: Windows os.open defaults to TEXT mode (doubled \n→\r\r\n) → added O_BINARY; pyright needs sys.platform=='win32' (not os.name) to narrow fcntl/msvcrt. Scope: local fs only (O_APPEND/locks unreliable on NFS/SMB). Install-mechanics → gated on human release test.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary:
Windows os.open() defaults to TEXT mode — os.write then translates 
→
, so writing b"...
" yields b"...
". Pass os.O_BINARY (getattr(os,"O_BINARY",0); 0 on POSIX) for raw bytes. Also: pyright only narrows fcntl/msvcrt platform imports on `sys.platform=="win32"`, not os.name=="nt".
data.detail: Bit me building journal_io's atomic append: the journal got \r\r\n and JSON parsing of the empty split-line failed. O_BINARY fixes it. For the lock, fcntl doesn't exist on Windows / msvcrt doesn't on POSIX; guarding the conditional import with `if sys.platform == "win32"` (a literal pyright special-cases) excludes the unreachable branch from per-platform analysis — `os.name == "nt"` does NOT narrow and yields 12 possibly-unbound/attribute errors.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Atomic cross-process file append: O_APPEND + a SINGLE os.write is atomic only in practice for small records; the exclusive lock is what makes a loop-to-completion (for short writes) interleave-safe — without it, a short write reintroduces the torn-tail-eats-next-entry bug. Lock a sentinel byte FAR past EOF on Windows (not byte 0) so a concurrent reader isn't blocked by the mandatory lock.
data.detail: The "single os.write is enough" framing is a trap: it has no good short-write story (loop without lock = interleave; no loop = truncated record). The lock is not optional for correctness. POSIX flock is advisory (reads unaffected); Windows msvcrt is mandatory — locking real data (byte 0) would block another process's catch-up read of that byte, so use a sentinel offset (1<<40) that no read/write ever touches. O_APPEND still writes at EOF even after lseek-to-sentinel-for-the-lock. Fallback on lock-timeout: single un-looped write + log, never block/drop. Verified: 4 procs x large records, zero interleave; old buffered append interleaved 4/4.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WP-4 CRs: binding tests + hygiene
data.detail: WP-4 CRs: binding tests + hygiene
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: A first-line hash CANNOT detect a journal replacement from a git pull/merge — append-only journals ALWAYS share line 1, so a divergent merge preserves it. Use a hash of the already-replayed PREFIX (bytes[0:offset]); re-hash the on-disk prefix before replaying from the stored offset, mismatch → rehydrate. (C-3/CR-1.)
data.detail: Vince's WP-4 CR-1, the headline catch: the first-line-hash identity (my first cut) matched on exactly the scenario C-3 exists for — a divergent merge (common-prefix + remote + local) leaves the offset mid-remote, one garbage line warned, earlier remote entries silently never replayed. The prefix hash covers everything replayed, so any in-[0:offset] change (insert/modify) is caught. Also kills the 64KiB first-line read cap that a >64KiB first line would have silently disabled C-3 through. Cost: O(offset) read+hash, only when size/mtime say the file changed. Maintained incrementally via a running hashlib object (.digest() is non-finalizing).

data.summary: Node-type survival = a question that retrieves it + a trigger that writes it. Usage audit (n=110): assumption 0 ever; 42/55 episodes hook-written; constraint write-rarely-read-always (4 written, all injected every session)
data.detail: Live-journal usage audit for MemCog's taxonomy adoption (2026-06-12). Deliberate-recording distribution: discovery ~30%, decision ~28%, episode ~19%, pattern ~13%, constraint 6%; assumption never recorded once — its retrieving question ('what am I believing without proof?') is never asked, so no write habit forms. fail/incident boundary proved fuzzy in practice (1 and 2 uses; mishaps routed to discovery instead). Top type by raw count (episode) is top only because the post-commit hook writes it — types without a write trigger or a retrieval question rot regardless of design merit. Constraint is the counterexample to 'more types = more signal': lowest write volume, highest read value (all constraints injected at every session start and they govern behavior). Apply when designing new types (e.g. document) or adjudicating taxonomy changes.

data.summary: Node-id collision under coarse clock: generate_node_id hashes type:summary:timestamp; Windows ~15ms clock makes same-title same-tick stores collide → add_node silently overwrites
data.detail: CI windows-latest flaked on WP-D1a: two _store_document calls with the same title within one OS clock tick produced an identical 12-char id (generate_node_id = sha256(type:summary:timestamp)[:12]), and storage.add_node overwrote the first node. Linux's finer clock hid it; force_new is the obvious victim but two DIFFERENT documents sharing a title in one tick clobber each other too. Fix (commit 65b1194): in _store_document, salt the summary fed to generate_node_id and retry while storage.has_node(id). General lesson: any create path that derives its id purely from time+content-summary is overwrite-unsafe under coarse clocks — salt-until-free or add entropy.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Defer the GLOBAL generate_node_id collision fix to its own WP; D1a ships only a document-scoped salt-retry. Pre-existing data-loss bug (coarse-clock same-type+summary+tick → silent overwrite) affects _record_node + post-commit hook, not just documents.
data.detail: Gate decision on PR #8. Vorpid's CI surfaced that generate_node_id hashes type:summary:timestamp and Windows' ~15ms clock lets two same-title stores in one tick collide → add_node silently overwrites (data loss). His fix is a salt-retry-while-has_node loop LOCAL to _store_document — correct and in-scope for D1a, but generate_node_id itself is unchanged, so _record_node (all node types) and the hook's hand-rolled id still collide globally. Decided: accept the document-scoped fix for D1a (keeps the WP tight; D1a's own path is safe), and file the GLOBAL fix as its own WP. Rejected: cramming the global fix into D1a (touches the shared minter + the hook's reimplementation + replay — needs cross-writer composition review and its own fails-before, exactly the ballooning the seam discipline forbids). Fix DIRECTION for the global WP: hoist the uniqueness loop INTO add_node's locked block (or a shared storage.mint_unique_id) so all writers benefit AND the cross-process has_node→add_node TOCTOU shrinks. Interim asymmetry (documents retry, other nodes overwrite) tracked in BACKLOG so it's not mistaken for intent.
context: PR #8 merged at 870ff09 (pinned 65b1194). Anchored by Vorpid's discovery e434566c8440 (document-framed); this node frames the global scope + the gate's defer decision.
data.author: vince

data.summary: WP-D1a (document storage, reference mode) shipped: PR #8 merged at 870ff09. Gate held once on a sidecar-leak (entity-citer in deletion twin-check), passed on re-push; CI also surfaced a node-id collision data-loss bug.
data.detail: Vorpid implemented WP-D1a: DOCUMENT node type, reference-mode store/get tools, text sidecar (+deletion), pair-level graph-inert matcher guard, and a sync-path guard so documents are never embedded (closing both embedding writers per ledger 11). Seam re-cut by Vorpid to 'each PR creates nothing it can't delete' (better than my pre-authorized cut). GATE: CI green + my manager-read looked clean, but the decorrelated adversarial pass REPRODUCED a sidecar leak (F1) — the deletion twin-check used find_nodes_by_ref which counts ANY node citing doc:<hash>, including the descriptor ENTITIES the design intends to cite it, so deleting the sole document node left the sidecar orphaned forever. Held PR #8 with a CR; Vorpid fixed it with _other_document_has_sha (mirrors the dedup type==document+sha filter) + a regression test = my repro. Two ride-alongs: a tautological x==x assertion, and a plan-doc scope correction (extension sanitization → D1b). BONUS: the re-push went red on Windows CI — generate_node_id collision under the coarse clock silently overwrites same-title same-tick nodes (data loss); Vorpid root-caused + fixed it document-scoped (discovery e434566c8440), global fix deferred to its own WP (decision 0bd725b83bd0). Re-gate (2nd adversarial pass) found no re-leak path; merged pinned to 65b1194. Lesson: a cleanup/refcount check must share the create/dedup path's identity filter — an asymmetry between them is a leak waiting to happen (ledger 11/18, candidate margin note).
context: vince=ultimate authority. Gate structure (manager-read + decorrelated adversarial + CI) caught two real bugs CI+manager-read alone missed. Follow-ups filed to BACKLOG: global id-collision

data.summary: Dashboard search (dashboard/api.py search() → vector_search raw) has NO graph-existence filter, so it still serves cross-process ghosts — a SECOND N1 surface the WP-D1b cognition_search fix does not cover. Pre-existing; escalates at D2 when documents become searchable.
data.detail: Surfaced by the WP-D1b adversarial gate. WP-D1b fixed N1 (ghost search of cross-process-deleted nodes) on the MCP cognition_search path via a query-time has_node filter + startup reconciliation sweep. But the dashboard has its own search endpoint that calls vector_search directly with no has_node filter — so deleted-on-another-machine nodes still ghost there until a restart runs the sweep. Harmless in D1b (documents aren't embedded yet), but D2 makes documents searchable, which escalates the dashboard ghost to verbatim deleted-document-chunk text. Fix is a one-line has_node filter mirrored into dashboard/api.py search(). Tracked in BACKLOG under WP-D4; flagged to Vorpid as a D2 scoping call (pull it into D2 since D2 raises the stakes, or confirm it stays D4 with the residual stated).

data.summary: WP-D1b (document storage: copy mode + matcher pair rules + refcounted deletion + N1 ghost fix) shipped: PR #9 merged at 0faf302. Held once on a proof gap (committed N1 cross-process harness undelivered), passed on re-push. Document-storage core complete.
data.detail: Vorpid implemented WP-D1b in 5 commits: matcher 6-pair truth table (replacing D1a's inert guard; doc-gated on the iterated ref key for the §9 S4 vacuum defense; per-(from,to,edge_type) idempotency so a curator's same-type manual edge is never clobbered), the single shared documents_with_sha identity predicate (dedup + sidecar-delete + blob-delete + tests all call it), opt-in copy-mode content-addressed blobs (write-once/atomic, extension whitelist-or-DROP, §9 S1 size policy, local self-ignoring documents/.gitignore, S3 promote/demote), refcounted deletion, and the N1 ghost-search fix. GATE round 1: manager-read + fresh decorrelated adversarial pass (2 fails-before reverts) found NO code bug — confirmed Vorpid's per-blob-path refcount DEVIATION is an improvement (per-sha would leak a same-sha/diff-ext sibling's blob). HELD anyway on a PROOF gap (ledger 8): the plan committed to a real cross-process N1 harness and explicitly called the hand-built backstop insufficient, but only the backstop shipped — the highest-stakes path (don't-serve-deleted-content) lacked the end-to-end proof that guards the wiring, not just the filter function. Also corrected an N1 overclaim (dashboard search surface unfiltered — discovery c7d948583b4e, → WP-D4). Round 2: Vorpid extracted _search_cognition and added the genuine cross-process e2e harness (A records→B embeds→A deletes→B replays tombstone→real search returns count==0, fails-before RUN), plus per-blob-path + promotion-replay proofs, and fixed an inert gitignore add. Verified the refactor is behavior-preserving and the gitignore fix doesn't regress fresh-local_only. Merged pinned to 3f6cdf5. 174 tests, pyright 31→29.

data.summary: WP-D1a + WP-D1b shipped: first-class document storage core (reference + copy mode, matcher pair rules, refcounted deletion, N1 ghost-search fix)
data.detail:
Two-PR delivery of the document-storage core (DESIGN-document-storage.md §1-§9), split along the seam "each PR creates nothing it can't delete."

WP-D1a (PR #8, merged 870ff09): DOCUMENT node type + metadata dict; reference mode (path+sha256+metadata, bytes stay in place); agent-extracted text sidecar; doc:<sha[:12]> ref; store/get tools (testable _store_document/_get_document cores); O(1) dedup via find_nodes_by_ref + full-sha confirm; agent refs→context (S4/N3); pair-level matcher INERT guard (temporary); sidecar deletion; _sync_cognition_embeddings document-skip (N1-neutral by construction); .gitattributes documents/** -text. CR caught F1: the deletion twin-check used find_nodes_by_ref (any citer), so an entity citing doc:<hash> (the intended S4 pattern) was treated as a twin and leaked the sidecar permanently — fixed with _other_document_has_sha (document+sha only). CI surfaced a real latent bug: generate_node_id hashes type:summary:timestamp and Windows' ~15ms clock collides same-title same-tick stores → add_node silently overwrites; fixed with salt-until-free (discovery e434566c8440).

WP-D1b (PR #9, merged 0faf302, pinned 3f6cdf5): matcher pair rules replacing the inert guard (entity→episode part_of any-ref; entity→document part_of doc:-gated; document→episode relates_to doc:-gated; idempotency keyed per (from,to,edge_type) so a deterministic re-mint never clobbers a same-type manual edge; §9 S4 vacuum gate). Extracted ONE shared documents_with_sha identity predicate (dedup + sidecar-delete + blob-delete + guarding test all call it — Vince/Reginald ledger 11, the F1-class structural binding). Copy mode: content-addressed blob, extension whitelist-or-DROP, atomic write-once, size policy §9 S1 (50/95MB, no hard cap), LOCAL self-ignoring documents/.gitignore (per-machine local_only), S3 promote/demote. Paired deletion: per-BLOB-PATH refcount (deviation from the plan's per-sha — needed for force_new copy twins sharing a sha but different ext) + chunk-purge wiring (delete_by_node_id, no-op pre-D2). N1 ghost-search fix: query-time has_node drop in _search_cognition (correctness path) + startup _reconcile_orphan_embeddings sweep (best-effort reclamation, catch-up-before-enumerate, empty-ids guard, documented residual TOCTOU); baseline 31→29. Manual-edge guard resolved as scope note (documents intentionally manually-linkable for §4 supersedes versioning). Both gated hard: D1b held once for a missing real cross-process e2e harness (the shipped test only proved the filter function, not that the search PATH calls it) — fixed by extracting _search_cognition and driving the real vector_search→filter path over a genuine two-storage-one-journal ghost.

Process: plan→decorrelated peer review→Vince align→build (each commit fails-before RUN, ledger 12)→push→CI green 3 legs→SHA-pinned merge gate. Out of scope, tracked: D2 (chunked search), dashboard N1 filter (WP-D4), global node-id collision + has_node→add_node TOCTOU (Vince backlog).

data.summary: WP-D2 (chunked document search) shipped: PR #10 merged at dd11cd2. Documents are now searchable — chunked embeddings + dedupe-to-best-per-node + matched_excerpt + N1 filter on both surfaces. Held once on an over-query starve gap (B3 claimed-met but unmet); fixed with adaptive over-query.
data.detail: Vorpid implemented WP-D2 in 6 commits: chunk text into the embedding store (upsert_embedding gains document=, vector_search returns matched_text, mix verified); pure word-window chunker (cognition/chunking.py); store-time chunking via the single shared _embed_document (node vector + <node_id>#chunk-N chunks, is_chunk:True marker, delete-then-write per A5); re-sync chunking + interim backfill (precise A4 'synced?' key so text-less docs don't re-embed every boot); cognition_search over-query + dedupe-to-best-hit-per-node + matched_excerpt; get_status node/chunk count split (is_chunk filter — chromadb 1.5.5 has no $exists, falsified live per ledger 23) + dashboard N1 filter. GATE round 1: fresh decorrelated adversarial pass verified all SAFETY-critical paths sound (N1/ghost drop on both surfaces via shared search_hit_is_live, delete-then-write, deleted-text leak closed) — but REPRODUCED that over-query k=5 fails its own B3 (a single document with ≥limit*k chunks starves other live nodes), and the shipped 8-chunk test was below the limit*k=10 threshold so it passed by luck (tautological w.r.t. sufficiency, ledger 12). HELD on that (a written acceptance criterion claimed-met but reproducibly unmet — same principle as the D1b hold). Round 2: Vorpid replaced the fixed window with ADAPTIVE over-query (double n_results until limit distinct live nodes / Chroma exhausted / cap; logarithmic; residual = single doc >cap chunks degrades recall only, capped+documented), the boundary test now uses 12 chunks (>10) fails-before RUN, and the false B3 plan claim was rewritten. Merged pinned to 63f2246. 190 tests, pyright 29. Dashboard-N1: SAFETY filter shipped (no deleted text), NAVIGATION (un-deduped chunk rows) deferred to WP-D4 with a scope note (discovery c7d948583b4e partially resolved).

data.summary: WP-D2 shipped: chunked document search (chunk-text embeddings, adaptive over-query dedupe, re-sync backfill, dashboard N1, status split)
data.detail:
Made documents searchable (DESIGN §3). PR #10, merged dd11cd2 (pinned 63f2246), on top of the D1 core. 6 commits + 1 CR fix:

1. (82408ba) ChromaDBStorage.upsert_embedding gains document= (→ Chroma documents field); vector_search includes documents → matched_text. Verified a collection mixes text-bearing chunks + text-less node vectors (no all-or-none).
2. (8f8766f) Pure word-window chunker cognition/chunking.py — deterministic ~1000-word/100-overlap approximation of §3's ~1000-token (dependency-free; agent bears the real token cost). Shrinking chunk counts handled by delete-then-write in the write paths, not here.
3. (a2edb9d) Store-time embedding: documents now embed at store time via the shared _embed_document (node vector + sidecar chunks, delete-then-write, is_chunk:True marker). _store_document gains optional embedding_storage/generator (skip-if-None; the tool defers to next sync when the model is still loading). Removed D1a's store-path document-skip.
4. (37f2653) Re-sync + interim backfill: replaced D1a's blanket _sync document-skip; precise "fully synced?" key (node vector exists AND (sidecar empty OR #chunk-0 exists)) so text-less docs don't re-embed every boot; backfills D1a/D1b-interim documents never embedded.
5. (d7fa76e) Search over-query + dedupe-to-best-hit-per-node + matched_excerpt.
6. (75182e3) get_status {nodes,chunks,total} split (count_documents(filter={is_chunk:True}) — public param filter=, not where=); dashboard N1 ghost filter pulled into D2 (D2 escalates the dashboard ghost to verbatim deleted client-document text); shared storage.search_hit_is_live used by BOTH search surfaces.
7. (63f2246, CR fix) ADAPTIVE over-query: a FIXED limit*k can't satisfy B3 (one doc can yield >limit*k chunks and starve other live nodes); now doubles n_results until limit distinct live nodes / Chroma exhausted / cap. Dashboard document-search NAV deferred to WP-D4 with a scope note (safety intact, only navigation incomplete).

Peer review (2 decorrelated rounds, ran chromadb 1.5.5 directly) caught 5 blocking before build: A1 count-split impossible (no $exists; $ne matches missing-field → positive is_chunk marker), A5 shrinking re-chunk orphans high-N chunks into ghost excerpts (→ delete-then-write), A2/A3 optional embedding args + fake signatures, A4 retire skip-test + precise idempotency key. Vince's gate caught the real one: over-query k=5 didn't satisfy B3 and the test passed by luck (8 < limit*k=10) — fixed with adaptive widen + a genuine 12-chunk boundary test.

Cross-cutting discipline held (ledger 11): one predicate per identity question — documents_with_sha (dedup/sidecar/blob), search_hit_is_live (both search surfaces), _embed_document (chunk-write contract). Every guard fails-before RUN (ledger 12).

The docs/skill WP making document storage usable (DESIGN §7 WP-D3 + §9 S4/N3). PR #11, merged 9afc538 (pinned c3b1bbf). 4 commits:

1. (693d85c) skills/vibe-document/SKILL.md — auto-discovered skill making the load-bearing S4/N3 workflow the DEFAULT: store_document → returns doc_ref → record the document's contents as descriptor ENTITY nodes citing that doc_ref in THEIR references (auto-link part_of via the matcher) → /vibe-curate. The worked example leads with a WRONG-vs-RIGHT contrast: doc_ref MUST go in references, NOT context — that single misplacement is the difference between a linked document and an inert one. Covers reference/copy/local_only + git-history privacy caveat, get_document/freshness, matched_excerpt search, deletion of managed artifacts only.

(d8bf585) /vibe-cognition SKILL — closed the tool-table gap (was missing 7 of 17, not 2: 2 doc tools + get_uncurated_nodes + mark_curated + reload + a service row for get_status + cognition_dashboard) and fixed the edge-type drift in both undercounted spots (3 and 4 → the full 5 semantic); corrected the deterministic claim to part_of + relates_to (doc→episode) and spelled out relates_to's three provenances.
3. (5ec54d6) README — 2 doc tools, Document Storage feature bullet, edge-table + "only automatic edge" claims corrected to post-D1b/D2 reality, vibe-backfill consider→MANDATORY (audit S-3).
4. (c3b1bbf) tests/test_doc_drift.py — turns audit S-3 into a regression GUARD: enumerates all 17 registered tools via asyncio.run(FastMCP; register_all_tools; list_tools()) (FastMCP 3.1.1 async API; 2.x _tool_manager gone — ledger 23) and asserts each is documented in the SKILL; pins the 6 non-duplicate_of CognitionEdgeType values (len==6 tripwire) to appear in SKILL + README. Presence-check only; the relates_to provenance SEMANTICS were corrected by hand (the guard locks the SET, the human locks the LABELS).

Peer review (2 decorrelated rounds) verified the spine against live code — cognition_record keeps agent references ON the node (NOT rerouted to context like _store_document does for documents), so a descriptor entity citing doc:<hash> genuinely auto-links — and caught the 7-missing-tools composition gap (the guard over all-registered-tools couldn't go green against a Commit-2 documenting only 2). data.summary: Agent-controlled mime flowed RAW into the dashboard download Content-Type header — a real header injection (verified in the test harness, NOT merely h11-backstopped). Lesson: clamp EVERY agent-controlled value that becomes an HTTP header, same as the filename — don't rely on the parser to reject your own injection (ledger 17).
data.detail: WP-D4 gate finding. The dashboard download endpoint sanitized the Content-Disposition filename (_safe_filename, strict charset) but passed meta.get("mime") — also agent-controlled (cognition_store_document mime= param) — RAW into FileResponse media_type → Content-Type. The first adversarial pass judged it h11-backstopped (the ASGI parser rejects illegal CRLF), but Vorpid's fails-before showed "text/html\\r\\nX-Injected: evil" landing straight in the header in the TestClient harness — a real header injection, not luck. Fixed: _safe_media_type validates ^[\\w.+-]+/[\\w.+-]+$ (strict type/subtype, no params/CRLF/control) and falls back to application/octet-stream, mirroring _safe_filename. General rule: when two sibling fields are both agent/user-controlled and both flow into the same sink (HTTP headers, a filesystem path, a query), sanitizing one and not the other is the bolt-out-of-place — the create/delete-predicate-asymmetry pattern (ledger 11) applied to a write sink. And never let "the downstream layer happens to reject it" stand in for sanitizing at your own boundary (ledger 17).
context: PR #12 WP-D4 (dashboard download). Backstop was real (h11 rejects) but the app-layer injection was real too — fixed at the app boundary. Sibling-field asymmetry (filename sanitized

data.summary: WP-D4 shipped: dashboard document list + token-gated path-safe download + dashboard hardening (D-1/D-4/D-5/D-6) — completes the document-storage feature
data.detail:
The last functional WP of the document-storage feature (DESIGN §7 WP-D4 + dashboard audit cluster). PR #12, merged 6939c99 (pinned 7f07dea). 5 commits:

1. (6c51fc7) D-6 dashboard search dedupe-to-node + hydrate (the navigation half D2 deferred): dedupe chunk hits to the navigable node id, hydrate summary from the graph, carry matched_excerpt, preserve entity_type (no JS change). Reuses shared search_hit_is_live.
2. (24bee54) GET /api/documents (metadata-only) + Documents panel. Shared _document_has_blob/_document_blob_path helpers (ledger 11) for list + download.
3. (efef6cd) token-gated, path-SAFE download GET /api/document/{node_id}/download: node_id is a graph KEY (no traversal); copy-mode blob path reconstructed server-side + validated under documents_dir (is_relative_to on the RESOLVED path — defeats ../, absolute, symlink, Windows-sep, null-byte); reference-mode serves the text SIDECAR never the absolute original (§9 N2); 404 not traceback; filename sanitized; middleware accepts ?token= for the download path only (an <a download> can't send the header).
4. (7a30cff) hardening: D-1 (poll server.started, return failed+cache-nothing if it never comes up); D-4 (vendored cytoscape/layout-base/cose-base/fcose into static/vendor/ — no CDN/SRI gap, offline); D-5 (secrets.compare_digest, malformed-body→400, limit clamp); D-5h (export stop_dashboard); D-3a (Refresh button); S-3 MED README standalone-dashboard fix.
5. (7f07dea, CR) mime header-injection clamp (_safe_media_type — agent-controlled mime was flowing RAW into Content-Type; the fails-before showed a real CRLF injection landing in the header, not h11-backstopped; ledger 17) + traversal regression tests for the two subtlest vectors (absolute blob_path, symlink escaping documents_dir).

Two gate holds, both resolved: over-query starve (the D-6 nav was fine; the recall item tracked) and the CR security pair (mime clamp + traversal tests). Ledger 23: re-verified the 3-day-old audit against live code — D-2/D-3d/D-5j already fixed, not re-touched. Tiering held (deferred D-3 rest + D-5 cosmetics to BACKLOG). Every guard fails-before RUN, incl. the path-safety test serving "TOP SECRET" without the guard.

This completes the document-storage feature D1a→D4: stored (reference/copy), searchable (chunked + adaptive over-query dedupe), deletable (refcounted, one shared identity predicate), documented (/vibe-document skill + doc-drift guard), dashboard list + hardened download. Next: WP-R3 = the v0.8.0 release commit (version bump + CHANGELOG; pin waits for Colton's morning go + render check + the owed non-ASCII journal test — feature release, not the delegated patch-pin authority).
data.author: Vorpid
