LIGHT HELD on two security-hygiene items: (1) agent-controlled mime flowed RAW into Content-Type — a REAL header injection (discovery 9dc4a49ac093), fixed with _safe_media_type mirroring _safe_filename; (2) missing regression tests for the symlink + absolute traversal vectors (defended in code, untested), added with fails-before. data.summary: v0.8.0 RELEASED: first-class document storage shipped end-to-end (WP-D1a→D4 + release commit), PRs #8–#13, all on main
data.detail:
The complete first-class document-storage feature landed on main and was cut as v0.8.0 (merge 8f3079f, pinned 1eef757). Six PRs (#8–#13), each SHA-pinned-merge-gated by Vince; six gate holds across the run, all resolved on re-push.

THE ARC:
- WP-D1a (PR #8, 870ff09): DOCUMENT node type + metadata; reference mode (path+sha256, bytes in place); agent-extracted text sidecar; doc:<sha[:12]> ref; store/get tools; O(1) dedup; pair-level matcher inert guard; sidecar deletion; .gitattributes -text. Holds: F1 (sidecar leak via a citing entity — find_nodes_by_ref counted any citer, not just documents) + a real CI-surfaced latent bug (generate_node_id collision under Windows' ~15ms clock → salt-until-free, discovery e434566c8440).
- WP-D1b (PR #9, 0faf302): matcher pair rules (entity→doc part_of, doc→episode relates_to, doc:-gated, per-(from,to,type) idempotency); the ONE shared documents_with_sha identity predicate (dedup+sidecar+blob); copy mode (content-addressed blob, extension whitelist-or-DROP, write-once/atomic, size policy §9 S1, local self-ignoring .gitignore, S3 promote/demote); per-BLOB-PATH refcounted deletion + chunk-purge wiring; N1 ghost-search fix (query-time has_node drop + startup reconciliation sweep); pyright baseline 31→29. Hold: missing real cross-process e2e harness → extracted _search_cognition core to drive the real path.
- WP-D2 (PR #10, dd11cd2): chunk-text in ChromaDB (upsert document=, vector_search matched_text); pure word-window chunker; store-time + re-sync embedding via shared _embed_document (delete-then-write, is_chunk marker); search over-query + dedupe-to-best-hit-per-node + matched_excerpt; get_status node/chunk split; dashboard N1 filter. Holds: peer review (ran chromadb 1.5.5 live) falsified the count-split (no $exists; $ne matches missing-field → is_chunk marker) + caught A5 (shrinking re-chunk orphans high-N chunks → delete-then-write); gate caught fixed-k over-query starve → adaptive widen.
- WP-D3 (PR #11, 9afc538): /vibe-document skill making the S4/N3 link-by-doc_ref workflow the default (WRONG-vs-RIGHT example); /vibe-cognition SKILL + README tool-table/edge-type drift fixes (audit S-3); doc-drift GUARD test (all 17 tools via live list_tools; edge types pinned to enum). Hold: SKILL was missing 7 tools not 2 → guard couldn't go green against a half-fix.
- WP-D4 (PR #12, 6939c99): dashboard D-6 nav (dedupe+hydrate), document list endpoint+panel, token-gated path-SAFE download (node_id is a graph key; blob path validated under documents_dir; reference→sidecar never the original), D-1 liveness, D-4 vendored cytoscape (no CDN/SRI), D-5 security (compare_digest, malformed→400, limit clamp), D-3a refresh. Hold: mime header-injection (agent-controlled mime flowed raw into Content-Type — a real CRLF injection, not h11-backstopped) → _safe_media_type clamp + traversal regression tests (absolute, symlink).
- WP-R3 (PR #13, 8f3079f): v0.8.0 release commit — 4-file no-code bump (pyproject, plugin.json, uv.lock version line, CHANGELOG cut-and-stub); uv lock --check green so CI --locked passes.

DISCIPLINE THAT HELD: plan→decorrelated peer review→build→every guard's fails-before RUN (ledger 12)→CI green 3 platforms→SHA-pinned gate. One identity predicate per question (documents_with_sha, search_hit_is_live, _embed_document — ledger 11). Live-endpoint falsification over doc-trust (ledger 23). Don't rely on the parser to catch our own injection (ledger 17). The shared-worktree journal protocol held: I never committed the journal; Vince flushed at each align.

PIN: v0.8.0 is a FEATURE release (not the delegated patch-pin authority) — landed UN-PINNED on main; Loki pin queued HOLD for Colton's morning go + two human checks (D-4 vendored-libs render, owed v0.7.4 non-ASCII-commit journal test). Un-pinned main publishes nothing.

GATE: manager-read + a focused decorrelated REGRESSION hunt (chosen because the tool layer was previously UNTESTED — audit T-1 — so a faithful-extraction shape-change wouldn't be caught by any pre-existing test). Verdict MERGE-AS-IS: success-path return shapes byte-identical to base, every delta an intended fix, no caller depended on the old raise/silent paths, count mirrors exactly, direction allowed-sets match downstream. pyright objectively 29→8, every fails-before RUN, 220 tests (incl the first-ever tool tests + test_tool_utils.py). Merged clean, no hold.

data.summary: WP-ID (global node-id collision data-loss fix) shipped: PR #15 merged at d585a22, no hold. The last P1 audit item — mint-on-collision hoisted into add_node's locked block (replay-safe by construction), unified out the D1a salt loop, hook commit-hash discriminator, embedding-id rebind closes the orphan-vector.
data.detail: The careful core change (data-loss class). generate_node_id hashes type:summary:timestamp; under a coarse clock two same-type+summary nodes in one tick collided → add_node silently OVERWROTE (data loss); D1a fixed it document-scoped only. WP-ID (3 commits): C1 — add_node(node, *, mint_unique_id=False) -> str: salt-on-collision UNDER the _synced lock (which catches up first → the check sees cross-process journaled nodes → in-process collision CLOSED, cross-process TOCTOU SHRUNK-not-eliminated, residual documented backlog #2). Routed _record_node + _store_document through it, REMOVED _store_document's document-scoped salt loop (one mechanism, ledger 11). C2 — post-commit hook folds the commit hash into its id input (separate stdlib process, can't share the lock, but the commit hash is a naturally-unique discriminator). C3 — cross-writer inventory + composition. THE REPLAY SEAM (chief concern): safe BY CONSTRUCTION — _replay_entry/_catch_up write self._graph.add_node DIRECTLY, never the public add_node, so the mint fires ONLY at generation, never replay (idempotent cross-process convergence preserved); documented in the add_node docstring so it can't be hoisted. THE PEER-REVIEW CATCH (A1/A2, the unification crux): on a salted mint the embedding upserts would use the STALE pre-mint id → node under minted id, vector/chunks under old id → silently unsearchable + orphaned vectors; AND the graph-count test passes even with the orphan. Fix: rebind node_id from add_node's return BEFORE the embedding block in both paths; the load-bearing test asserts the embedded-id set == the graph-id set (RecordingEmbed fake). GATE: manager-read + decorrelated adversarial pass on the data-loss surface, both MERGE-AS-IS; the pass proved the replay/cross-process/A1 tests non-tautological by neutering the mint. 5 fails-before tests + N≥3 green, pyright held at 8, 226 tests, Windows CI green (where the collision originally surfaced). Closes the last P1 audit-remainder item.
context: vince=ultimate authority. Both P1 audit items (WP-T
WP-ID) now done + the document feature. Remaining audit tail is P2/P3 (WP-Cap next
then Emb/Core-tail/dashboard cosmetics). Milestone flagged to Colton.

data.summary: WP-T + WP-ID shipped: both P1 audit-remainder items — tool-layer correctness (pyright 29→8) and the global node-id-collision data-loss fix
data.detail:
The two P1 items from the 2026-06-10 audit, on top of the merged document-storage feature.

WP-T (PR #14, merged cc9cd73): tool-layer correctness + the pyright ratchet.
- T-9: routed all 20 raw ctx.request_context.lifespan_context sites through get_lifespan → pyright 29→9, then T-2's free B2 fix (test_cognition.py:863 None-guard) → 8. The deliverable was a DROP, not a hold; baseline file moved with it. Wiring test: get_lifespan raises on None request_context.
- T-2: storage.count_uncurated_nodes (uncapped, mirrors the get filter) → total_uncurated stops lying past the 500 list-cap. 501-node boundary test.
- T-6: one _parse_node_type + _validate_direction; fixed get_uncurated (bare raise), get_edgeless (silent-empty), get_chain/get_neighbors (silent-wrong direction) — one error-dict shape.
- T-3 + C-5: extracted _add_edge_core/_add_edges_batch_core; isinstance guard kills the batch partial-commit crash; both surface add_edge's False return (cross-process node-vanished race).
- composition: unified {"error": str} contract test. 220 pass.

WP-ID (PR #15, merged d585a22): the GLOBAL node-id-collision data-loss bug, the careful one (gated hardest).
- Bug: generate_node_id hashes type:summary:timestamp; coarse clock (Win ~15ms) → two same-type+summary nodes one tick collide → add_node overwrites (silent data loss). D1a fixed document-scoped; _record_node + the post-commit hook still collided.
- Fix: add_node(node, *, mint_unique_id=False) -> str salts-on-collision UNDER the _synced lock (atomic, sees catch-up'd cross-process nodes → shrinks the has_node→add_node TOCTOU). _record_node + _store_document opt in and REBIND node_id BEFORE the embedding upsert (peer-review A1/A2 — else the salted node's vector lands under the stale id → silently unsearchable + orphaned chunks). Removed _store_document's own salt loop (one mechanism). Post-commit hook (separate process, writes journal directly) closed by folding the commit hash into its id.
- THE REPLAY SEAM, safe BY CONSTRUCTION: _replay_entry/_catch_up/reload write _graph.add_node DIRECTLY, never the public add_node, so the mint can't reach replay → idempotent cross-process convergence preserved (documented in the docstring so it can't be hoisted). 226 pass, N≥3 green on the timing/replay/embedding suite, pyright 8 held.

Both peer-reviewed decorrelated; the WP-ID review caught the A1 embedding-id-rebind gap (the obvious graph-count test wouldn't catch it — needed the embedding-id-specific test, ledger 12). Both gated hard by Vince, merged as-is on re-gate. Next: WP-Cap (P2 capability gaps: get_node, update_node-with-re-embed, persist edge reason, expose get_superseded_chain). After Cap the audit tail is mostly P3; Vince flagging the P1s-done milestone to Colton.

data.detail: WP-Cap closed the P2 audit/BACKLOG capability gaps in 6 commits on fix/wp-cap-capability, merged via PR #16 (merge 9876b43, pinned to tip 9d8a8f8). C1 cognition_get_node (read full narrative incl detail — search/get_neighbors omit it). C2 persist edge `reason` (T-4): added reason field to CognitionEdge, add_edge writes it (model_dump→journal), replay reads data.get("reason") gracefully, single+batch tool paths carry it, get_neighbors surfaces it; residual: redirect_edges still drops reason in its hand-built journal payload. C3 cognition_update_node + mandatory re-embed (gate-hard): whitelist of summary/detail/context/severity only (structural fields locked to protect doc sha/mode/doc: ref + part_of index + minted id); extracted single _embed_entity_node embed path (ledger 11) shared by _record_node and update_node; proven non-tautological with a TEXT-KEYED fake embedder (orthogonal vector per marker). C4 exposed get_superseded_chain + get_incident_resolution (T-11) and collapsed a dead identical LED_TO if/else in get_incident_resolution (no behavior change). C5 composition (id/edges/curation survive an edit; record+update share one embed path). C6 gate fix (see linked discovery): re-embed on ANY whitelisted change, not just summary/detail. Vince gated hardest on the re-embed crux + a search-staleness hunt; one light hold (C6) resolved as a commit-on-top (not amend) to keep the gate trail honest. Full suite 241 green, ruff clean, pyright == baseline (8), CI green on all 3 legs. This completes ALL P1+P2 audit-remainder work (WP-T, WP-ID, WP-Cap) atop the document-storage feature + v0.8.0. Pipeline paused after merge pending Colton's steer on the P3 tail.

data.summary: update_node re-embed must refresh BOTH the match vector AND the Chroma metadata: context/severity are surfaced in search results, so re-embed on ANY field change, not just summary/detail
data.detail: _embed_entity_node writes context + severity into the Chroma metadata, and _format_search_results surfaces those in every hit (r.get("severity"), r.get("context")). So a context/severity-only update_node mutated the graph correctly but left the search-result metadata showing the OLD value until the next summary/detail edit or a restart resync — the same silent search-staleness the WP exists to kill, just on the displayed metadata rather than the match vector. The fix: re-embed on `if embeddings_ready:` (any whitelisted change). For a context/severity-only edit the regenerated vector is identical (embed text unchanged) but the same upsert refreshes the displayed metadata — negligible cost on a rare path. General lesson: when re-embedding gates on "did the searchable TEXT change", check whether the embedding's METADATA (also stored and surfaced) carries other edited fields — staleness hides in the displayed metadata, not just the vector.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WP-Core-tail (P3 core robustness) shipped: PR #17 merged at 3961f32. C-4 journal-first-then-mutate across all 8 write paths (no phantom on append failure), C-6 documented (don't advance offset — would corrupt C-3), C-7 path-based cycle detection (diamonds no longer mislabeled). Held once for regression-guard completion.
data.detail: Vorpid in 4 commits (+1 test-only CR fix): C-4 — reorder every write op (add_node, add_edge, update_node, remove_node, remove_edge single + remove-all loop, redirect_edges ×2) from mutate-then-journal to journal-FIRST-then-mutate, so a failing _append_journal (disk-full/Windows-AV-lock) leaves NO phantom in-memory write; validation stays first (read-only), add_node's mint stays first (WP-ID composition — journals the post-mint node, never runs on replay). C-6 — DOCUMENTED why _append_journal must NOT advance _offset/_journal_hasher: the in-process RLock and the journal_io append lock differ, so a remote append can land between this op's catch_up and its own append → our bytes don't land at _offset → advancing would corrupt the C-3 prefix-hash → false re-hydrate; the catch-up log reworded to not imply remote origin (a _pending_self_appends counter was considered + rejected as no-safer). C-7 — path-based cycle detection in get_reasoning_chain (pass path|{nid}, cycle = nid in ancestors), so a diamond A→B→D/A→C→D re-expands the second D instead of flagging it a cycle; true cycle still flagged; O(b^max_depth) re-expansion documented as an accepted depth-bounded trade-off. GATE: manager-read + decorrelated adversarial pass on the data-loss surface, both found the CODE correct (reorder complete + per-site correct, WP-ID composition preserved, no catch_up re-entrancy, remove_node self-replay a guarded no-op, redirect_edges replay-bytes identical). LIGHT HELD on a regression-guard gap: only 4 of 8 reordered sites had a fails-before append-failure test; required the other 4 (remove_edge ×2, redirect_edges) so a future revert-to-mutate-first on any path goes red. Vorpid added them test-only (raw-graph asserts, fails-before run). 255 tests, pyright 8. The C-4 fails-before tests assert against the RAW unsynced graph/ref-index, never a _synced accessor (which would hide the un-journaled phantom and tautologize the guard — the load-bearing test detail).

data.summary: chromadb 1.5.5 Rust backend handles cross-process SQLite lock contention internally
data.detail: WP-Emb E-4 investigation (2026-06-20): chromadb 1.5.5 uses chromadb_rust_bindings (Rust extension, not the Python sqlite3 path). Tested 300 concurrent upserts x 2 separate OS processes on the same PersistentClient path. Result: zero exceptions surfaced to Python. The Rust backend handles SQLite WAL + busy_timeout internally, so no "database is locked" or "busy" exception ever reaches Python-level code. Consequence: a Python-level _chroma_call_with_retry wrapper around upsert/delete calls would be a no-op shell. Decision: E-4 dropped; do not ship dead defense. Residual: multiple PersistentClient instances on the same path are write-safe (Rust handles locking) but read-stale across processes. Full multi-process safety requires HttpClient. XP0 impact: the close()/SQLite-handle question is now a Rust-layer question.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Two ChromaDBStorage instances at different persist_dirs coexist in one process with isolated results
data.detail: WP-XP0 Q1 (2026-06-21): opened two ChromaDBStorage instances at different tmp dirs (both collection_name="cognition_embeddings") in a single process. Seeded distinct vectors into each. Queries returned only their own seeds — no cross-contamination. A shared EmbeddingGenerator (SentenceTransformersBackend) can drive both queries. Constraint: SentenceTransformersBackend.encode() serializes under a threading.Lock (generator.py:52,72) — concurrent A+B searches queue at the encoder, not at Chroma. Option A (shared read of B's chroma) is feasible.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: client.close() releases Windows SQLite/Rust handle (proven by rename); del+gc alone does not
data.detail: WP-XP0 Q2 (2026-06-21): tested 4 incantations; verified by os.rename() of chromadb dir. client.close() -> RENAME OK. client._system.stop() -> RENAME OK. del+gc -> RENAME FAILS. client.close() calls SharedSystemClient._release_system(self._identifier) which decrements ref count and stops the System when the last client closes. Re-open after close works. Fix for XP1: ChromaDBStorage.close() (currently a no-op at storage.py:200) must call self._client.close(). One-line change.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Foreign reads from process A against B's chroma produce 0 errors under concurrent B writes
data.detail: WP-XP0 Q3 (2026-06-21): separate OS process wrote 200 upserts to B's chroma (10ms sleep). Process A ran collection.query() against B concurrently: 100 reads, 0 exceptions. Consistent with E-4 finding (Rust handles SQLite locking). Caveat: reads may be stale (per-process read cache; accepted freshness ceiling). Foreign read lock-retry NOT needed for XP2.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Dim mismatch raises InvalidArgumentError (not silent); model identity needs collection metadata stamp (prerequisite for XP1)
data.detail: WP-XP0 Q4 (2026-06-21): Q4a: querying with wrong dimension raises InvalidArgumentError: "Collection expecting embedding with dimension of 3, got 5". NOT silent garbage. Dimension probe: col.get(limit=1, include=["embeddings"]) returns numpy ndarray; shape[1] = stored dim. Q4b: collection.metadata = {"hnsw:space": "cosine"} — NO model provenance stored today. Guard strategy: stamp embedding_model + embedding_dimensions into collection metadata at creation (PREREQUISITE src change to ChromaDBStorage.__init__ before XP1). Three-state guard: present+match=allow, present+mismatch=hard-refuse, absent=warn-and-allow (all existing collections pre-stamp are absent; refusal on absent would block every pre-stamp project).
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: CognitionStorage catch-up: 0.02ms no-data (stat only), ~0.2ms/record on replay; no throttle needed
data.detail: WP-XP0 Q5 (2026-06-21): 2000-node / 960KB journal. No-new-data path (stat only): ~0.02ms. Replay path (50 new records per batch): avg ~9.5ms, ~0.19ms per record. Stat guard makes the common case (no B activity) essentially free. Replay only fires when B has new data; at ~0.2ms/record, a typical burst (10-50 new nodes) costs 2-10ms — acceptable for MCP tool invocations at human pace. No throttle required by measured numbers. Optional throttle (catch-up at most every N sec) is available for pathologically large deltas if XP2 needs it.

data.summary: CI ruff leg silently unenforced since v0.9.0: lockfile drift failed `uv sync --locked` BEFORE ruff ran, masking 39 accumulated violations (fixed in WP-Lint)
data.detail: The v0.9.0 release (0c2c52f) bumped pyproject version 0.8.0→0.9.0 without `uv lock`, so `uv sync --locked` failed on every branch off main, dying before the ruff step — masking the ruff leg entirely. 39 violations (under floor-pinned `ruff>=0.3.0`, locked 0.15.7) accumulated invisibly across XP1/XP2/E-3 code. Compounded by local-vs-CI ruff skew (running bare `ruff` instead of `uv run ruff check .`, same class as the `uv run pyright` lesson). Surfaced when WP-Readme (PR #24) became the first PR to clear sync. Fixed in WP-Lint (PR #25, 0be175a): regen uv.lock + clear all 39. Durable guards: release runbook needs a `uv lock` step after the version bump; standing pre-gate `uv run ruff check .` alongside `uv run pyright`. NOTE: re-record of node lost when the shared-worktree journal was reset by concurrent git ops mid-session.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: S-2 root cause confirmed: agents/plan.md uses old `mcp__vibe-cognition__*` tool names, but plugin MCP tools resolve as `mcp__plugin_<plugin>_<server>__*` — so the Plan agent's cognition tools don't resolve at all
data.detail: claude-code-guide (vs CC docs) confirmed: a plugin-declared MCP server's tools are named `mcp__plugin_<plugin>_<server>__<tool>` (here `mcp__plugin_vibe-cognition_vibe-cognition__cognition_search`). agents/plan.md line 4 still lists the standalone-.mcp.json-era `mcp__vibe-cognition__*` form, which doesn't match the runtime namespace after the plugin migration — the agent's 5 cognition tools silently fail to bind (CC drops unknown tools at load time). WP-Plan-XP-Discovery fixes the prefix (closes S-2) while adding the cross-project tools. Also: an unavailable tool in a subagent `tools:` list is silently dropped at load, NOT a load-time failure — safe to list optional plugin tools (e.g. teammate-comms) for graceful degradation.

data.summary: Manager journal-flush gotcha on Windows: native Python reads `/tmp/...` as `C:\tmp\...` (FileNotFoundError on write) while GitBash coreutils resolve /tmp correctly — convert worktree paths with `cygpath -w` before handing them to Python
data.detail: During a worktree journal flush, `python -c open('/tmp/vc-flush/.cognition/journal.jsonl','wb')` failed FileNotFoundError twice (silently committing docs-only) because Windows Python interprets the MSYS `/tmp` mount as a literal drive path. The relative READ (`.cognition/journal.jsonl`) worked because python's cwd was the repo root. Fix: `WTWIN=$(cygpath -w "$WT")` then build the path under $WTWIN. Also note `git worktree add` on Windows can return before the checkout fully materializes `.cognition/` — a brief race. Prefer pure-coreutils for the complete-records truncate, or cygpath-convert for Python. Verify prefix-integrity (committed journal is a true prefix of the snapshot) and trailing-newline (no torn tail) before committing the flush.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: v0.10.0 post-release verification: auto git-hygiene + Plan-agent cross-project memory both pass on a live updated install
data.detail:
After Colton updated to v0.10.0 (plugin SHA 638e9cc) and restarted the session, Vince verified both shipped features end-to-end on the live install.

FEATURE 1 — Automatic git hygiene (WP-Git-Hygiene-Auto, #27). SessionStart hook announced "vibe-cognition configured: chromadb ignore (.cognition/.gitignore)". On-disk: .cognition/.gitignore written with chromadb/, .git-hygiene-managed, *.lock under the managed header; .git-hygiene-managed sidecar flag present. KEY RESULT: the gitattributes side was a correct no-op — the repo's ROOT .gitattributes already carried `.cognition/journal.jsonl merge=union -text`, so _needs_gitattributes() saw the existing merge= token and left the line untouched (no clobber, no duplicate stanza, no second .cognition/.gitattributes). That is why the hook only announced the gitignore half. Confirms the idempotent, non-destructive, merge=-token-specific design works against a real repo that already had (scarred) journal attrs. The tool correctly does NOT touch a journal line that already has a merge driver, so the pre-existing -text scar is untouched (repo's own legacy concern).

FEATURE 2 — Plan agent cross-project memory (WP-Plan-XP-Discovery, #28; closes S-2). Tasked vibe-cognition:Plan with an embedding model/dimension-mismatch design — a domain MemCog (sibling MCP memory server) has hit. Agent ran the full discipline: discovered sibling via teammate-comms list_projects; resolved label memcog -> C:\Users\colto\Documents\Projects\memcog and confirmed .cognition/journal.jsonl before loading; loaded read-only (400 nodes, model_guard match) — its 5 cognition tools RESOLVED, proving the mcp__plugin_vibe-cognition_vibe-cognition__* prefix fix (S-2) is live; searched + ported the CATEGORY not the command (3 MemCog decisions: one-vector-space, embed-before-write, single-tenant-rebuild-freely), each attributed to MemCog/Reginald, explicitly excluding Voyage/Supabase specifics as non-transferable; unloaded unconditionally. Post-run cognition_list_projects = home only, foreign_count 0 — registry left clean, no leaked foreign graph, READ-ONLY contract intact.

Both features behave exactly as designed in shipped v0.10.0. No defects found.
context: vibe-cognition v0.10.0 post-release live-install verification by Vince (manager). Plugin SHA 638e9cc.

data.summary: Shared-worktree branch-switch clobbered 2 live journal nodes recorded after the last flush — gone from file AND server memory
data.detail: 2026-06-23: Vorpid (implementer) and Vince (manager) operate in the SAME shared working tree. After Vince flushed the journal to main (e99f9e9, 628 records), Vince recorded two decision nodes to the LIVE journal (acb29c42 task-node, d9ca8085 sequencing) — i.e. uncommitted appends, records 629-630. Vorpid then started WP-Test/T-1 by doing git ops in the shared tree: `reset: moving to HEAD` (70bda0f) then `checkout main` then `checkout fix/wp-test-t1` (branched off e99f9e9). Those ops rewrote .cognition/journal.jsonl back to e99f9e9's 628-record version, DISCARDING records 629-630. The running MCP server then detected the rewrite (C-3 replacement detection) and reloaded from the truncated file, so the two nodes were lost from BOTH the file and server memory — cognition_get_node returned 'does not exist'. RECOVERY: Vince had full content in conversation context; re-recorded both as d1192f7e + 6726bb9d (new ids; old-id prose refs left as cosmetic), then froze Vorpid's git ops (urgent blocker msg) and flushed to main before any further branch op. ROOT CAUSE: the shared-worktree journal protocol (manager flushes via TEMP worktree) protects MAIN's journal but does NOT protect live appends made in the shared tree BETWEEN flushes — any branch switch/reset by either agent rewrites the tracked journal.jsonl out from under those appends. This is the high-sev 'destructive-op ban near the journal' constraint manifesting. MITIGATION (decided): (a) manager flushes IMMEDIATELY after recording, never leaving live appends sitting in the shared tree across a teammate's git op; (b) before ANY checkout/reset/pull in the shared tree, the actor must confirm the journal tail is flushed (coordinate via teammate-comms); ideally (c) future: make the live journal resilient to branch switches (e.g. keep it out of the switched branch's tracked set, or a pre-checkout stash-the-journal hook).
context: vibe-cognition shared-worktree journal safety. Vince+Vorpid same tree. 2026-06-23 during WP-Test/T-1 gate.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-Workflow-Node shipped v0.11.0 + two shared-tree recoveries
data.detail:
Shipped the `workflow` first-class node type as v0.11.0 (PR #31, merge 54c35da; marketplace pinned 54c35da, commit 05d4a1e by Loki). The type stores step-by-step procedures as one cohesive, chunk-embedded unit (_embed_workflow on the _embed_document model), versioned by SUPERSESSION (a new full-body node + supersedes edge; head authoritative via get_workflow_head walking incoming supersedes; update_node BLOCKED on workflow to avoid orphaned/stale chunks — blocker B5). Survival pair shipped: /vibe-workflow skill (write trigger) + cognition_get_workflow tool & semantic search (retrieval trigger). Also folded blockers B1 (_INERT_TYPES={DOCUMENT,WORKFLOW} short-circuits the deterministic edge matcher), B9 (dashboard TYPE_COLORS), and four hardcoded type-list docstring refreshes. Gate trail: vibe-cognition:Plan design + sonnet adversarial review (REVISE->folded), manager isolated-worktree verify green @ 6c9a96a (ruff clean, pyright clean but for known server.py:167 local skew, 425 pytest), CI 3-leg green, merged. Vorpid implemented (19 files, +744/-22); Vince planned/reviewed/gated.

TWO RECOVERIES this session, same root cause (shared single working tree between Vince+Vorpid; a branch-switch/reset destroys artifacts that live only in the working tree): (1) live journal appends recorded after the last flush were clobbered by a checkout — now covered by the shared-worktree journal protocol (ping manager to flush/confirm before any checkout/reset/pull). (2) The WP-Workflow-Node planning brief (docs/wp-workflow-node-plan.md) was authored but NEVER committed; the same reset discarded it. Recovered VERBATIM (14049 chars) from the session transcript Write call — not reconstructed — committed (3ec5cac) and independently re-landed on main via temp worktree (470cecf) so it's durable regardless of the feature PR. New prevention rule (routed to Lord-Wellington / EP to fold into shared-worktree guidance): commit planning briefs to main the MOMENT they're written, BEFORE dispatch — a brief referenced by BACKLOG must exist in git, not just on disk.

Process note: the CI Monitor (poll-based, 30s, comm-diff) misfired — emitted only its 15-min timeout despite a ~2min CI completion. Direct `gh pr checks` is the source of truth; verified CLEAN/MERGEABLE before merge rather than trusting monitor silence.
context: Vince (manager) closing out the WP-Workflow-Node release after Loki confirmed the marketplace pin. Session also handled two data-loss recoveries from the shared-worktree hazard.

data.summary: Commit planning briefs to main before dispatch — uncommitted working files are shared-tree casualties
data.detail: In a shared-working-tree setup, a planning brief (docs/wp-*-plan.md) that lives only on disk is destroyed by any branch-switch/reset, exactly like un-flushed journal appends. Prevention: commit the brief to main the MOMENT it's written, BEFORE dispatching the WP. A brief referenced by BACKLOG must exist in git, not just the working tree. Recovery (if lost) is possible verbatim from the session transcript's Write tool_use input, but prevention is cheap and avoids the scramble.

data.summary: vibe-cognition plugin release procedure
data.detail:
Procedure to cut a vibe-cognition plugin release. This repo ships CODE ONLY and does NOT carry its own marketplace.json (a second file named "coltondyck" would collide — Claude Code keys marketplaces by name). The marketplace lives in the separate repo Haagndaazer/colton-claude-plugins (marketplace name "coltondyck"), maintained by Loki.

Steps:
1. Make your code changes on a WP branch.
2. If the change is user-facing, bump the version in BOTH pyproject.toml AND .claude-plugin/plugin.json (the plugin system reads version from plugin.json), and run `uv lock` so uv.lock self-version matches.
3. Pass the WP gate: sonnet-reviewed spec, manager isolated-worktree verify (ruff + whole-repo pyright + pytest at the pinned SHA), CI green on all 3 legs (ubuntu 3.11/3.13, windows 3.11).
4. Merge to main. This is the "code commit" — the SHA the marketplace will pin.
5. Flush the live journal to main via a temp worktree (clean-prefix verified).
6. Ping Loki with the code-commit SHA and the version. Loki re-pins that SHA in colton-claude-plugins's marketplace.json and pushes, so installs/updates pick it up.
7. Release gates on a human's machine: install-mechanics fixes can't be self-verified (no headless plugin install/update harness).

The marketplace sha always points to the code commit on this repo's main (a journal-flush-only commit on top has 0 code delta and is fine to pin too, but convention is to pin the code commit).
context: First real workflow node — recorded to test the v0.11.0 workflow node type end-to-end (chunked embedding
supersession). Content mirrors the Plugin Release Procedure in CLAUDE.md.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: vibe-cognition plugin release procedure
data.detail:
Procedure to cut a vibe-cognition plugin release. This repo ships CODE ONLY and does NOT carry its own marketplace.json (a second file named "coltondyck" would collide — Claude Code keys marketplaces by name). The marketplace lives in the separate repo Haagndaazer/colton-claude-plugins (marketplace name "coltondyck"), maintained by Loki.

Steps:
1. Make your code changes on a WP branch.
2. If the change is user-facing, bump the version in BOTH pyproject.toml AND .claude-plugin/plugin.json (the plugin system reads version from plugin.json), and run `uv lock` so uv.lock self-version matches.
3. Pass the WP gate: sonnet-reviewed spec, manager isolated-worktree verify (ruff + whole-repo pyright + pytest at the pinned SHA), CI green on all 3 legs (ubuntu 3.11/3.13, windows 3.11).
4. Merge to main. This is the "code commit" — the SHA the marketplace will pin.
5. Flush the live journal to main via a temp worktree (clean-prefix verified).
6. Ping Loki with the code-commit SHA and the version. Loki re-pins that SHA in colton-claude-plugins's marketplace.json and pushes, so installs/updates pick it up.
7. CONFIRM with Loki that local==remote on the marketplace repo after the re-pin, and that plugin.json + pyproject.toml both read the new version at the pinned SHA. (NEW in v2: explicit post-pin verification step.)
8. Release gates on a human's machine: install-mechanics fixes can't be self-verified (no headless plugin install/update harness). Have a human run /plugin update and confirm the new version loads.

The marketplace sha always points to the code commit on this repo's main (a journal-flush-only commit on top has 0 code delta and is fine to pin too, but convention is to pin the code commit).

data.summary: WP-Task-Node spec FINAL (dispatched to Vorpid): `task` first-class node type to retire BACKLOG.md — git-resolved creator, mutable status+transition log, arbitrary-depth parent hierarchy w/ atomic re-parent, session-start injection + list tool, folded curate-skill pass
data.detail:
Resolved design contract for the `task` node type (origin decision d1192f7e7bf8; sibling of the shipped `workflow` type). Brief: docs/wp-task-node-plan.md @ commit 4bd2339. Sonnet adversarial peer-review verdict REVISE → 2 blockers + 8 should-fix folded.

CLARIFIERS (Colton, this session): (1) lifecycle = MUTABLE status + transition log (each change stamps {status,at,by} in metadata); (2) EDITABLE in place — opposite of workflow which blocked update_node; (3) CURATED into the graph — opposite of workflow's matcher-inert; (4) ownership = server-resolved git created_by (never client-trusted) + free-form owner; (5) surfaced via session-start prime injection + cognition_list_tasks. Round 2: status vocab open/in_progress/blocked/done/cancelled (reopen allowed, no team review-state); ARBITRARY hierarchy depth (cycle-guarded); list_tasks HOME-project only; write-trigger FOLDED into vibe-cognition SKILL.md (no dedicated /vibe-task skill). Deferred: no BACKLOG.md migrator, done-tasks retained as history, inter-task sequencing post-v1.

TWO BLOCKERS the review caught (would have bitten impl): (B1) `cognition_update_node`/`_update_node` CANNOT write `metadata` (whitelist is summary/detail/context/severity only) and `_embed_entity_node` doesn't embed status/owner — so status edits can't ride update_node; cognition_update_task must mutate metadata + call _embed_entity_node directly, and _embed_entity_node must be extended to surface status/owner. (B2) `_INERT_TYPES` held ONLY workflow — document is handled by separate doc_gated pair-rules, not the inert set; the plan's "alongside workflow/document" was false. Also F6: git_hygiene.py has ZERO subprocess (pure filesystem) — the real git-shelling precedent is hooks/post-commit.py.

KEY DESIGN: severity reused as priority; metadata carries status/created_by/owner/parent_id/transitions; parent is metadata.parent_id (authoritative) mirrored by a part_of edge (source="task-parent"), kept in sync ONLY via add_task/update_task; re-parenting = atomic edge-swap under the lock, cycle-guarded, parent_id="" detaches to top-level; matcher-inert (add task to _INERT_TYPES) but fully /vibe-curate-able; concise single-vector embed (no chunking). Tools: cognition_add_task, cognition_list_tasks, cognition_update_task. Curate pass updates vibe-curate SKILL.md + edge-analyzer.md (task relates_to the decision/pattern it implements; done task resolved_by closing episode; never propose part_of). Land as ONE PR (doc-drift node-type guard is red on steps 1-5). Gate: full WP protocol — Vince does not write code.

The spec deliberately applied the node-type-survival pattern: write trigger folded into SKILL.md + session-start injection as the retrieval trigger, precisely to avoid an orphaned trigger like the dead 'assumption' type.

Task and workflow are sibling planned node-types from the same backlog; the task spec repeatedly contrasts its design against workflow (mutable vs blocked-update, curate-able vs matcher-inert). Peer planning decisions, no causal link.

data.detail: WP-Task-Node: a task minted via cognition_record would carry a client-supplied author and skip server-resolved created_by + status/transition seeding — an un-attributed, lifecycle-less task that guts the WP's core intent. So cognition_record returns an actionable error naming cognition_add_task (mirrors how _update_node blocks workflow). Rejected alt: allow both write paths (cleaner doc-drift symmetry) — but it reintroduces un-attributed tasks. The block is scoped to the WRITE path only: _parse_node_type/the enum still accept 'task', so search/get_history/edgeless/uncurated keep task as a legit filter. data.summary: Extend the SINGLE shared embed path (_embed_entity_node) to surface a new searchable metadata field once — create + re-embed both benefit, no-op for nodes lacking it
data.detail: To make task status/owner both metadata-filterable AND semantically searchable, the change went in ONE place: _embed_entity_node (the single node-vector embed path used by record AND re-embed). It pulls status/owner from node.metadata and appends them to BOTH the Chroma metadata dict and the embed text, only when present. Because it's the shared path, create and cognition_update_task's re-embed are once-and-done; non-task nodes have empty metadata so it's a pure no-op (no regression). The B1 regression guard asserts the SPECIFIC new status string lands in Chroma after update — fails against the un-extended path (not tautological).
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Mutable metadata edit = read-modify-write the WHOLE metadata dict + explicit _embed_entity_node re-embed; cognition_update_node CANNOT write metadata
data.detail: cognition_update_node's whitelist is summary/detail/context/severity only — it physically cannot touch metadata, where task status/owner/parent/transitions live. So cognition_update_task is the ONLY status path: it fetches current metadata, mutates (status + appended {status,at,by} transition, owner, parent), and writes the WHOLE dict back via storage.update_node (which replaces fields wholesale — a partial dict would drop keys). Then it re-embeds explicitly by calling _embed_entity_node on the fresh node (NOT via _update_node), guarded by embeddings_ready (deferred otherwise). priority→severity and summary/detail still ride storage.update_node top-level.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: CI pyright is a baseline RATCHET (count ≤ baseline, currently 8) not zero-gate; local pyright 1.1.408 flags a dashboard install_signal_handlers error in the baseline that CI's pinned pyright doesn't
data.detail: .github/scripts/pyright_ratchet.py fails only if errorCount EXCEEDS .github/pyright-baseline.txt (8). So a change passes the pyright leg as long as it adds NO new type errors — it need not reach zero. Local pyright 1.1.408 reports exactly 1 error (dashboard/server.py:167 uvicorn install_signal_handlers reportAttributeAccessIssue), which is pre-existing and inside the baseline; CI's pinned pyright doesn't flag it (documented in BACKLOG WP-Readme-GitAttr). DO NOT lower the baseline from the local count — local (1) and CI counts differ by pyright version, and editing the baseline requires manager sign-off. WP-Task-Node added 0 new errors.
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Atomic task re-parent: remove ONLY the exact (child→old_parent, part_of) triple — a cluster-membership part_of has a different target so it survives; cycle-guard via metadata.parent_id ancestor walk
data.detail: cognition_update_task(parent_id=...) keeps metadata.parent_id and the part_of edge in sync under the storage path. It removes the old parent edge by the EXACT (child, old_parent, part_of) triple sourced from metadata.parent_id — a /vibe-curate cluster-membership part_of edge points at a DIFFERENT target (a pattern/episode), so it's untouched (no need to inspect edge source). New edge tagged source='task-parent'. Cycle guard walks the new parent's metadata.parent_id chain (cycle-safe via a seen-set) and rejects if the moved task appears. parent_id='' detaches (clear pointer + drop edge); children attach to THIS task so the subtree rides along with one edge change.

data.summary: list_tasks(status="done") returned EMPTY: the default closed-status exclusion fired BEFORE the status filter, contradicting its own docstring; the green suite missed it (no explicit closed-status-filter test)
data.detail: _list_tasks._keep excluded done/cancelled (when include_done=False) before applying the status== filter, so cognition_list_tasks(status="done") dropped every done task → empty, despite the docstring promising "filtering for a closed status implies include_done." Vince caught it on gate of 93441fd via an adversarial read; no test exercised an explicit closed-status filter. Fix: skip the exclusion when `status` itself names a closed status (status not in CLOSED and st in CLOSED). Lesson: when a default-exclusion guard and an explicit filter both gate the same field, an explicit filter for the excluded value must override the default — and that interaction needs its own test (a green suite that only tests the default path hides it).
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Edge-swap atomicity: ADD the new edge first and bail on add_edge()==False BEFORE removing the old edge or writing the mirrored pointer — never leave a metadata pointer with no backing edge
data.detail: storage.add_edge returns False (never raises) if a node vanished since validation (cross-process delete between get_node and the add). _reparent_task originally removed the old part_of edge and wrote metadata.parent_id unconditionally, so a failed add left parent_id pointing at a parent with no edge — breaking depth/cycle traversal. Fix: add the NEW edge first, return an error on False before any other mutation; then remove the old edge (guarded old!=new so a no-op move doesn't drop the just-refreshed edge); then set the pointer. General rule for keeping a mirrored pointer+edge in sync: do the fallible write first, verify it, then mutate the rest.
data.author: Vorpid

data.reason: Hardens the atomic re-parent edge handling: the original pattern swapped the task-parent edge; this adds the add-first/bail-on-False ordering so a failed add can't orphan metadata.parent_id.

DESIGN (final): severity=priority; metadata={status, created_by(server git, never client), owner, parent_id, transitions[]}; parent = metadata.parent_id mirrored by a task-parent part_of edge, kept in sync only via add/update_task; re-parent = atomic cycle-guarded edge-swap, ""=detach; matcher-inert but /vibe-curate-able; concise single-vector embed extended to surface status/owner. See the fail node for a manager journal-flush-topology near-miss this session.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Manager journal-flush near-miss: flushed WP nodes to LOCAL main via temp worktrees but never PUSHED them; the GitHub PR merged onto origin/main (which lacked them), diverging origin-code from local-journal. No data lost (records were in local commits + the live working file); fixed by a reconciliation merge + push.
data.detail:
During WP-Task-Node closeout. The shared-worktree journal-flush protocol (rules-managers §5) says: flush the live journal to main via a temp worktree at checkpoints. I did that correctly THREE times (ed91b7b, c3c89b9, 49463cd) — but only ed91b7b reached origin (it was an ancestor of the pushed WP branch). c3c89b9 + 49463cd were committed to LOCAL main and NEVER PUSHED. When PR #32 merged on GitHub, its base was origin/main (79edb62-era, journal 668), so the merge commit 4d7b85e carried a 668-line journal — missing the 40 records in c3c89b9/49463cd (Vorpid's impl + gate-fix nodes, the WP decision, curate edges). Result: origin/main had the CODE; local main had the JOURNAL; they diverged.

WHY NO LOSS: the records lived in (a) local main commits, (b) the live working journal (708, the server's full view), (c) server memory. The protocol's worktree-flush protected them locally; the gap was purely PUSH.

RECOVERY: a checkout-main attempt was refused (dirty journal vs branch HEAD) and the follow-on merge accidentally ran on the branch (harmless). Reconciled by: snapshot live journal → commit the 40-record superset onto the merged-code commit (hooks off, no auto-episode churn) → point main at it (descends origin/main → FF) → push. origin/main b895890 now = task code + full 708 journal.

LESSON (manager, ledger-worthy): in the shared-checkout topology where the IMPLEMENTER's branch (not main) is what gets pushed + PR-merged, a journal flush to LOCAL main is invisible to the PR merge base. EITHER push main after each flush, OR flush onto a branch that will actually reach origin. A local-only flush protects against branch-switch clobber but NOT against a PR merge building on an origin base that lacks it. Add a pre-merge check: assert the records you flushed are reachable from origin/main BEFORE merging the PR.
data.author: Vince

data.reason: The finalized WP-Task-Node spec contract was the direct trigger for dispatch + the implementation arc that shipped as this episode.
The episode invokes the install-mechanics constraint as the reason v0.12.0 is on main but not released — it gates the release on Colton's real-machine check.
The fail is a gap in the shared-worktree journal-flush protocol constraint: flush-to-local-main was done, but the constraint is silent on PUSHING main, so the PR merge base lacked the records.
Sibling failure modes in the shared-worktree journal-safety area: prior was a branch-switch clobber (data loss); this is a push-gap divergence (no loss). Cross-link surfaces the full failure surface.
Same root topology as the commit-briefs-before-dispatch pattern: in a shared checkout, local-only commits are invisible to a PR merge base on origin.

data.summary: cognition_add_task hung forever (6+ min) in detached MCP server — git-identity subprocess wedged on Windows; dashboard + teammate-comms also hung once server was wedged
data.detail: v0.12.0 task tools: every cognition_add_task call hung indefinitely on Colton's Windows machine (observed 5-6 min before abort). The server was fully wedged — cognition_dashboard and even teammate-comms calls into the same server also hung. Task-specific because only the task tools (add/list/update) call resolve_git_identity server-side; cognition_record takes a client-supplied author and never touches git, so it was unaffected. Initially mis-diagnosed twice (embedding cold-load, then process-pileup lock contention) before a py-spy thread dump located the real wedge. User escalated to P0 and forced evidence over theory.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Root cause: subprocess.run(git, capture_output, timeout=5) never returns in a detached/windowless MCP server — Git-for-Windows launcher never closes stdout pipe, communicate() blocks joining reader thread, timeout= can't fire (block is in pipe drain, not process wait)
data.detail: resolve_git_identity shelled `git config user.name/user.email` via subprocess.run(capture_output=True, timeout=5). In the detached MCP server (spawned by Claude Code via `uv run`, no console/window), the Git-for-Windows launcher process leaves the inherited stdout pipe open, so Python's communicate() blocks forever joining the stdout reader thread. The timeout= parameter governs process *wait*, not the pipe *drain* the call is stuck in, so it never fires — the call hangs indefinitely. Crucially the pytest suite is console-attached and single-process, so it CANNOT reproduce the wedge — green tests gave false confidence. Secondary symptom: when the git call failed fast in other contexts, created_by resolved to the OS user "colto" with empty email instead of "Colton Dyck".
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Fix: resolve git identity by reading git config FILES directly in Python (zero subprocess) — global (GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL→XDG→~/.gitconfig) then local .git/config overriding; getpass→"unknown" fallback; never raises. Rejected: subprocess with hard kill, async subprocess, thread+timeout wrapper
data.detail: Rewrote git_identity.py to remove subprocess entirely and hand-parse git config files: hand-scan the bare [user] section (case-insensitive; [user "sub"] subsections ignored), strip quotes + whitespace-preceded inline comments, merge global then local (membership not truthiness so an explicit empty value clears), getpass.getuser()→"unknown" fallback, never raises. Rejected alternatives: (a) subprocess + hard process kill — still races the pipe drain; (b) async subprocess — the embed path proved sync-vs-async was a red herring, both record and add_task use the same guarded embed; (c) thread+timeout wrapper — leaks a wedged thread per call. File-read is zero-IPC, deterministic, and also fixes attribution (now "Colton Dyck"). Bound by 10 unit tests incl. a never_spawns_subprocess regression pin (spies on subprocess.run/Popen/call/os.system, asserts none called).
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Constraint drift (ledger 21): a "proven" pattern lifted to a new execution context unexamined is NOT proven there. subprocess-git worked in the console-attached post-commit hook; the SAME call wedged in the detached server. Re-validate every environmental assumption when the runtime changes — and measure (thread dump) before theorizing
data.detail: Two compounding lessons from the P0. (1) CONSTRAINT DRIFT: subprocess.run(git...) was a proven pattern in post-commit.py (runs in a console-attached git hook). It was reused in the MCP server's resolve_git_identity without re-examining that the server is detached/windowless — a different execution context where the same call deadlocks. A pattern is only proven in the environment it was proven in; lifting it elsewhere silently re-opens every environmental assumption (console attached? pipes drained? signals deliverable?). (2) MEASURE, DON'T THEORIZE: the bug was mis-diagnosed twice (embedding cold-load, lock contention) by reasoning from plausibility. A py-spy thread dump (`uvx py-spy dump --pid <pid>`) located the exact wedged frame in seconds and ended the guessing. When a process hangs, dump the threads first. Corollary: a green pytest suite that runs in a different process topology (console-attached, single-process) than production (detached) cannot prove the production path — pair it with a mandatory manual check in the real topology.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Live verification on running 0.12.1 server PASSED: cognition_add_task returned instantly (no hang), created_by resolved to "Colton Dyck"/colton.dyck@studiomonsoon.net (not "colto"/empty); list/update/transition-log all server-resolve git correctly. Old SMOKETEST nodes showed pre-fix "colto" attribution side-by-side as before/after proof

After /plugin update + restart, exercised all four task surfaces on the live server: add_task (instant, correct attribution), list_tasks (the two pre-fix SMOKETEST nodes still carried the broken "colto"/empty created_by — a clean side-by-side before/after vs the new node's "Colton Dyck"), update_task (status transition's audit record `by` also server-resolved to "Colton Dyck"), remove_node (cleaned up all three test nodes incl. a cascaded part_of edge). reembed:"done" confirmed the guarded embed path is non-blocking. data.summary: v0.12.1 P0 hotfix: diagnosed + fixed the cognition_add_task infinite hang (git subprocess wedge in detached Windows MCP server) by reading git config files directly. Merged PR #33 (467d73f), live-verified on Colton's machine, Loki pinged for marketplace re-pin. Manager no-code rule overridden for this fix
data.detail: NARRATIVE. v0.12.0 shipped the `task` node type; on first live use cognition_add_task hung 5-6 min and wedged the whole server (dashboard + teammate-comms too). Colton escalated to P0 and asked pointedly whether it was the synchronous embed vs the async pattern. It was NOT — both cognition_record and add_task use the identical guarded embed (`if _embeddings_ready: _embed_entity_node`); the real differentiator is that only task tools resolve git identity server-side. A py-spy thread dump (after two wrong theories — embed cold-load, lock contention) pinned the wedge to subprocess.run(git config...) in resolve_git_identity: in the detached/windowless server the Git-for-Windows launcher never closes the stdout pipe, communicate() blocks forever, and timeout= can't fire. FIX: rewrote git_identity.py to read git config FILES directly (zero subprocess) — global then local override, getpass→"unknown" fallback, never raises — hardened against Path.home() raising, empty-value precedence, [user "sub"] subsections, inline comments/quotes. Bound by 10 unit tests incl. a never_spawns_subprocess regression pin. 476 tests pass, ruff clean, pyright 0-new. Colton OVERRODE the manager no-code rule ("you will need to implement the fix yourself"). Bumped to v0.12.1 (pyproject + plugin.json + uv.lock), merged PR #33 via SHA-pinned squash → main 467d73f (CI green incl. Windows), pinged Loki to re-pin the coltondyck marketplace. Live-verified on the running 0.12.1 server (add_task instant, "Colton Dyck" attribution). LESSON: constraint drift (ledger 21) — a subprocess pattern proven in the console-attached post-commit hook deadlocked in the detached server; re-validate environmental assumptions on a runtime change, and measure (thread dump) before theorizing. Follow-ups queued: server-lifecycle self-exit WP (docs/wp-server-lifecycle-plan.md), backfill.py subprocess hardening (same pattern, CLI-only today).

data.summary: Tool-surface self-sufficiency audit (RECURRING) — re-run every time an MCP tool is added or changed: confirm each tool is usable from its name/args/description/return-shape ALONE, with no user-supplied convention
data.detail: STANDING AUDIT (Colton, 2026-06-21). Not one-and-done — a recurring obligation, modeled as a workflow (not a task) because it never reaches "done". TRIGGER: every time an MCP tool is added or its signature/description changes. PROCEDURE: (1) Read each tool's name, args, description, and return shape in ISOLATION — assume the LLM has NO extra rules or prompting from the user; the tool surface is the SOLE contract. (2) Confirm: the purpose is clear from the description alone; every arg is unambiguous (types, defaults, what each does); the return shape is documented; error semantics are stated; any special semantics ("*"/project routing, status enums, server-resolved fields) are spelled out in the docstring, not assumed. (3) Flag any tool that needs context the user would otherwise have to supply. (4) Pairs with the SKILL.md tool-table drift check (S-3) — when a tool changes, the skill table and README must track it. SCOPE NOTE at last review: sweep newly-shipped tools first (the XP tools cognition_load_project/_unload_project/_list_projects + the project arg; and now the task tools cognition_add_task/_list_tasks/_update_task shipped v0.12.0). Provenance: archived from docs/BACKLOG.md "Standing audits" at the 2026-06-25 task-node migration.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: [SHIPPED v0.12.0] task node type — the one that retires BACKLOG.md
data.detail: Merged PR #32 (4d7b85e), main reconciled b895890; episode 21fa79e52827. First-class `task` node type for trackable open work: created_by resolved SERVER-SIDE from git config (never client-trusted; new git_identity.py), mutable lifecycle (open/in_progress/blocked/done/cancelled) + transition log, severity=priority, arbitrary-depth re-parentable hierarchy (atomic cycle-guarded edge-swap), graph-inert to the matcher but /vibe-curate-able, session-start prime injection + cognition_list_tasks. Tools add_task/list_tasks/update_task; cognition_record rejects node_type=task (write-only redirect). THIS migration (2026-06-25) is the retirement of BACKLOG.md it was built to enable. NB: v0.12.0 shipped a P0 — the git resolver hung in the detached server; fixed in v0.12.1.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: [SHIPPED v0.12.1] git-identity P0 hotfix — file-read, never subprocess
data.detail: Merged PR #33 (467d73f); episode ff601364f0ef. Fixed the cognition_add_task infinite hang: resolve_git_identity shelled git config via subprocess, which wedged forever in the detached/windowless MCP server (Git-for-Windows launcher never closes the stdout pipe; communicate() blocks; timeout= can't fire). Rewrote git_identity.py to read git config FILES directly in Python (zero subprocess) — global then local override, getpass->"unknown" fallback, never raises; bound by a never_spawns_subprocess regression pin. Live-verified on Colton's machine (add_task instant, created_by "Colton Dyck"). Lesson: constraint drift (ledger 21).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-Server-Lifecycle: orphan server self-exit on parent death (Windows process pileup)
data.detail: Reliability bug exposed by the v0.12.1 firefight: ~11 orphaned vibe_cognition.server processes accumulated on Windows (Claude Code spawns one per session, never reaps old ones), several holding 0.5–1 GB. Plan already drafted + peer-reviewed: docs/wp-server-lifecycle-plan.md. Mechanism = additive in-process self-exit when the launching parent is gone (self-regarding, concurrency-safe — concurrent same-repo sessions are first-class valid; never kills a live server). Phase 0 read-only investigation of why stdin-EOF doesn't reach the detached server on Windows, then the self-exit backstop + a one-time manual cleanup command. Target ~v0.13.0.
data.author: Colton Dyck

It's CLI-only today (console-attached), so lower urgency, but it must be hardened before any server-side/detached use. Apply the file-read or no-pipe-drain approach; add a regression guard.

data.summary: Low-pri P3-audit task nodes went STALE: re-verifying the 2026-06-10 residuals vs current src, ~70% already shipped. Trust the code, not the year-old audit note — re-verify every residual against HEAD before dispatch.
data.detail:
Human asked to clear all low-pri backlog as one invisible-hardening pass. Mandatory peer review of my decomposition + a decorrelated ground-truth verification agent both found most "residuals" already SHIPPED since the 2026-06-10 audit filed them.

CONFIRMED PHANTOM / ALREADY-DONE (close, no work):
- 7851da60 search over-query unify — dashboard/api.py:239 already calls adaptive_vector_search; _SEARCH_OVERQUERY_K lives in storage.py:270. DONE.
- 134076ab IPv6 [::1] host-check — middleware.py:31 already has "[::1]:". Dup type/edge_type keys — none (type at api.py:117; edge_type at 137/141, different endpoints). Unused context/severity payload — /api/graph never emits them, /api/node emits+uses them (app.js:200-203). All phantom.
- a4f99136 bulk_upsert — already pruned (E-6), zero hits.
- d7071a43 D-3 search-wiring-on-init — app.js:497-514 already wires listeners before any await; comment documents it. DONE.
- 650ce498 dead `json` import in backfill.py — does not exist; session-start.sh header comment accurate. Phantom.
- f70c9ad1 S-3: README dashboard-instructions already flag CLI-vs-plugin (162-175); edge-types consistent (models.py:41-47 = 7 types; edge-analyzer lists 5 semantic by design); vibe-backfill SKILL.md:125 says "MUST" not "consider"; instructions.py IS stdlib-only (json+sys) with correct hook name. Phantom.

GENUINELY REMAINING, confidently invisible (dispatched):
- D-5(c) port dedup: dashboard_tool.py:22 literal 7842 → DEFAULT_PORT (dashboard/server.py:33).
- E-8 prune generate_batch: generator.py:173, zero callers in src/ or tests (test_e3_doc_prefix.py is a spy stub, not a caller).
- H-6(a) breadcrumb-log the two swallowed excepts: backfill.py:49,65 — add logger.debug before return [], control flow unchanged.
- H-6(c) migrate_mcp.py:68 encoding utf-8 → utf-8-sig (BOM .mcp.json currently silently skipped).
- H-6(d) remove redundant hatch force-include pyproject.toml:41-42 — REQUIRES wheel build+diff (must prove app.js still ships; install-mechanics can't be self-verified per standing constraint).
- H-5 scope SessionStart entry-1 matcher → "startup|resume|clear" (hooks.json). Claude Code guide: NO negation supported; explicit alternation is the only safe form; never ""/"*"/omitted (would break first-install startup).
- S-3(b) README:83 "15 tools" → "29 tools"; S-3(e) prime.py:145 docstring drop bogus "PreCompact".

PARKED for human (out of invisible scope or a deliberate-decision conflict):
- H-3 (33719f0d): persist hook stderr instead of 2>/dev/null — the only non-invisible item (creates a log file). Design/ops call.
- D-5(d) ExitStack-close-on-join-timeout: code comment says leaving it open is INTENTIONAL (daemon thread exits with process); closing under a still-alive daemon thread risks use-after-close. Likely WONTFIX.
- H-6(b) expose --days CLI param: a feature, not invisible.
- D-3 auto-poll + --no-embeddings banner: visible UX features.

LESSON: an audit's residual list is a claim with a timestamp; by the time you action it, parallel WPs may have closed items. Re-verify each against HEAD before planning a dispatch — the peer-review hard rule is what caught this.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Low-pri hardening re-scoped to 7 invisible deltas in 2 WPs: WP-LP-A (src/doc one-liners) + WP-LP-B (hooks.json matcher + hatch force-include, both build/CC-verified). 4 items parked for human. Forks resolved per invisible-only invariant.
data.detail:
After the stale-audit reconciliation, the real remaining low-pri work is 7 tiny, behavior-preserving edits. Decomposed into two work packages with low file overlap:

WP-LP-A — src/doc tidies (all invisible, no behavior change on success path):
- D-5(c) dashboard_tool.py:22 → import DEFAULT_PORT.
- E-8 prune generator.py generate_batch (dead).
- H-6(a) backfill.py:49,65 add logger.debug breadcrumbs (flow unchanged).
- H-6(c) migrate_mcp.py:68 utf-8-sig.
- S-3(b) README:83 15→29 tools; S-3(e) prime.py:145 docstring fix.
Files: dashboard_tool.py, generator.py, backfill.py, migrate_mcp.py, README.md, prime.py — no intra-WP overlap.

WP-LP-B — config/packaging (verify by building, isolated for a careful gate):
- H-5 hooks.json entry-1 matcher → "startup|resume|clear" (CC guide: no negation; alternation only; never blank/*).
- H-6(d) remove pyproject.toml:41-42 hatch force-include. ACCEPTANCE: build wheel before+after, prove vibe_cognition/dashboard/static/app.js present in BOTH; if absent after, ABORT this change. (Standing constraint: install-mechanics can't be self-verified — wheel-content diff is the strongest local proof.)

FORKS RESOLVED (one direction each, per invisible-only invariant):
- 7851da60 unify-vs-document → moot (already unified).
- a4f99136 batch-rewire-vs-prune → PRUNE only; startup hot-path rewire deferred (perf ≠ invisible).
- H-6 git-failures swallowed → LOG breadcrumb, PRESERVE control flow/exit codes.
- H-6 --days → DEFER (feature).
- H-3 log destination → parked entirely (persist-or-not is the human's call).

KNOWN-INTENTIONAL — do NOT "fix":
- D-5(d) ExitStack left open on join-timeout is a documented deliberate choice; closing under a live daemon thread risks use-after-close.
- v0.12.1 git-identity = config-file read, zero subprocess; backfill tidy must NOT reintroduce subprocess-git. backfill SUBPROCESS HARDENING is task 8cd9c158 (NORMAL pri, separate WP) — keep out of H-6.
- .cognition/*.jsonl merge=union -text gitattributes load-bearing; chromadb under .cognition/ intentionally gitignored.
- Dashboard user-facing default limit=20 intentional.

Process: plan peer-reviewed (sonnet) per hard rule — review caught the stale-audit phantom class. Re-scoped plan is a strict subset, each item independently file:line-verified by a decorrelated agent; no third review for 7 one-liners.

data.summary: v0.13.0: trim session-start prime (~1346→~634 tok, ~53%) + REMOVE the post-commit journal hook (auto-episode-on-commit re-dirtied the tree after clean commits)
data.detail:
Two bundled changes, both user-facing session/hook behavior, shipped in v0.13.0.

PART 1 — prime trim. prime.py is the entire steady-state session-start token cost (dep-sync/migration only emit on install/upgrade). Made it a "get your bearings" digest; full context is pulled on demand via cognition_search/get_history/list_tasks. Levers: (A) counts — tasks 10→5, patterns/decisions 5→3, incidents 30d→14d; (B) _truncate summaries at 110 chars on a word boundary with a HARD-CUT fallback for no-whitespace strings; (C) severity gating — incidents high+ only, constraints drop-only-`low` (C2); (E) 7 env-overridable Settings knobs. Threaded via a frozen PrimeConfig dataclass (NOT Settings-read-inside-formatters); main() builds config from Settings in one try/except and falls back to PrimeConfig() defaults == the same trimmed shape, so a broken env never goes silent nor reverts to the old fat output. Measured 5387→2539 chars.

PART 2 — removed the post-commit journal hook (hooks/post-commit.py/.sh + PostToolUse wiring). It appended an episode to .cognition/journal.jsonl after every git commit, re-dirtying the tree the instant after a clean commit — redundant with deliberate cognition_record. Kept /vibe-backfill (opt-in recovery) + journal_io (server caller).

REJECTED ALTERNATIVES: (1) read Settings() directly inside _format_* — rejected: couples formatting to live env, breaks test isolation, and a formatter exception would crash the hook into silence (all context lost); chose PrimeConfig dataclass threaded as a param. (2) gate constraints to high+ (C1) — rejected: hides no-severity/normal hard rules (e.g. the marketplace.json ban); chose C2 (drop only `low`, keep None/normal/high/critical). (3) single global prime_min_severity gate — rejected: collides with C2; split into a dedicated incident-only validated Literal knob. (4) truncate cutting at last whitespace only — rejected: a no-whitespace URL/hash would drop just its last char (misleading near-complete bullet); added hard-cut-at-maxlen branch. (5) drop Decisions+Patterns sections entirely (lever D) — parked to phase-2. (6) keep the hook — rejected: redundant + the headache. Two sonnet peer reviews caught the C1/C2 collision, the silent-hook-crash risk, the no-whitespace truncation bug, an invalid-JSON hooks.json comma (would silently kill SessionStart), and two extra hook-loading tests in test_journal_concurrency.py that would have failed the suite.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP brief: prime token trim + post-commit hook removal (v0.13.0)
data.detail:
WP: Prime token trim + post-commit hook removal (v0.13.0). Branch wp-prime-hook. Implementer Vorpid, reviewer/release Vince. Two parts, one release.

PART 1 — Trim the session-start prime injection (~1,346 → ~634 tok measured). PrimeConfig frozen dataclass in prime.py holds 7 trim knobs as defaults (single source of truth); generate_prime(storage, config=None) defaults to PrimeConfig(); _format_* take plain limit/maxlen params — NO Settings import in formatting logic. main() builds config from Settings in one try/except, falls back to PrimeConfig() (same trimmed shape). config.py adds 7 env-overridable Settings fields with defaults == PrimeConfig: prime_constraint_limit=5, prime_task_cap=5, prime_pattern_limit=3, prime_decision_limit=3, prime_incident_days=14, prime_summary_maxlen=110, prime_incident_min_severity=Literal high. _truncate cuts at last whitespace before maxlen; hard-cut at maxlen when no whitespace (prevents URL/hash dropping only last char). Severity gating: incidents keep severity>=min within days window; constraints drop only 'low' (C2 — keep None/normal/high/critical). Tests: truncation no-whitespace hard-cut, task cap via PrimeConfig, incident gate, constraint C2, PrimeConfig==Settings defaults, rewritten 14d-boundary test (10d kept / 20d excluded).

PART 2 — Remove the post-commit journal hook. It appended an episode to .cognition/journal.jsonl after every git commit, re-dirtying the tree after a clean commit. Delete hooks/post-commit.py, hooks/post-commit.sh, the PostToolUse block in hooks/hooks.json (AND strip the trailing comma on SessionStart's closing line or the JSON goes invalid and kills SessionStart), tests/test_post_commit_hook.py, and the 2 hook-loading tests + _HOOK var + importlib import in tests/test_journal_concurrency.py. Reference cleanup: server.py comment, README (3 sites), journal_io.py docstring, git_identity.py comment (annotated 'now removed'), CHANGELOG. Keep journal_io (server caller), backfill, /vibe-backfill. Bump 0.12.
data.author: Colton Dyck
