data.summary: v0.15.3 shipped a mid-session tool-freeze: bg heavy-import wedges in native DLL load, dispatch hangs forever
data.detail: The background heavy import (sentence_transformers/torch/scipy) wedges in native DLL load under load, holding the Windows loader lock + import locks; tool dispatch then hangs forever and the MCP client never times out mid-session. Pre-0.15.3 the same wedge was at least VISIBLE as a 30s connect-timeout at launch; WP-C's lazy import moved it mid-session where nothing bounds it. Agents' vibe-cognition tool calls intermittently stalled forever, project-wide.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: v0.15.4 post-fix wedges same day: probe ok 7.3s yet in-process load 1,463s; both loop-alive and loop-frozen modes captured
data.detail: Same-day recurrence after WP-Wedge shipped: pid 35220 (loop-alive mode) — import probe succeeded in 7.3s yet the in-process load took 1,463s; get_status hung 21min post-watchdog-fire while cancel answered instantly. pid 50760 (loop-frozen mode) py-spy'd mid-wedge: MainThread stuck in Thread.start() called from fastmcp dispatch, bg thread in create_module under scipy — the OS loader lock held by the bg import froze the event loop itself. pid 2604 reproduced the loop-alive mode. Proved the probe premise false and motivated WP-Wedge-2 + the sidecar endgame.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: A subprocess import probe does NOT immunize the in-process import — separate process means separate loader state
data.detail: WP-Wedge §3a's premise — that a successful DEVNULL subprocess import probe warms the OS/disk state so the subsequent in-process import is safe — was falsified in production: probe ok in 7.3s, in-process load then wedged for 1,463s (pid 35220). A child process's DLL loader state, import machinery, and lock acquisition are entirely its own; nothing about its success transfers to the parent process's import. Only moving the import OUT of the server process permanently (sidecar) removes the risk class.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-Wedge design: DEVNULL-only probe + 120s watchdog with late recovery + warm anyio workers
data.detail: Chosen: subprocess import probe (DEVNULL-only stdio per the v0.12.1 lesson) before the in-process load, a 120s watchdog that degrades embedding state with LATE RECOVERY (never permanent), and pre-warmed anyio worker threads. Rejected: reverting WP-C's lazy import (loses the launch-stability win that fixed the 30s connect-timeout class); building the sidecar immediately (right durable fix, too big for the P0 clock — staged instead as the endgame). The probe premise was later falsified in production; watchdog + late recovery carried the value until the sidecar subsumed both.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-Wedge-2: INV-1 spawn-free event loop + INV-2 import-free tool surface + 300s watchdog + dispatch-stall self-forensics
data.detail: INV-1: the event loop must never spawn a thread at dispatch time — dedicated prewarmed ThreadPoolExecutor dispatch seam (@dispatch_tool); the stdio transport keeps anyio's warm pool + heartbeat. INV-2: the tool surface must be import-free at runtime — function-body-import AST guard across src/. Plus watchdog raised to 300s and a _DispatchStallForensics middleware that dumps all stacks if dispatch exceeds 30s. §W2-a forensics NEGATIVE ratified: pure Python cannot reproduce either production wedge mode (per-module import lock ≠ OS loader lock), so WP2-AC2 was replaced by the production stack-dump instead of a vacuous repro test.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Full-fix program ordering: Wedge-2 → Lifecycle → Sidecar; Lifecycle promoted P1 because orphans amplify the wedge
data.detail: Three-layer program to fully fix the wedge P0: WP-Wedge-2 first (dispatch survives wedges — stops the bleeding), then WP-Lifecycle promoted to P1 (orphaned servers churn disk/CPU and their concurrent model loads are the stampede that wedges the NEXT load — the problem is self-sustaining until orphans self-exit), then WP-Sidecar as the durable fix (the heavy import leaves the server process entirely). Rejected: jumping straight to the sidecar (leaves sessions unprotected during the build); treating lifecycle as low-priority cleanup (it is a wedge AMPLIFIER, not hygiene).
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-Wedge → v0.15.4: RCA, brief rev3, two fix rounds, gate green 682, shipped and pinned f7089ba
data.detail: RCA from py-spy pid 78496 + breadcrumbs + client logs pinned the bg heavy-import wedge. Brief rev3 (9fb55c2) survived 2 peer reviews — rev-1's PIPE-probe defect (undrained pipe = the v0.12.1 wedge class) caught pre-implementation. Vorpid implemented (ffed2ed → 0f7a446 → d0f5902 across an AC3-near-vacuous HOLD and a comment fix round). Gate: 682 green. Merge 39ba4df, ship 9ce361b, marketplace pin f7089ba by Loki. Same-day production wedges then falsified the probe premise — the watchdog + late recovery carried the value forward.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-Wedge-2 → v0.15.5: two FAIL brief reviews caught real design defects; gate with tautology drives; shipped and pinned d72468a
data.detail: Briefs 413b373/916ca3a survived 2 adversarial reviews, both FAIL rounds catching real defects pre-implementation: rev-1 a 2-BLOCKER round incl. AC-fidelity, rev-2 the stdio-transport-rides-anyio-pool BLOCKER (the dispatch seam must NOT replace the transport's warm pool). Vorpid implemented 9274a4d. Gate: 708 green, chromadb cross-process failure proven pre-existing by rerunning at base SHA, tautology drives, adversarial PASS with 1 MAJOR (ordering-pin gap) fixed in 1fd8a49. Merge 680bf88, ship 61b52bb, pin d72468a. Production proof came later: a live wedged server answered get_status instantly while degraded.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-Lifecycle → v0.15.6: uv-intermediary BLOCKER caught in brief review; WAIT_FAILED MAJOR fixed with failure-drive; shipped and pinned cbf36a1
data.detail: Brief rev-1 FAILED review on the uv-intermediary BLOCKER: the plugin launches server via uv, so a direct-parent watch would watch uv and deadlock the pair when the client dies — THE reason orphans come in pairs; the fix watches BOTH uv and the client via NtQueryInformationProcess ancestor walk + WaitForMultipleObjects. Vorpid implemented 3a6f82f: ancestor-death watch, PeekNamedPipe stdin watch (FILE_TYPE_PIPE-gated), armed pre-yield, os._exit. Gate 737 green; adversarial PASS with a WAIT_FAILED silent-busy-loop MAJOR found independently by manager and reviewer; fixed ba1e4dd (740 green; failure-drive: stripped fix → regression test hangs, pytest-timeout kill). Merge 1789233, ship 419a616, pin cbf36a1. Production proof: clean instance close reaps the pair.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Python default args bind at def time — monkeypatching module constants after def has no effect on already-bound defaults
data.detail: A function signature like def f(timeout=MODULE_CONSTANT) captures the constant's VALUE at def time; monkeypatching the module constant later does not change the default the function actually uses. For testability, parameterize signatures (pass the value through) or read the constant inside the body. (WP-Wedge for-the-record item.)
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WP-Wedge AC1 first cut: monkeypatched probe whose fake re-imported the patched name → infinite recursion
data.detail: The AC1 test's fake probe re-imported the very name that had been monkeypatched, so the fake called itself — infinite recursion. Fix: the fake returns its result directly instead of re-importing. Lesson: a test double must not resolve the patched symbol through the import system it is standing in for. (WP-Wedge for-the-record item.)
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: fastmcp Context.request_context rides a contextvar that anyio to_thread propagates but bare ThreadPoolExecutor.submit does NOT
data.detail: fastmcp's Context.request_context is a contextvar. anyio's to_thread.run_sync copies context automatically; a bare ThreadPoolExecutor.submit does NOT — so the WP-Wedge-2 dispatch seam must wrap calls in contextvars.copy_context().run(). Only caught by real in-memory fastmcp Client dispatch testing; mock_mcp structurally can never see it because it bypasses the dispatch layer entirely. (WP-Wedge-2 for-the-record item.)
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WaitForMultipleObjects rejects GetCurrentProcess() pseudo-handles; uv's .venv Scripts\python.exe is a trampoline adding a process tier
data.detail: Two Windows quirks from WP-Lifecycle: (1) WaitForMultipleObjects flatly rejects the GetCurrentProcess() pseudo-handle with ERROR_INVALID_HANDLE — real handles must come from OpenProcess. (2) uv's generated .venv/Scripts/python.exe on Windows is its own small trampoline binary, not a copy of the base interpreter — invoking it directly (bypassing `uv run`) inserts an extra unplanned process tier, which briefly looked like an ancestor-walk bug. (WP-Lifecycle for-the-record item.)
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: WP-Lifecycle test doubles "passed" BECAUSE of the WAIT_FAILED silent busy-loop bug they should have caught
data.detail: Several WP-Lifecycle test doubles used GetCurrentProcess() pseudo-handles, whose waits always returned WAIT_FAILED — and the WAIT_FAILED silent busy-loop bug meant exit_fn was simply never called, which the tests read as "no false exit = pass." The doubles were unknowingly relying on the very bug under test. Fixed to real OpenProcess handles when the bug itself was fixed (ba1e4dd). Lesson: a test that passes for the wrong mechanical reason is worse than a failing one — failure-drive the fix path. (WP-Lifecycle for-the-record item.)
data.author: Vorpid

data.summary: Mock-based tool tests structurally cannot catch dispatch-layer defects — real in-memory fastmcp Client dispatch is mandatory for dispatch-adjacent ACs
data.detail: Mock-based tool tests bypass threading, contextvars, and schema handling at the dispatch layer, so they structurally cannot catch defects there. Twice-proven in this program: (1) the contextvars propagation bug was only visible via real in-memory fastmcp Client dispatch; (2) WP-Wedge's AC3 test passed on the very build that hung production. Rule: any AC that touches dispatch behavior (threading, timeouts, context propagation, degraded-state gating) must be verified through real dispatch, not mock_mcp.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Sessions stay pinned to stale pre-update servers until manual /mcp reconnect; server spawn also races the SessionStart venv sync
data.detail: Variant 1: sessions stay pinned to stale pre-update wedged servers until a manual /mcp reconnect; the client's "Terminating MCP server process tree" sometimes fails to reap (3 same-morning hangs all traced to pre-venv-sync servers; venv synced 09:08:36, last old spawn 09:08:33); clean instance close DOES reap. Variant 2 (pinned at the 0.16.0 rollout, 2026-07-06 evening): server spawned 18:27:48 FROM the 0.16.0 --directory, but the SessionStart hook's uv sync flipped the venv's editable install 0.15.6→0.16.0 at 18:27:50 — Python bound site-packages at interpreter startup 2s earlier, so a server launched from the NEW plugin root ran the OLD code (probe breadcrumbs present, which 0.16.0 deletes). Server spawn races the SessionStart venv sync: launch-directory version ≠ imported-code version. Remedy in both variants: /mcp reconnect after the sync settles.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Concurrent model loads are the wedge trigger: 27s healthy load vs 1,463s under stampede; orphan churn is self-sustaining
data.detail: The wedge is load-dependent, not random: a healthy sentence-transformers model load takes ~27s; under a stampede of concurrent loads (multiple sessions/orphans loading at once) the same load took 1,463s. Orphan churn is self-sustaining — each wedged orphan adds the disk pressure that wedges the NEXT load, which creates more orphans. This is the direct evidence for both WP-Lifecycle's P1 promotion (break the churn) and WP-Sidecar's machine-wide load mutex (serialize the loads).
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-Sidecar → v0.16.0 (P0 endgame): heavy import leaves the server process; gate fix round with 4 failure drives; shipped and pinned 2a3a43c
data.detail: The durable fix: embeddings/sidecar.py becomes the ONE sanctioned heavy-import site, run as a supervised subprocess; _load_mutex.py (Local\vibe-cognition-model-load named mutex, WAIT_ABANDONED = designed acquisition) serializes model loads machine-wide, killing the stampede trigger; sidecar_client.py's SidecarSupervisor is the sole writer of embedding state with in-budget kill/respawn/backoff + lazy-on-demand (notify_demand from get_lifespan) + periodic recovery; probe and watchdog deleted as subsumed. Vorpid implemented 93a447a; gate found 3 BLOCKERs (dead notify_demand, generate() failure path not writing embedding_error, hardcoded stub path) + 1 MAJOR (untested version-skew claim); fix round 873e7b0 also fixed a self-found send_load lock-vs-response race. Re-gate: 754/1 pinned rerun, 4 failure drives (embedding_error write, notify_demand wiring, heavy-chain guard flip, WAIT_ABANDONED semantics) all non-vacuous. Merge + ship 7a595c8 as v0.16.0 (minor bump — architecture changed shape), Loki pin 2a3a43c. Live validation same evening: fresh 0.16.0 server went bg_model_load_start → lock_acquired → bg_model_loaded → bg_sync_done in ~13 seconds, embedding_status ready — first fully healthy server of the saga, on the fix itself.
data.author: Vince

transitions.note: Shipped as WP-Lifecycle in v0.15.6 (419a616, marketplace pin cbf36a1): ancestor-death watch over uv AND client (NtQueryInformationProcess + WaitForMultipleObjects; direct-parent watch would deadlock the uv pair), PeekNamedPipe stdin watch (FILE_TYPE_PIPE-gated), armed pre-yield, os._exit. Production-proven: clean instance close reaps the server pair.

data.summary: Shared cross-session embedding daemon (one torch heap per machine)
data.detail: Follow-up epic per wp-sidecar-plan §S-e. WP-Sidecar's per-server sidecars still cost one torch heap per session, and time-to-ready under fleet load is bounded only per-load by the mutex (serialized loads mean the Nth session's wait is unbounded in fleet size). A single machine-wide embedding daemon shared by all sessions removes both. The sidecar protocol was deliberately kept transport-agnostic so nothing in the shipped design precludes this.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: dashboard run_in_threadpool spawn risk on its own uvicorn loop
data.detail: Residual outside INV-1's scope: dashboard/api.py:259 uses run_in_threadpool on the dashboard's own uvicorn event loop, which can spawn threads at request time — the same risk class INV-1 eliminated on the MCP dispatch path, but on a separate loop. Opt-in feature (dashboard must be explicitly launched), so low priority; fix is the same prewarmed-executor shape if it ever matters.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.detail:
The WP-4 parity test test_reconciler_embed_matches_embed_entity_node_directly failed intermittently twice during the 2026-07-02 overnight burn-down full-suite runs, passing in isolation and on immediate re-run both times. First sighting was attributed to mid-edit tree state (gate run while implementer had files half-written) — plausible then. Second sighting (during WP-11, no embeddings/chromadb code in the diff) surfaced actual failure detail: a chromadb rust-backend InternalError — which weakens the mid-edit theory and suggests a real low-frequency flake in the chromadb persistence layer under rapid suite-wide create/write/delete of collections (Windows; pinned chromadb 1.5.5). Neither sighting reproduced under isolation or x3 re-runs; committed content double-green at pins throughout. Investigate: capture the full InternalError next occurrence (run suite with -ra or --lf loop), check if it correlates with test ordering/tmp-dir reuse/handle cleanup, consider a targeted retry marker (pytest-rerunfailures) for the chromadb-touching tests ONLY if root-causing stalls — do not blanket-retry the suite. Related prior art: wp-emb-e4 chromadb rust-lock discovery (SQLite lock contention class).

APPEND (2026-07-06, WP-Sidecar gate evidence): also fails as AttributeError "'RustBindingsAPI' object has no attribute 'bindings'" under concurrent PersistentClient opens — a DIFFERENT signature that EVADES _retry_chromadb_open (which retries on InternalError only). LOAD-dependent, now proven: test_chromadb_cross_process failed 3/3 in the gate worktree under an active model-load stampede AND identically at the base SHA (pre-existing), then passed on a calm box. Same AttributeError signature also surfaced once in test_e3_doc_prefix's reconciler-parity test during Vorpid's WP-Lifecycle fix-round run. Any retry fix must cover both signatures (InternalError AND the RustBindingsAPI AttributeError).

From the WP-Lint finding (discovery bfba9f13e0b1). Add a `uv lock` step to the release runbook AFTER the version bump (the v0.9.0 release bumped version without uv lock → silently-red CI). Adopt a standing pre-gate `uv run ruff check .` (not bare ruff) alongside `uv run pyright`. PIN the pyright version (PYRIGHT_PYTHON_FORCE_VERSION or a deps pin) so local == CI — surfaced in WP-Readme-GitAttr where local pyright 1.1.408 flagged a uvicorn stub diagnostic CI's pinned pyright doesn't.

APPEND (2026-07-06, WP-Wedge-2 dependency risk): pyproject floats fastmcp>=2.0.0 unbounded while the WP-Wedge-2 dispatch seam depends on fastmcp 3.1.1 behavior (call_sync_fn_in_threadpool routing) — a silent `uv sync` drift to a future fastmcp could invalidate the INV-1 premise without any test necessarily failing loudly. Add an upper bound or pin discipline for fastmcp to this task's scope.

data.summary: glb-splitter-c: MSVC C1128 Debug fix via $<$<CXX_COMPILER_ID:MSVC>:/bigobj>, coordinate with Gregovich
data.detail: Relay note recorded in vibe-cognition's graph for tracking only — the work belongs to the glb-splitter-c project. MSVC Debug builds fail with C1128 (number of sections exceeded object file format limit); fix is adding /bigobj for MSVC via the CMake generator expression $<$<CXX_COMPILER_ID:MSVC>:/bigobj>. Pre-existing issue. Provenance: subordinate-reported by Gina via Gabrielle. Coordinate with Gregovich, who works that project.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Dashboard: surface task, document, and workflow nodes (currently invisible in the graph view)
data.detail: Extend the dashboard to show the three newer node types: tasks (with status/priority/owner — ideally a backlog-style view honoring the open/in_progress/blocked/done/cancelled lifecycle), documents (with staleness/freshness indicator), and workflows (HEADs only, respecting supersession like prime does). Acceptance: each type is visible and distinguishable in the dashboard; task lifecycle state is readable at a glance; superseded workflow versions are not shown as live. Colton request 2026-07-07.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Shipped Plan agent: account for task/document/workflow nodes when planning
data.detail: Revise the plugin's shipped vibe-cognition:Plan agent so plans consider the newer node types: check the open-task backlog (cognition_list_tasks) for existing/overlapping work, retrieve relevant workflows (cognition_get_workflow) for established procedures, and pull stored documents (cognition_get_document) when a spec exists. Note: the agent's current tool allowlist has cognition_search/get_chain/get_history/get_neighbors/get_status/load-unload-list_projects but NOT cognition_list_tasks, cognition_get_workflow, or cognition_get_document — both the tool list and the agent's prompt instructions need updating. Acceptance: Plan agent definition grants the three tools and its instructions direct it to consult backlog/workflows/documents before proposing a plan. Colton request 2026-07-07.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP brief: Personalized session-start prime (WP-P13n-1 provenance stamps, WP-P13n-2 prime personalization) — approved scope
data.detail:
Scope: Personalized Session-Start Prime (per-git-user context). Status: APPROVED by Colton 2026-07-07. Vorpid implements; Vince gates.

FINALIZED DECISIONS (Colton, 2026-07-07): 1. Auto-detect default: solo graphs (<=1 distinct non-empty stamped email) get byte-identical output; multi-user graphs personalize automatically; PRIME_PERSONALIZE=auto|on|off env override ships. 2. "Your recent activity" includes episodes, decisions AND discoveries, capped at 5 of each (per-type caps, config knobs). Exceeds the original flat-token recommendation; growth is Colton-approved. Acceptance: measure and report multi-user prime size at gate; solo prime byte-identical. 3. Task-claiming IN scope: cognition_update_task status->in_progress stamps metadata.claimed_by={name,email} from git identity (file-reads only); free-text owner untouched, display-only. "Your Open Tasks" matches created_by.email OR claimed_by.email. 4. The _format_constraints HEAD-filter fix (task 0d7e84d52537) folds into WP-P13n-2.

PROBLEM: hooks/session-start.sh -> python -m vibe_cognition.cognition.prime injects a single global digest. On a multi-user team sharing one graph via git, every section amalgamates everyone's work; a returning user's own open tasks and recent episodes are pushed out by section caps.

GOAL: personalize to the current git user (server-resolved), their tasks and recent activity first; shared knowledge (constraints, workflows, high-severity incidents) stays global.

VERIFIED FOUNDATION: git_identity.resolve_git_identity(repo_path) is file-read-only (NO subprocess — settled v0.12.1 P0 constraint), returns {name, email}, never raises. Task nodes carry server-resolved metadata.created_by={name,email} (trustworthy). All other nodes: author is caller-provided free text (mix of human and agent names) — NOT reliable for filtering; open attribution-honesty task 73f750d8d528. PrimeConfig frozen dataclass + env knobs in config.py is the sanctioned toggle surface. Prime is journal-read-only and light
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Personalized session-start prime approved: server-stamped recorded_by/claimed_by provenance (WP-P13n-1) + per-git-user prime with email-only matching and multi-user auto-detect (WP-P13n-2)
data.detail: Colton approved the personalization scope 2026-07-07. Prime personalizes to the current git user when the graph has >1 distinct NON-EMPTY stamped email (auto-detect; solo output byte-identical; PRIME_PERSONALIZE=auto|on|off override). Personal sections: "Your Open Tasks" (created_by.email OR claimed_by.email match — email is the ONLY match key; owner/author display-only) + "Your recent activity" (episodes/decisions/discoveries, 5 each, recorded_by.email). Constraints/workflows/incidents/documents stay global by design. Foundation: _record_node stamps metadata.recorded_by={name,email}; claiming (status->in_progress) stamps metadata.claimed_by — both via resolve_git_identity file-reads (subprocess banned, v0.12.1). The _format_constraints supersession HEAD-filter fix (task 0d7e84d52537) folds into WP-P13n-2. REJECTED: semantic relevance-biasing of patterns/decisions (embeddings in the hook path violate the Wedge/Sidecar program); manual agent-name->human mapping file (rots); client-passed identity (reintroduces the trust gap server-resolution closed); per-user filtering of constraints/incidents (they bind everyone). Peer-reviewed (sonnet): empty-email solo-flip blocker fixed via non-empty-email counting; owner-name matching cut; superseded-constraints composition surfaced. Full brief: doc:aa047b0b0e0a.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: EPIC: Personalized session-start prime (per-git-user context)
data.detail: Umbrella for the approved personalization program. Two WPs: WP-P13n-1 (provenance stamps: recorded_by on all node writes, claimed_by on task claims), WP-P13n-2 (prime personalization + constraints HEAD-filter fold-in). Approved scope and acceptance criteria: doc:aa047b0b0e0a. Decision: bfb34715d609. Vorpid implements, Vince gates, Loki pins releases.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-P13n-1: server-stamped provenance — recorded_by on all node writes, claimed_by on task claims
data.detail: (a) _record_node stamps metadata.recorded_by={name,email} via resolve_git_identity (file-reads ONLY, subprocess banned per v0.12.1 P0); (b) cognition_update_task stamps metadata.claimed_by on status->in_progress (re-claim re-stamps; other transitions untouched). Acceptance: stamps present with specific values asserted in tests; identity failure degrades to {name: getpass-or-unknown, email: ""} never raises/blocks; old journals load unchanged; fix+proof same commit. KNOWN-INTENTIONAL: author stays free text; owner stays free-text display-only; created_by unchanged. Gate: whole-repo `uv run python -m pytest` + `uv run ruff check .`. Ships alone with version bump. Full brief: doc:aa047b0b0e0a.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-P13n-2: prime personalization — per-user tasks/activity sections, multi-user auto-detect, constraints HEAD-filter
data.detail: Identity resolve in prime.main(); auto-detect on distinct NON-EMPTY stamped emails (>1 = multi-user; empty-email guard is a peer-review blocker fix); Open Tasks split ("Your Open Tasks" via created_by.email OR claimed_by.email — email ONLY match key; then "Team critical" high/critical); NEW "Your recent activity" (your episodes/decisions/discoveries, 5 each, recorded_by.email); PRIME_PERSONALIZE=auto|on|off + cap knobs in config.py/PrimeConfig; FOLD IN _format_constraints supersession HEAD-filter (task 0d7e84d52537), mirroring _format_workflows. Acceptance: solo output byte-identical EXCEPT constraint dedup (assert both directions); multi-user fixture personal sections, strictly email-matched; unconfigured identity -> global, no crash; superseded constraint never renders, HEAD does; NO new heavy imports in prime.py; measured token size in PR. KNOWN-INTENTIONAL: v0.13.0 trim defaults settled; constraints/workflows/incidents/documents stay global. Gate: whole-repo `uv run python -m pytest` + `uv run ruff check .`. Depends on WP-P13n-1. Full brief: doc:aa047b0b0e0a.

data.reason: v0.15.3 wedge incident directly motivated the WP-Wedge design decision
Same-day post-fix wedge (probe ok 7.3s yet in-process load 1463s) led directly to the discovery that the probe premise was false
Discovery that the probe premise was false motivated the stronger WP-Wedge-2 design (INV-1/INV-2)
WP-Wedge-2 design motivated the full three-layer program ordering decision
Contextvar-propagation discovery is the cited evidence generalized into the mock-tests-cannot-catch-dispatch-defects pattern
Pseudo-handle rejection by WaitForMultipleObjects explains the mechanism behind the test doubles silently passing on WAIT_FAILED
WP-Wedge-2 and WP-Lifecycle are the next two phases of the same P0 program, per the program-ordering decision
Concurrent-load-stampede discovery is explicit direct evidence motivating WP-Sidecar's machine-wide load mutex design
Both articulate the self-sustaining orphan-churn mechanism, from evidence and strategic-response angles respectively

data.detail:
The WP-4 parity test test_reconciler_embed_matches_embed_entity_node_directly failed intermittently twice during the 2026-07-02 overnight burn-down full-suite runs, passing in isolation and on immediate re-run both times. First sighting was attributed to mid-edit tree state (gate run while implementer had files half-written) — plausible then. Second sighting (during WP-11, no embeddings/chromadb code in the diff) surfaced actual failure detail: a chromadb rust-backend InternalError — which weakens the mid-edit theory and suggests a real low-frequency flake in the chromadb persistence layer under rapid suite-wide create/write/delete of collections (Windows; pinned chromadb 1.5.5). Neither sighting reproduced under isolation or x3 re-runs; committed content double-green at pins throughout. Investigate: capture the full InternalError next occurrence (run suite with -ra or --lf loop), check if it correlates with test ordering/tmp-dir reuse/handle cleanup, consider a targeted retry marker (pytest-rerunfailures) for the chromadb-touching tests ONLY if root-causing stalls — do not blanket-retry the suite. Related prior art: wp-emb-e4 chromadb rust-lock discovery (SQLite lock contention class).

APPEND (2026-07-06, WP-Sidecar gate evidence): also fails as AttributeError "'RustBindingsAPI' object has no attribute 'bindings'" under concurrent PersistentClient opens — a DIFFERENT signature that EVADES _retry_chromadb_open (which retries on InternalError only). LOAD-dependent, now proven: test_chromadb_cross_process failed 3/3 in the gate worktree under an active model-load stampede AND identically at the base SHA (pre-existing), then passed on a calm box. Same AttributeError signature also surfaced once in test_e3_doc_prefix's reconciler-parity test during Vorpid's WP-Lifecycle fix-round run. Any retry fix must cover both signatures (InternalError AND the RustBindingsAPI AttributeError).

APPEND (2026-07-07, WP-P13n-1 gate): test_cross_process_chromadb_concurrent_open_and_write_no_collision failed once in the full-suite gate run at ca8c747 (gate-p13n1 worktree, fresh venv build immediately prior — plausible load contribution), passed on isolated re-run. Error signature not captured (only the FAILED summary line was retained). Diff under test touched no chromadb/embeddings code. data.summary: Rehydrate-reset data loss: shared-tree branch switch replaced the live journal.jsonl and silently dropped 6 post-flush records (recovered from the stash)
data.detail: Sequence (2026-07-07, WP-P13n-1 closeout): Vince flushed the journal to main (e48f3bc) at ~22:27, merged (0d952cd), then continued writing to the graph (task-done transition, ship episode beb52bec01e8 + 2 deterministic edges, flake discovery 0ec7186308b7, chroma-tracker detail append) — 6 journal records landing AFTER the flush copy. Vince then told Vorpid to switch the shared tree to main. Vorpid, correctly unable to checkout over the modified tracked journal.jsonl, stashed it ("local journal.jsonl side-effects") and switched. The checkout replaced the on-disk journal with main's flushed version; the MCP server rehydrate-reset to it; the 6 records vanished from disk AND the in-memory graph (463→461 nodes) — detected because the curation run failed with "node does not exist" on both new nodes. TWO compounding misreads: (1) Vince instructed the switch without re-flushing first, treating the merge-flush as covering everything when his own writes continued after it; (2) Vorpid assessed the stash as "just my own MCP-call noise, already reflected in your real flush" — wrong because journal.jsonl is ONE shared file receiving every session's server writes; a stash of it contains ALL sessions' unflushed records, not just the stasher's. RECOVERY: extracted stash@{0}:.cognition/journal.jsonl, line-set-diffed against the live journal (6 missing lines, original order preserved, node-adds before their edges), appended byte-safely (UTF-8 no BOM, trailing-newline verified), cognition_reload → 463/797 restored, all 4 logical writes verified present. STANDING RULE going forward: the journal flush must be the LAST journal event before any shared-tree branch switch — sequence is: all sessions stop graph writes → manager flushes+pushes → switch → resume; and any stash containing .cognition/journal.jsonl is NEVER dropped until a line-set diff against the live journal proves it contains nothing missing.
data.author: Vince

data.reason: Discovery explicitly identifies chromadb flake task as one of three tracked distinct flaky-test identities in suite reliability; task text cross-references this discovery back
Recurrence of the same failure class (shared-worktree branch-switch clobbering the live journal and dropping unflushed records), same root-cause area, ~2 weeks after prior incident

data.summary: Shared-worktree journal safety: shipped visibility + snapshot tooling did not prevent recurrence of the same branch-switch clobber failure mode
data.detail: Arc: (1) 2026-06-23, incident 5d63a548783d — a branch-switch in the shared tree rewrote the tracked journal.jsonl back to its last-flushed state, discarding 2 live-appended records from both disk and server memory; root cause identified as live appends made between flushes being unprotected by any branch operation. (2) 2026-06-25, fail 59416463f1e3 — a separate, unrelated failure mode in the same protocol area: a local-only journal flush was never pushed, so a GitHub PR merge landed on an origin/main missing 40 records, diverging code and journal; no data was lost (records survived in local commits + the live file), but it showed the flush protocol had a push-side gap too. In response, three mitigations shipped 2026-07-02: task 1bce5542be63 (surface rehydrate-reset events — WARNING log + get_status field + prime flag, so a session learns memory was lost instead of silently continuing), task 4350a42fc4e5 (wire the existing snapshot_journal helper into the manager-flush procedure as a shipped console script, closing a torn-tail-copy gap), and task 5c9978ed8d29 (ship a user-facing topology guide documenting the full shared-checkout protocol, sourced directly from these incidents). Despite all three landing, 2026-07-07 fail 483c21761868 recurred: the SAME failure mode as incident 5d63a548783d (a branch-switch replacing the live journal.jsonl and dropping unflushed records) happened again ~2 weeks later, this time losing 6 records instead of 2. The recovery this time used a git-stash line-set diff (rather than conversation-context re-recording) — an improvement enabled by the intervening tooling/protocol work — but the trigger was identical: a manager continued writing to the live journal after a flush checkpoint, then authorized a branch switch without re-flushing first, and the person doing the switch treated a stash of the modified journal as harmless local noise rather than a shared file needing a diff-before-drop check. LESSON: the shipped mitigations improved observability (immediate loss detection) and recovery quality (torn-tail-safe snapshots, and a working stash-recovery technique), but none of them PREVENT the root trigger. The protocol's own rule — flush must be the LAST journal event before any branch switch — is enforced by human discipline and reminder only, not by any technical guard. A recurrence despite documented protocol plus shipped visibility/recovery tooling is a signal that this specific gap (unflushed live appends surviving a branch switch) needs a mechanical guard — e.g., a pre-checkout check that refuses/warns when the live journal has appends not yet reachable from the flushed copy — rather than continuing to rely on protocol discipline alone.
data.author: curate-orchestrator

data.reason: first incident in the shared-worktree journal-safety recurrence arc

data.summary: Mechanical guard against branch-switch journal clobber: refuse/warn when the live journal has unflushed appends
data.detail: Second occurrence of the same failure mode (incident 5d63a548783d 2026-06-23, 2 records; fail 483c21761868 2026-07-07, 6 records): a branch switch in the shared tree replaces the tracked .cognition/journal.jsonl with the target branch's committed version, and the server's rehydrate-reset silently drops every record appended since the last flush. The 2026-07-02 mitigations (rehydrate-reset surfacing in get_status/prime, snapshot_journal console script, topology guide) improved detection and recovery but do not PREVENT the trigger — the "flush must be the last journal event before any switch" rule is enforced only by discipline, and it failed again ~2 weeks later despite documentation. Synthesis + full arc: pattern d16558284745. Candidate designs (evaluate, don't pre-commit): (a) a git pre-checkout guard — hook or wrapper — that diffs the live journal against the HEAD-committed copy and refuses/warns when live-only appends exist; (b) server-side: on rehydrate, detect that the incoming journal is a strict PREFIX of the in-memory record stream and auto-splice the lost suffix back (self-healing, mirrors the manual line-set-diff recovery used both times) instead of resetting; (c) both. Acceptance: a branch switch over a journal with unflushed appends can no longer silently lose records on a default setup — either the switch is blocked with a clear message or the records survive the rehydrate. Must not break the normal flush-then-merge flow or single-user single-session use.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.reason: Pattern synthesis explicitly identified the gap (mechanical guard needed against branch-switch journal clobber) and directly motivated this task's creation and design

data.summary: Conflicting-decision warning on cognition_search results: flag when a teammate's existing decision contradicts what the caller is about to act on
data.detail: When a cognition_search (or session-start prime) surfaces material relevant to what an agent is doing, warn if another teammate already recorded a conflicting decision on the same topic — so the agent doesn't unknowingly relitigate or contradict a standing decision. Likely builds on contradicts/supersedes edges plus recorded_by provenance (WP-P13n-1). Acceptance: a search whose results include a decision that conflicts with another surfaced node (or the query intent) carries an explicit warning naming the decision, its author, and the conflict.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Weight human seniority in ranking/trust of nodes related to that person
data.detail: Nodes recorded by (or about) a more senior human should carry more weight — e.g. in search ranking, conflict resolution between contradicting decisions, and session-start prime selection. Needs a way to express seniority per person (config or graph metadata on the person/identity), then apply it wherever provenance already flows (recorded_by/claimed_by from WP-P13n-1). Open design question: seniority as global rank vs per-area authority.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Option to filter out nodes from certain people (per-author exclusion in search/prime)
data.detail: Let a user/agent exclude nodes recorded by specific people from cognition_search results and the session-start prime — e.g. mute a noisy author or a departed teammate. Builds on recorded_by provenance (WP-P13n-1). Design questions: filter as per-call parameter vs per-user persistent preference; whether excluded-author nodes should still surface when they are the only source of a constraint/incident (safety-relevant nodes probably shouldn't be muteable silently).
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Team-cognition scoping survey completed: epic cedf4a8457e9 created, 3 tasks re-parented, tool surface surveyed, 12 suggestions dual-POV validated, docs/260714-team-cognition-survey.md written
data.detail: Colton asked for a survey-only scoping pass on making vibe-cognition useful for multi-tiered teams (humans + manager/subordinate agents sharing one graph with hierarchy and cohesion). Executed: (1) created EPIC team-cognition (cedf4a8457e9) and re-parented tasks 888a21f729dd (conflict warning), f746c5f9361e (seniority weighting), 5222c93ca8f5 (per-author filter) under it. (2) Explore subagent (haiku) cataloged the identity/provenance/team surface — key facts: server-stamped recorded_by/created_by/claimed_by vs display-only author/owner; email the only match key; zero code-level roles/permissions/conflict-detection; curation containment docs-only; graph had 817 edges with only 1 contradicts. (3) Drafted 12 suggestions (S1 person-node roster, S2 seniority annotation, S3 recorded_via stamp, S4 read-time conflict annotation, S5 possible_conflict_with, S6 curation conflict lens, S7 per-call author filters, S8 persistent mute, S9 containment observability, S10 claim-collision warning, S11 role-aware prime, S12 since-you-were-gone digest), grounded in WP-P13n rejected alternatives. (4) Two sonnet subagent validity reviews (human-team-lead POV, LLM-consumer POV). Unanimous outcomes: S8 KILLED (durable invisible muting = rejected pattern reborn); S2 silent re-ranking KILLED (annotation only); S9 token-handshake dropped (minimal observability only); S1 must be person nodes, not a config file; S4 must ship paired with S6 or it trains false confidence. One real disagreement kept visible: S7 (human: valid as-is; LLM: misuse affordance — requires excluded-count disclosure, maybe agent-kind-only targets). Reviewers surfaced 4 missed gaps: M1 severity-gated push for constraints/incidents, M2 server-stamped assignment (assigned_by/assigned_to), M3 constraint acknowledgment, M4 search result-completeness disclosure (returned N of M). Recommended v1 slice: S6+S4 conflict pipeline, S10, S3, then S5. Deliverable: docs/260714-team-cognition-survey.md with 5 open questions for Colton (S7 restriction, role ownership vs teammate-comms, seniority model shape, re-parent 73f750d8d528, whether to file M1-M4). No implementation, no code changes, nothing committed.
data.author: Vince

data.reason: WP-P13n provenance decision (recorded_by/claimed_by) is the explicit foundation the team-cognition epic builds directly on
Episode narrates the scoping survey session that created and organized this epic

data.summary: Agent names never persist in the cognition graph: nodes carry the human identity stamp + an agent-origin bool; human-origin input always outweighs agent-origin (weighting, never erasure)
data.detail: Colton's ruling on the team-cognition survey's S3. REJECTED: the surveyed recorded_via agent-name stamp — agent identities do not belong in the graph at all. CHOSEN: every node keeps the human identity stamped by default (recorded_by, as today) plus a boolean flag saying whether the record came via an agent or directly from the human. That bool IS the weighting input: human decisions weigh more than agent decisions in retrieval/conflict contexts; agent decisions are never erased or hidden, human input just always carries more weight. Design notes carried into scoping: the bool is necessarily client-declared (the server cannot verify agent-vs-human origin; Colton explicitly accepts trust-based declaration), same trust class as author; the default value when undeclared and the exact declaration mechanism (param vs env) are open design questions for the WP brief. Side effect: this resolves the survey's role-ownership hazard cleanly — the cognition graph models HUMANS only; agent identity/roles stay entirely in teammate-comms.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Human identity nodes approved for the graph (name, role, seniority, reports-to), updated IN PLACE; seniority IS weighed in retrieval — weighted, never wiped, part of the story
data.detail: Colton's ruling on the team-cognition survey's S1/S2, overriding the dual-POV synthesis's annotation-only position. Person/human-identity nodes go in the graph, following a pattern similar to teammate-comms profiles (name, role, seniority, etc.) while remaining totally separate from teammate-comms. They must be updatable IN PLACE to reflect a person's current role — NOT supersession-versioned (rejects the survey's supersession-chain shape for people). Seniority SHOULD be weighed when retrieving results — not annotation-only. Crucial nuance in the rationale: weighting must never wipe out a junior's findings; results are weighed by the seniority of the humans driving the discoveries/changes because hierarchy is itself part of how the project's cognitive history was shaped. Example arc Colton gave: a junior finds a better solution than the senior's earlier one; later the senior revises it again — the seniority context makes that revision reflect powerfully on WHY it changed (the senior had the understanding and standing to change it). Combined with conflict edges, this paints a fuller picture without stomping anything — BETTER context is the whole goal. Scoping consequence: the LLM-POV review's silent-reranking objection is answered by a visibility requirement, not by dropping weighting — weights/seniority must be shown in results, never applied invisibly.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: New-user onboarding: vibe-cognition auto-detects a first-time user and prompts them (via Claude Code) to introduce themselves — name, role, seniority, and who they directly report to (trust-based)
data.detail: Colton's ruling extending the team-cognition scoping. When a user first interacts with vibe-cognition (a git identity/email not yet known to the graph's person registry), the system must detect that and prompt the user to tell Claude Code — and thereby vibe-cognition — who they are: name, role, seniority, and their direct manager (reports-to). We trust the user to be truthful (explicitly accepted; local trust domain, no verification). The introduction creates their human identity node and its reports-to relationship, which is what makes seniority weighting and any hierarchy-aware views possible. Open scoping questions carried forward: detection point (session-start prime is light-import and cannot prompt — likely surfaced as a prime notice instructing the agent to run the onboarding conversation, or a dedicated tool the agent calls), and how re-prompting is avoided once a person node exists.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.detail: APPROVED DIRECTION (Colton, 2026-07-14): seniority IS weighed in retrieval — not annotation-only (overrides the survey reviews' annotation-only synthesis). Weighting is based on the seniority of the humans driving the nodes, sourced from human identity nodes (see person-node task under epic cedf4a8457e9). Hard requirements: weighting never wipes out or hides lower-seniority findings (weighted, never wiped); weights must be VISIBLE in results, never silently applied (answers the LLM-POV silent-reranking objection); constraints/incidents are never outranked by seniority. Rationale: hierarchy is part of the project's cognitive story — e.g. junior finds a better solution, senior later revises it; seniority context explains why things changed. Composes with the agent-origin bool ruling: human input always outweighs agent input as a second weighting axis.

data.summary: Server-stamped task assignment: assigned_to + assigned_by with provenance (manager assigns, subordinate can verify it came from the manager)
data.detail: Approved by Colton (2026-07-14) from the team-cognition survey's reviewer-found gaps (M2). Today claiming stamps claimed_by (self-claim) but assignment is only free-text owner — no way to verify an assignment actually came from the manager. Add server-stamped assignment: when a task is assigned, stamp metadata.assigned_to (target) and metadata.assigned_by (server-resolved git identity of the assigner) with a transition-log-style audit trail. Design questions for the WP brief: assignment target shape (email, matching the established key); interaction with claimed_by (assignment ≠ claim; claiming an assigned task should be the normal accept path); whether "Your Open Tasks" in the prime matches assigned_to.email too (likely yes); collision semantics with S10 claim-collision warnings.

data.summary: Dashboard redesign direction: PM-style views become primary (Overview/Board/Workflows/Documents/Activity + shared detail drawer); constellation demoted to a lazy tab; read-only v1
data.detail: Colton's ask (2026-07-14): make the dashboard feel like sprint-management software, meaningful for task/workflow/document nodes; constellation needn't survive. CHOSEN: nav-rail SPA with PM views primary; constellation kept only as a lazy-loaded tab (already built and vendored — zero keep-cost, still useful for curation debugging; its navigation job moves to the detail drawer's related-nodes-by-edge-type list); read-only v1 because the dashboard has no per-viewer identity — writes would stamp misleading server-machine provenance, the exact class of problem WP-P13n fixed (existing delete precedent: removed_by="dashboard"); vanilla JS retained (views are lists/columns/tables — no framework migration). REJECTED: keeping the graph-first UI (organizes the UI around the graph-as-picture while the newest value is structured task/workflow/document data the picture can't show; no recorded decision mandates graph-first — checked); deleting the constellation outright in v1 (buys nothing, forecloses cheap utility); dashboard task-status writes in v1 (identity problem above). Trust-class rendering rule from second-opinion review: every person chip is backed by server-resolved identity; pre-P13n nodes fall back to free-text author and must render visibly "unverified" — never identical to a verified chip. Design doc docs/260714-dashboard-redesign.md + mockup docs/dashboard-redesign-mockup.html; task filed under epic cedf4a8457e9.

data.summary: Team-cognition rulings round 2 (Colton): author-filter is an optional user-invoked param defaulting to NONE (LLM never adds it unprompted); push notifications will never be a thing; stamped assignment approved; attribution-honesty task stays out of the epic
data.detail: Colton's rulings closing the team-cognition survey's remaining open questions (2026-07-14). (1) Per-author filter (task 5222c93ca8f5): stays an OPTIONAL parameter on read tools that the USER can ask the LLM to include; default is none/off so the LLM doesn't accidentally add it on its own initiative. Settles the survey's S7 disagreement between the human-POV (any author, disclosed) and LLM-POV (agent-kind only) positions with a third answer: no target restriction, but invocation is human-directed — the tool docstring must say the agent applies it only on explicit user request, and the excluded-count disclosure from the review still applies. (2) Push notifications (survey gap M1): REJECTED permanently — "won't ever be a thing." Do not re-propose. (3) Server-stamped assignment (survey gap M2): APPROVED — filed as task under the epic. (4) Attribution-honesty task 73f750d8d528: stays OUT of the team-cognition epic (not re-parented); the agent-origin bool decision 6be2e867f91e substantively addresses its going-forward concern anyway. Survey gaps M3 (constraint acks) and M4 (search completeness disclosure) were not ruled on and remain open items in the battle plan.

data.detail: APPROVED DIRECTION (Colton, 2026-07-14): per-call optional filter parameter on read tools (cognition_search, cognition_list_tasks) matching recorded_by/created_by email. DEFAULT IS NONE — the LLM must never add the filter on its own initiative; it is applied only when the user explicitly asks for it (tool docstring must state this). Constraints/incidents are never filtered out. Persistent/env-var muting (survey S8) is permanently rejected.

data.summary: Team-cognition rulings round 3 (Colton): onboarding is triggered from the prime; agent-origin bool defaults to TRUE (via-agent) when undeclared
data.detail: Colton's rulings on the battle plan's open items (2026-07-14). (1) TC7 onboarding trigger: driven from the session-start prime — when the prime finds no person node for the current git identity's email, it injects a notice instructing the agent to run the introduce-yourself conversation (name, role, seniority, direct manager) and record the person node. This fits the prime's light-import constraint (a notice, not an interactive prompt, journal-scan detection only). Sub-questions for the WP brief: whether a solo/fresh project also prompts (prime-based detection implies yes — any email lacking a person node; recommend yes with a decline/snooze path so it never nags), and no-re-prompt once the person node exists. (2) TC6 agent-origin default: TRUE ("via agent") when a session doesn't declare origin — Colton confirmed the recommendation. Rationale: every record physically comes through an agent; direct human dictation is the marked case, so unlabeled records honestly default to agent-origin and human-origin is the deliberate assertion (which also means the human>agent weighting can never be accidentally inflated by an undeclared default). Still open from battle plan §5: M3 (constraint acks) and M4 (search completeness disclosure) — Colton asked for plain-English explanations before ruling; overall plan approval also pending.

data.summary: WP brief: Identity Node Layer (WP-TC5 person nodes + WP-TC6 agent-origin bool) — approved scope
data.detail:
Scope: WP-TC5 + WP-TC6 — Identity Node Layer. Status: APPROVED (peer-reviewed, 9 changes incorporated). Vorpid implements; Vince gates. One release batch. Epic cedf4a8457e9; task 1c29cff92e20.

FINALIZED RULINGS (Colton): person nodes model HUMANS ONLY (agents stay in teammate-comms); in-place updates with audit trail (not supersession); email is the identity key; agent-origin bool on every write, client-declared, DEFAULT TRUE (via agent), human-origin is the marked assertion; weighting is TC9 (data only here); onboarding is TC7 (tools here must suffice for it).

WP-TC5 DESIGN: New CognitionNodeType.PERSON. Full node-type survival touchpoints: models.py enum; storage/journal replay; PERSON added to _INERT_TYPES (deterministic-edge matcher would otherwise treat it as entity — task/workflow precedent, test in test_deterministic_edges.py); _embed_entity_node wiring; dashboard TYPE_COLORS; cognition_record NODE TYPES docstring (person NOT creatable there — dedicated tools only, like document); hardcoded node_type lists in search/get_history/get_edgeless_nodes docstrings gain "person"; readme.py COGNITION_GUIDE (verified safe — on-demand tool only, not session-injected); SKILL.md + README (test_doc_drift force-fails until updated); SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS explicitly NOT touched (TC7 territory).

Shape: summary "Name — role" (regenerated on update when name/role change); metadata.person = {email (casefolded), name, role, seniority, reports_to_email ("" = top)}; seniority PINNED closed set ordered "owner" > "senior" > "mid" > "junior", module-level constant for TC9 to import; metadata.profile_history append-only [{changed: {field: {from,to}}, at, by(server-resolved)}]; recorded_by as usual. Invariant: one person node per email — re-register returns existing node with "already_registered" signal.

Tools: cognition_register_person(name, role, seniority, reports_to_email?, email?, detail?) — email omitted = server resolves current git identity (self-registration, impersonation-re
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-TC5/TC6 identity-layer brief approved (peer-reviewed, 9 engineering changes) and handed to Vorpid — first WP of the autonomous team-cognition epic run
data.detail: Colton went unavailable after directing: execute the entire team-cognition epic autonomously ("you are on your own with your memory to lean on"), curate at lower cadence (batch at wave boundaries), identity layer first. Operating structure confirmed: Vorpid is live (idle, awaiting assignment) so the durable role split holds — Vince briefs/gates/releases, Vorpid implements; Loki pinged per release batch; human-gated items (hands-on install checks) queue for Colton's return — this WP is not install-mechanics so none expected. The TC5+TC6 brief (doc:7cda55bb788b) was peer-reviewed by a sonnet subagent that verified claims against the codebase; 9 changes incorporated, notably: PERSON must join _INERT_TYPES (matcher would default it to entity); from_agent must also stamp the new person tools (else person nodes are born legacy); readme.py COGNITION_GUIDE is a required touchpoint (verified not session-injected); SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS explicitly untouched (behavioral change = TC7 territory); cross-version risk traced precisely — old-version journal replay and live-search catch-up are safe but old-version startup embedding sync aborts unguarded at the first person node and silently skips everything behind it, recurring until upgrade (rollout note required; our own sync gets a per-node tolerance regression test); reports_to cycle guard is an email-keyed metadata chain walk with graceful dangling termination, NOT the task-parent edge walk; seniority vocabulary pinned owner>senior>mid>junior as an importable constant.

data.summary: Gate C: stability audits — C1 after Wave 1 (identity: journal compat, re-embed cost, convergence), C2 after Wave 3 (weighting latency, multi-user prime size)
data.detail: Epic audit gate (Colton's explicit requirement). C1 after Wave 1: old journals load with new metadata (additive-only), cross-version reader tolerance, re-embed cost of person/metadata writes, onboarding-path behavior, multi-process convergence unaffected, chromadb flake watch. C2 after Wave 3: search latency with weighting enabled, multi-user prime size/latency. Authorship split: any measurement harness is implementer work (Vorpid) under a Vince brief; Vince gates results. REMEDIATION CLAUSE: a Gate C failure blocks the next wave — becomes a fix WP (or an explicitly recorded accepted-risk decision) before new feature WPs ship. Battle plan: docs/260714-team-cognition-battle-plan.md.

data.summary: Gate B-final: fresh-eyes tool-surface coherence pass — an LLM given ONLY server instructions + tool docstrings must correctly explain team usage of the plugin
data.detail: Epic audit gate (Colton's explicit requirement). Beyond the standing per-WP self-sufficiency workflow (67751ebc39bd, runs at every tool add/change including return-shape changes), the epic closes with one holistic pass: a fresh-eyes LLM subagent, given ONLY the pushed SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS and tool docstrings (no repo docs, no CLAUDE.md, no conversation history), must correctly explain how the plugin is used on a multi-tier team — person registration, from_agent semantics, conflict warnings, claim etiquette, filters and their disclosures. Failures become doc-fix WPs. Includes SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS / SKILL.md / README drift check. Battle plan: docs/260714-team-cognition-battle-plan.md.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Gate D: dual-persona consumer audit on an ISOLATED test graph — 5 scenarios must pass before the epic closes
data.detail: Epic-close gate (Colton's explicit requirement: "final consumer auditing to see if your changes will actually serve the goal of this epic"). Subagents simulate BOTH personas (human team lead; LLM agent) executing the epic's core scenarios against an ISOLATED THROWAWAY test graph — NEVER the production graph (scripted multi-user conflict/claim exercises on the live shared journal are exactly the corruption class topology-guide.md documents). Scenarios: (1) junior/agent attempts to relitigate a settled decision → warned; (2) conflicting decision recorded → surfaced at write AND read; (3) claim collision → visible; (4) seniority story arc (junior's better find, senior's later revision) → retrievable as narrative with visible weights; (5) fresh user onboarded → person node + personalized prime. Pass/fail per scenario; failures become tasks; the epic does not close until all five pass. Battle plan: docs/260714-team-cognition-battle-plan.md.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP brief: Dashboard V1 (WP-TC11 — nav rail, Overview, Board, detail drawer, /api/tasks + /api/overview) — approved scope, queued behind WP-TC5/6
data.detail:
Scope: WP-TC11 — Dashboard V1 (PM core). Status: APPROVED (peer-reviewed, 6 corrections). Queued: hands to Vorpid when WP-TC5/6 gates. Design source of truth: docs/260714-dashboard-redesign.md + mockup (layout only — doc spec wins). Direction Colton-approved (decision 7f6f6bf22ca9). Task 30fabf12c81b; epic cedf4a8457e9.

IN: nav rail replacing 3-column layout; Overview; Board; shared detail drawer; GET /api/tasks; GET /api/overview (REQUIRED); constellation demoted to lazy Graph tab; header keeps search/banner/refresh/stats.
OUT: V2 (Workflows/Documents/Activity), V3 (person-NODE lookups/People view, seniority badges, live conflict banners — SLOTS only), any new write path (read-only v1), framework migration, CDN assets.

KEY ENGINEERING POINTS (peer-review verified):
- /api/tasks row: {id, summary, status, priority, owner, parent_id, depth, created_by, claimed_by, timestamp, claimed_at, last_transition_at, transitions_count}. claimed_by/transition fields are NOT in _list_tasks's shape — implement the dashboard handler independently against storage.get_nodes_by_type(TASK); do NOT extend the MCP tool contract (no MCP surface in this WP). claimed_at = at of latest →in_progress transition; last_transition_at = transitions[-1].at; node top-level timestamp is creation-only. Claim age + done-7-day-cap derive from these.
- /api/overview REQUIRED: server-computed aggregate — task counts by status, done-this-week, active constraints (severity≠low, HEAD-filtered via the one-line storage predicate `not storage.get_predecessors(node_id, SUPERSEDES)` — do NOT import prime.py: private markdown funcs + CLI deps), needs-attention (stale claims >5d via claimed_at; blocked), recent episodes (5), recent incidents (high+ 14d).
- /api/node/{id} passes metadata through already — NO backend change; drawer renders provenance block (recorded_by/created_by/claimed_by/author with trust-class labeling: server-resolved = normal identity chip; author-only pre-P13n fallback = dashed "unverified")

data.summary: Invariant guards added on the update path but forgotten on the create path — check EVERY entry point that accepts the field, especially when deferred/dangling references are legal
data.detail: WP-TC5 gate finding: reports_to cycle rejection existed only in _update_person; _register_person only checked self-reporting. Because dangling reports_to is legal (manager may register later), a cycle could be closed AT REGISTRATION: B registers with reports_to pointing at unregistered a@x, then A registers reporting to B — no guard fires. General shape: when a validity invariant depends on other records and the schema legally permits forward references, creation is just as capable of completing a violation as mutation; test suites naturally cluster invariant tests on the update path because that is where the guard was first written. Gate reviews should enumerate every write path that accepts the constrained field.

data.detail:
# Scope: WP-TC7 — Prime-Triggered New-User Onboarding

Status: APPROVED (peer-reviewed 2026-07-15, 4 corrections: empty-graph non-co-occurrence; git_hygiene version bump for gitignore entry; notice position pinned; TC5 byte-identity test RETAINED not replaced). QUEUED — hands to Vorpid when WP-TC11 gates. Epic cedf4a8457e9; task 62d8264bdb1d. Depends WP-TC5 (shipped v0.19.0). NO tool/schema changes.

## RULINGS: Trigger FROM THE PRIME (decision 8f3612ad9583); trust the human's answers (name, role, seniority, direct manager). Sub-question delegated to brief: solo decline/snooze path.

## DESIGN

Detection in generate_prime (light-import safe; current_email already resolved+casefolded at prime.py:395; person nodes via storage.get_nodes_by_type(PERSON), email at metadata.person.email). Notice fires when ALL: (1) current_email non-empty; (2) no person node matches; (3) email not in decline file; (4) prime_onboard knob on. Fires for solo AND multi-user graphs.

Empty-graph interaction: notice and ONBOARDING_BLOCK are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE by construction — main() (prime.py:521-529) appends the block on the empty branch and only calls generate_prime on else. Person notice first fires the session AFTER the first node lands. Do not add detection to main()'s empty branch.

Notice position pinned: FIRST section of generate_prime's output, before Active Constraints. Full payload order: migration note → rehydrate warning → [notice → constraints → …].

Decline file: .cognition/onboard-declined (via storage.cognition_dir, storage.py:126-129), one casefolded email per line, per-machine, written by the AGENT when the human declines (no new MCP tool, no graph write). Snooze == decline (remove line to be re-asked). Gitignore entry MUST land via git_hygiene.py::_write_gitignore + GIT_HYGIENE_VERSION bump 2→3 (versioned-writer precedent) — hand-editing this repo's .cognition/.gitignore is insufficient for downstream projects.

Notice content contract: tell human graph doesn't know them; a

data.summary: TC7 decline/snooze path: per-machine git-ignored file .cognition/onboard-declined (agent-written, prime-read), entry shipped via git_hygiene versioned writer — not a graph write, not a new tool, not a solo carve-out
data.detail: Resolves the sub-question Colton delegated to the TC7 brief. Chosen: plain per-machine file, one casefolded email per line; the prime notice instructs the agent to append on decline; prime skips listed emails; snooze==decline (delete the line to be re-asked). Gitignore coverage must go through git_hygiene.py's versioned _write_gitignore with GIT_HYGIENE_VERSION 2→3 so downstream projects that already ran the hygiene pass re-fire (peer-review finding — hand-editing one repo's .cognition/.gitignore leaves every other project tracking the file). Rejected: multi-user-only firing (ruling says new USER — solo users seed the roster); decline recorded in the graph (a decline is personal preference, not team knowledge, and would sync to everyone); a new MCP decline tool (widens Gate B surface for a file append the agent can do directly); SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS prose (per-session token cost for a one-shot flow); timed re-ask (needs richer per-user state; revisit with TC14's marker). Also pinned: notice is generate_prime's FIRST section; notice and empty-graph ONBOARDING_BLOCK are mutually exclusive by main()'s branch structure (notice first fires the session after the first node lands); onboarding registration uses from_agent=false (human-dictated by construction).

data.summary: WP-TC8 brief: stamped task assignment (APPROVED, peer-reviewed)
data.detail:
# Scope: WP-TC8 — Stamped Task Assignment (assigned_to / assignment audit)

Status: APPROVED (peer-reviewed 2026-07-15, 2 blocking corrections: no-op guard modeled on _update_person NOT the adjacent owner block; creation-time blank assigned_to_email == not-provided). QUEUED — hands to Vorpid after TC7. Epic cedf4a8457e9; task fc2c8b522ed8. Ruling: "stamped assignment is worth keeping" (Colton).

## PROBLEM
Tasks know creator and claimant (server-stamped) but there is no directed "this is YOURS" — a manager cannot assign work such that the assignee's next session surfaces it. owner is free text (often an agent), deliberately never identity-matched (prime.py:118). Assignment must be email-keyed, stamped, auditable.

## DESIGN
Field shape (metadata, additive): metadata.assigned_to = casefolded email string or absent (unassigned); client-declared, trust-based, dangling legal (assignee need not be a registered person — mirrors reports_to). metadata.assignments = append-only audit [{to, by, at}] — to = casefolded target ("" for explicit unassign), by = SERVER-RESOLVED git identity (unforgeable, same mechanism as transitions/profile_history), at = timestamp. assigned_by is the by of the LAST entry — no standalone field (would drift).

Tools (no new tools; Gate B self-sufficiency audit triggers):
- cognition_add_task: assigned_to_email param — non-blank seeds field + first audit entry. Creation-time blank/whitespace == NOT PROVIDED: seed nothing (never store "" — do NOT copy owner's creation path which stores raw).
- cognition_update_task: assigned_to_email param — set/reassign; "" unassigns; omit = no change. Each effective change appends exactly one entry; same-email no-op appends NONE. No-op guard modeled on _update_person's compare-before-append reports_to handling (cognition_tools.py:1810), NOT the adjacent owner block (which unconditionally sets metadata_changed, cognition_tools.py:1521-1523 — wrong template).
- Docstrings: owner = free-text display, may be an agent,

data.detail:
# Scope: Gate C1 — Wave 1 Stability Audit (identity layer)

Status: APPROVED (peer-reviewed 2026-07-15, 5 precision corrections). Runs AFTER WP-TC8 gates (Wave 1 complete). Vorpid builds the Part B harness under this brief; Vince runs the checklist, gates results, records the C1 report. REMEDIATION CLAUSE: C1 failure blocks Wave 2 — fix WP or explicit accepted-risk record before TC1 starts. Epic cedf4a8457e9; task 85b2b4de8aee.

## Part A — Vince-run checklist
A1. Journal compat on real data: replay THIS repo's production journal at Wave-1-final commit, zero replay errors. Invariant = distinct-SET arithmetic (expected_nodes = add_node ids − remove_node ids; expected_edges = add_edge triples − removed triples; compare get_statistics()) — NOT raw line counts (add_edge lines aren't deduped at tool layer while graph edge-add is idempotent by triple). Vince runs the harness's --journal-dir mode (manager runs a script, writes none).
A2. Additive-only metadata review: desk check from the four gate diffs that every Wave 1 key is NEW (person, profile_history, from_agent, assigned_to, assignments), no existing key changed shape. Record with commit refs.
A3. Cross-version story: v0.19.0 upgrade-promptly note shipped; old replay stores unknown metadata inertly — code-verified (storage.py:1150 raw metadata pass, 1206-1213 blind key assign; replay never constructs the Pydantic model). Cite in report.
A4. Flake review: suite ran ≥6 full times across Wave 1 gates; report any nonreproducible failure (count so far: zero).
A5. Onboarding end-to-end manual pass in a scratch repo: unconfigured email → no notice; unregistered → notice; register per notice → next prime silent + person node correct (from_agent=false, empty profile_history, recorded_by stamped); decline → silent + file gitignored (hygiene v3 re-fire observed on pre-v3 scratch project).

## Part B — Vorpid-built harness: scripts/bench_wave1.py
Stdlib + project imports; NOT packaged (outside wheel packages list, pyproject.tom

data.summary: storage.remove_node() cascades incident-edge removal via networkx WITHOUT journaling per-edge remove_edge entries — journal-arithmetic tooling must exclude edges touching removed node ids or it false-mismatches
data.detail: Found by Vorpid building the C1 harness's A1 set-arithmetic mode: naive added_edges − removed_edges arithmetic false-mismatched against any journal where a removed node had incident edges, because the cascade happens inside networkx at replay/live time and only the remove_node line is journaled. Fix pattern (implemented in scripts/bench_wave1.py): filter expected-edge triples to exclude any touching a removed node id. Companion Windows finding from the same WP: ChromaDBStorage.close() (embeddings/storage.py:394) must be called explicitly to release sqlite/HNSW file handles before a tempdir containing a chromadb persist dir can be rmtree'd — bare object deletion is insufficient on Windows (PermissionError).
data.author: Vince (manager, relaying Vorpid's findings)

data.summary: WP-TC10 brief: per-person search filter + returned-N-of-M completeness (APPROVED, peer-reviewed)
data.detail:
# Scope: WP-TC10 — Per-Person Search Filter (user-invoked) + "Returned N of M" Completeness

Status: APPROVED (peer-reviewed 2026-07-15, 7 corrections). Vorpid implements; Vince gates. Epic cedf4a8457e9; task 5222c93ca8f5. Rulings: filter optional param, default none, USER-INVOKED ONLY per docstring; M4 N-of-M approved. Zero Wave 2 dependencies.

## DESIGN — filter
cognition_search gains exclude_people: str|None (comma-separated emails, casefolded). Docstring guard: "Include ONLY when the human explicitly asked to filter people out this session; never volunteer it." Matching: server-resolved metadata.recorded_by.email (non-task) / created_by.email (task) — prime.py:137-159 _node_email doctrine; free-text author NEVER matched; unstamped pre-P13n nodes NEVER excluded (unverifiable ≠ excludable).
Where: _format_search_results — requires ADDING an unconditional storage.get_node per hit (N1 ghost filter is membership-only, storage.py:144-153; full fetch exists today only for document hits). Exclusion before limit fill so adaptive widening refills. excluded_count: NEVER accumulate across widening rounds — adaptive_vector_search re-runs dedupe from scratch each round and discards prior output (embeddings/storage.py:403-422); take only the terminating round's count.
Disclosure: excluded_count (distinct nodes) + excluded_for (casefolded list) present iff the param was passed and something was excluded; silent exclusion forbidden.
Scope: cognition_search only (single + multi-project uniformly).

## DESIGN — N of M (M4)
cognition_search gains total_found (distinct live nodes discovered before stopping) + exhaustive (true = Chroma exhausted, M exact; false = stopped at limit/cap, M is a floor). Implementation: changes adaptive_vector_search's dedupe contract — remove _format_search_results' early break at limit (cognition_tools.py:593-594; bounded by _SEARCH_OVERQUERY_CAP=500), return list+count; exhaustive from terminating round's len(results)<n. THREE call sites: MCP path + d

Peer review verified the brief against CODE, not against the ruling record, so it couldn't catch scope-vs-ruling drift. Fixed same-hour via a binding addendum message + brief edit before implementation started. Standing lesson for brief-writing: ALWAYS re-read the task node's detail field (and any cited decisions) immediately before drafting a WP brief — the battle plan is an index, not the ruling record; and consider adding "verify against the task node's ruling text" to the peer-review prompt template.
