data.summary: WP-TC1: curation conflict lens — dedicated contradiction/supersession-hunting pass in the curate pipeline (agent-file change; precision-gated before ship)
data.detail: Battle-plan Wave 2 opener. Today the edge-analyzer treats contradicts as "genuinely rare" with no hunting criteria — 2 contradicts edges exist in a 900-edge graph, so the read-time warning surface (TC2, task 888a21f729dd) has almost nothing to render. TC1 adds a dedicated conflict-hunting pass to the background curation pipeline: for stance-bearing node types (decision/constraint/pattern), actively search same-topic nodes and propose contradicts (incompatible stances, same subject) or supersedes (same lineage, stance evolved) edges, propose-only, orchestrator reviews at a hardened bar. RULED ACCEPTANCE (battle plan): precision measured on a labeled sample BEFORE ship — false contradicts edges poison a trusted signal. TC2 ships only after TC1 produces real edges.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: WP-TC1 brief: curation conflict lens (APPROVED, peer-reviewed, precision-gated)
data.detail:
# Scope: WP-TC1 — Curation Conflict Lens (contradiction/supersession hunting, precision-gated)

Status: APPROVED (peer-reviewed 2026-07-15, 4 corrections — two HIGH: candidate-list timing; eval-runner split). Vorpid implements; Vince gates AND authors the labeled eval set (labeler independence). Epic cedf4a8457e9; task ad3a9c794814; feeds TC2 (888a21f729dd — ships only after TC1 produces real edges). Ruled: precision measured on a labeled sample BEFORE ship.

## PROBLEM
2 contradicts edges in ~900 — the edge-analyzer calls contradicts "genuinely rare" with no hunting criteria (curate-edge-analyzer.md:34). Signal consumers (dashboard banners, TC2) are built; the producer is missing.

## DESIGN
NEW dedicated analyzer agents/curate-conflict-analyzer.md (haiku pin, read-only tools: get_node/get_neighbors/search/get_history), propose-only, spawned by the orchestrator as `vibe-cognition:curate-conflict-analyzer`. NOT extra prose in the edge-analyzer (different retrieval pattern; attributable precision).

Candidate set (timing LOAD-BEARING): stance-bearing nodes (decision/constraint/pattern/assumption) captured from the Step-1 cognition_get_uncurated_nodes worklist and passed explicitly — NEVER re-fetched at the pass's insertion point: Step 2 marks batches curated as it goes (curate-orchestrator.md:61), so a post-Step-2 uncurated fetch returns EMPTY (silent zero-edge failure mode).

Lens criteria: contradicts ONLY for incompatible stances on the same subject, both HEAD; supersedes instead for same-lineage evolution (esp. same recorded_by); never for scope differences/refinement/topic overlap/already-connected pairs; every proposal carries reason + verbatim stance quotes from BOTH nodes; output = established JSON contract with source "curate-conflict" (distinct provenance for TC15 + post-ship tracking).

Orchestrator: new step between edge curation and clustering; explicit model override at spawn (fail f09e770da046); review hardening: quotes must oppose; HEAD both endpoints
data.author: Vince (manager, for Colton)

transitions.note: WP-TC1 brief approved (peer-reviewed, 4 corrections incl. 2 HIGH silent-failure modes) stored as doc:83c250e463ae (node 57eba5997b87). Queued to Vorpid behind TC10. Vince authoring the labeled eval set in parallel.

data.summary: TC1 labeled eval set: conflict-lens ground truth, 36 pairs (10 contradicts / 6 supersedes / 20 none)
data.detail: WP-TC1 conflict-lens precision evaluation ground truth, authored by Vince (manager) 2026-07-15 — labeler independent of the implementer by design. 36 node pairs: 10 true conflicts (contradicts), 6 supersession lineages (lens should propose supersedes, NOT contradicts), 20 hard negatives labeled none (scope differences, refinements, topic-overlap-no-stance, causal sequences, default-plus-override traps, near-duplicates, different environments, incident-confirms-rule, non-HEAD superseded stance, opposed-wording-different-subject traps). Each pair carries both nodes' full shape (type, summary, detail, context, recorded_by_email, timestamp) plus label and rationale. Scoring: precision(contradicts) = true contradicts / all contradicts proposals, gate bar >= 0.9; recall reported, not gated. Destination in repo: tests/fixtures/conflict_lens_labeled_pairs.json (Vorpid checks it in with the TC1 WP; content is Vince's, verbatim). Canonical bytes at time of authoring live in the session scratchpad; this doc stores a copy.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-TC10 shipped: exclude_people filter (search + list_tasks) and returned-N-of-M completeness — gated and merged 1ab8f68, release held for 0.22.0 TC1 batch
data.detail:
Vorpid implemented WP-TC10 per brief doc:8622b44dbe4b + binding addendum (branch wp-tc10-filter, single commit 8538c3e off ed5d53a). Vince gated in the isolated worktree at the pin: independent pytest 894 passed/1 skipped (matching Vorpid's report exactly; +15 over the 879 baseline from tests/test_tc10_filter_completeness.py), ruff clean, full diff review, off-limits files untouched (no pyproject/plugin.json/uv.lock/journal/instructions.py). Merged --no-ff as 1ab8f68 after journal flush 6b03243 (which also committed the doc blobs for the TC1 brief and the labeled eval set).

Delivered: (1) exclude_people on cognition_search AND cognition_list_tasks — comma-separated casefolded emails matched on the server-resolved identity stamp (_node_email: recorded_by.email / task created_by.email), never free-text author/owner, unstamped never excluded; constraint/incident hits exempt on search (never-wipe doctrine) and don't count in excluded_count; user-invoked-only docstring guard; no persistent muting; disclosure keys excluded_count/excluded_for present iff param passed AND something excluded. (2) total_found/exhaustive always present on cognition_search + cognition_get_history; multi-project sums total_found, AND-reduces exhaustive, combines exclusion counts; count < total_found possible from truncation alone.

Load-bearing internal contract change (future-caller hazard): adaptive_vector_search's dedupe callback now returns (list, excluded_count) with the list NO LONGER capped to limit — the envelope caller ({results, total_found, exhaustive, excluded_count}) owns the limit-slice. Three conformance call sites: MCP path, cognition_get_workflow's internal lookup (["results"]), dashboard/api.py _dedupe closure (plumbing only, dashboard JSON unchanged). storage.get_recent_nodes gained additive keyword-only with_total=False with @overload for pyright; pre-existing callers untouched. Vorpid's full-suite run caught a real call site (get_workflow) that grep-and-fix missed — reinforces pattern: run the full suite before declaring an internal-contract refactor complete; grepping remembered call sites is insufficient.

Gate accepted Vorpid's reading that the test_xp2_routing.py "byte-identical default shape" guards needed their key-sets extended: M4 is a deliberate always-on shape change, not a regression. Fails-before verified by Vorpid on carve-out, dedupe-contract capping, and list_tasks filter. No version bump — 0.22.0 ships with TC1 per batching policy. Task 5222c93ca8f5 closes with this episode.

data.summary: Agent subagent_type registry resolves from the INSTALLED plugin at session start — worktree/branch-local agents/*.md files are unreachable by name pre-merge; inline-verbatim-body via general-purpose is the eval workaround
data.detail: Found by Vorpid during WP-TC1, confirmed by Vince's independent eval re-run. A new agents/*.md shipped on a WP branch cannot be spawned via subagent_type: "vibe-cognition:<name>" until the branch is merged AND the plugin reinstalled/updated — the session's agent registry is resolved from the installed plugin cache at session start, not from working-tree files. Consequence: any pre-merge exercise of a NEW agent definition (precision evals, smoke tests of agent behavior) must spawn subagent_type "general-purpose" with the shipped .md file's FULL VERBATIM BODY inlined as the prompt (frontmatter stripped), pinning the same model the frontmatter pins (explicit model param). No mocking, no paraphrase — the thing measured must be the shipped text. Used twice for TC1: Vorpid's eval run (precision 1.0, 4/4) and Vince's independent gate re-run (precision 1.0, 3/3), both at explicit haiku pins over 36 spawns each. Applies to every future WP that ships or edits agent definitions with a pre-merge eval/verification acceptance step.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-TC1 shipped as v0.22.0 (batching TC10): curation conflict lens gated with INDEPENDENT precision eval — contradicts precision 1.0 both runs, zero false positives across 72 judgments
data.detail:
Vorpid implemented WP-TC1 per brief doc:83c250e463ae (branch wp-tc1-conflict-lens, code c63b01a off 1ab8f68, bump bf70e0b). Vince gated: independent pytest 895/1 skipped + ruff clean; ZERO src/ diff (agent files/tests/scripts/docs only — curation containment untouched); fixture tests/fixtures/conflict_lens_labeled_pairs.json git-blob byte-identical to the gate-authored original doc:243f61758a3d (working-tree sha differs by CRLF checkout conversion only — compare git blobs, not checked-out files); orchestrator implements both HIGH corrections (Step-1.5 worklist capture with the silent-zero failure mode documented in place; eval split — stdlib scorer scripts/eval_conflict_lens.py + agent-driven model exercise) plus whole-run >=15-candidate 20% suspect cap, downgrade-to-supersedes review rule, report-template clause, model-pin + anti-fabrication extensions.

PRECISION GATE (ruled): Vince re-ran the eval independently of the implementer — spawned eval-orchestrator (sonnet) fanning 36 general-purpose haiku spawns, each given the shipped analyzer body verbatim + one labeled pair (labels never leaked). Result: contradicts precision 1.0 (3/3 tp, 0 fp), recall 0.3; Vorpid's run: 1.0 (4/4), recall 0.4. Zero false positives across both runs — none of the 26 hard negatives (scope-difference, refinement, default+override, opposed-wording-different-subject, non-HEAD, near-duplicate, incident-confirms-rule traps) drew a false contradicts from either run. Recall variance is haiku run-to-run noise on the ungated metric; the lens's uncertain-falls-to-supersedes/none bias is working as designed. No sonnet escalation needed.

Smoke: Vorpid's scripts/smoke_conflict_pass.py reproduced (live graph had 0 stance-bearing uncurated candidates → 0-candidate run is legitimate); Vince strengthened it by seeding a copy with a synthetic uncurated decision — timing invariant exercised NON-vacuously (1 captured vs 0 stale re-fetch). Accepted deviation: no full agent-driven /vibe-curate run on a copy (MCP tools bind to the live project; cognition_load_project is read-only) — compensating control: the first live curation run after the plugin update ships the conflict pass on real data, and Vince manually reviews any source=curate-conflict edges it commits (standing gate follow-up).

Non-blocking notes for a future WP: analyzer standalone-eval mode can emit supersedes verdicts as prose-only with empty JSON (9/36 pairs in Vince's run) — depresses measured supersedes recall, production JSON path unaffected; smoke script invariant check is vacuous at 0 candidates (a --seed-synthetic flag would fix).

Merged --no-ff as 3e07127 after flush 5cc1d0d. v0.22.0 = TC10 (exclude_people + N-of-M) + TC1 (conflict lens). NOTE: the conflict pass only goes live in sessions after Loki pins 3e07127 and users update the plugin (agent registry resolves from the installed cache — discovery 39cd2cecba3c); TC2 (read-time conflict annotation, task 888a21f729dd) stays queued until real curate-conflict edges exist. Task ad3a9c794814 closes with this episode.

data.summary: WP-TC4 brief (rev 3, APPROVED): claim-collision + reopen warnings on cognition_update_task
data.detail: Approved implementation brief for WP-TC4 (task f75cf9fa2b72, epic cedf4a8457e9). Three peer-review rounds (rev 1 REJECT: note-guard interaction + wrong test enumeration; rev 2 REJECT: bare same-status poke dies at the no-updatable-fields gate :1673-1679, and the same-status takeover had no mutation path; rev 3 APPROVE-WITH-CORRECTIONS, applied). Contracts: structured claim_warning key (kinds claim_collision / takeover_note_required / reopen; claimant + claimed_at + optional assigned_to info + message; absent-key discipline). Live claim = claimed_by present AND status in {in_progress, blocked}; identity = casefolded email equality, either-side-empty disengages everything. 2a transition-based takeover (blocked→in_progress over live foreign claim): note required, retryable rejection before mutation. 2b same-status takeover with note: NEW mutation branch parallel to the :1605 body; identity hoisted before the :1593 note guard; guard condition gains AND NOT same_status_takeover_shape; snapshot prior claimant before restamp. 2c foreign poke without note: no seizure, warns "you did NOT take it over"; bare poke succeeds via a no-updatable-fields gate carve-out keyed on the stashed warning, falling through to normal reembed+assembly. Reopen: warning on foreign done/cancelled→open naming closer + closed-at; suppressed when no closing transitions entry. _task_claimed_at extracted from dashboard/api.py:314-321 into the tools layer, single shared implementation. Test changes enumerated: test_task.py:418 gains note + sibling rejection test; :479 stays green unmodified. Never-blocks vs note-required interpretation recorded as a decision node, flagged for Colton's async review.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: TC4 interpretation (Vince, FLAGGED for Colton's async review): "takeover requires a transition note" is ENFORCED as retryable validation for unambiguous takeover shapes; every ambiguous/non-takeover shape warns-and-proceeds per "never blocks"
data.detail: Colton's TC4 ruling contains a genuine tension: "claiming a task already claimed_by someone else returns a warning... never blocks" vs "takeover (re-claim over a live claim) requires a transition note." Manager interpretation, made autonomously (Colton away) and OPEN TO OVERRULE: the note is enforced — blocked→in_progress over a live foreign claim rejects without a note (instructive error naming claimant + age; one retry with note always succeeds), and the same-status seizure only happens WITH a note (without one it warns "you did NOT take it over" and does not seize — never errors). Grounds: (1) each ruling sentence must do work — an unenforced "requires" is decoration; (2) the alternative (silent no-seizure without warning) recreates the double-work failure TC4 exists to prevent; (3) a single-retry-recoverable instructive error is a nudge, not a wall. REVERSAL PATH if Colton rules the other way: 2a's rejection relaxes to warning-plus-proceed in a patch — the warning plumbing is identical, only the early-return flips. Rejected alternative reading: "never blocks" as absolute (proceed always, note merely recommended) — makes sentence 2 unenforceable decoration. Recorded per the standing rule that rulings are not relitigated but interpretation gaps are resolved, recorded, and surfaced.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-TC9 brief (APPROVED): seniority + agent-origin weighting in retrieval — penalty-only, visible, never-wiping
data.detail: Approved implementation brief for WP-TC9 (task f746c5f9361e, epic cedf4a8457e9), peer-reviewed APPROVE-WITH-CORRECTIONS with all 8 findings applied. Core: penalty-only multiplicative weighting (multiplier ≤ 1.0 from a 1.0 baseline) makes the ruled invariants structural — constraints/incidents pinned 1.0 (reuse _NEVER_EXCLUDED_SEARCH_TYPES) can only rise; unverifiable = neutral 1.0; nothing filtered (total_found/exhaustive untouched). Two axes: seniority from person nodes (monotone down SENIORITY_LEVELS, small spread) and agent-origin penalty STRICTLY stronger than the whole seniority spread (human always outweighs agent, verified well-defined incl. unregistered-neutral vs agent-of-owner). Visibility: raw score never mutated; every hit ALWAYS carries weight {multiplier, seniority, from_agent, basis} + weighted_score. Basis vocabulary mirrors the dashboard's EXACT unverified boundary: human:<seniority> / human:unregistered (stamped, no person node — NOT unverified) / agent / exempt:<type> / unverified (no stamp at all). Where: inside _format_search_results (sorts uncapped list by weighted_score so the [:limit] slice takes weighted top); THE safety-critical edit is the multi-project outer merge at cognition_tools.py:3254 switching to weighted_score (else weighting silently no-ops for project="*"). Person resolution: ONE get_nodes_by_type(PERSON) scan per top-level call building an email→seniority map, threaded into the dedupe lambda from _search_with_embedding's enclosing scope (NOT a local in _format_search_results — resets per widening round); cache-inputs-vs-accumulate-outputs distinction from TC10 documented. get_workflow inherits weighting deliberately (can change WHICH workflow matches on multi-topic graphs) — own fixture + unconditional docstring note. Scope: cognition_search only (not get_history/list_tasks/prime/dashboard). Fallback fixtures a-d incl. the compound post-P13n/pre-TC6 unrostered case. Perf fixture forces a widening round and asserts the person sc
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-TC16 brief (APPROVED): role-aware prime sections — manager rollup + subordinate manager-decisions view
data.detail: Approved implementation brief for WP-TC16 (task 9f563272da15, epic cedf4a8457e9). Peer-review round 1 REJECT (3 blocking: reports_to vs actual reports_to_email field name — would have made role derivation silently empty; dashboard-changes wording contradicting the required api.py:21 import edit; missing subordinate-section placement) + 5 lesser findings; all applied; revision APPROVED. Contracts: role derivation from ONE person-node scan (manager = someone's reports_to_email casefolds to session email; subordinate = own person node's reports_to_email non-empty; middle manager = both; role-less/unregistered/personalization-off = prime BYTE-IDENTICAL, the strongest fixture). Manager section "## Your Team": one TASK scan bucketed by claimant email; in-progress rows with claimant+age, stale iff now-claimed_at > timedelta(days=prime_stale_claim_days default 7) STRICT (exactly-7d not stale; null claimed_at never stale), blocked rows; prime_rollup_cap default 5 total, stale-first ordering, overflow line. Subordinate section "## Your Manager's Recent Decisions": decisions by manager email, NO HEAD-filter (mirrors _format_decisions exactly — the global model has no supersedes check), prime_manager_decision_limit default 3; own-claims half of the ruling already delivered by Your Open Tasks claimed_by branch (documented mapping). Pinned order: Team Critical → Your Team → Your Manager's Recent Decisions → Your Recent Activity. Helper relocation: _task_claimed_at moves to a light shared module (cognition/task_meta.py or models.py — both verified cycle-free), cognition_tools RE-EXPORTS the name so the old import path keeps working (test_task.py:32-37 unmodified), dashboard/api.py:21 gets the mechanical one-line path edit (authorized in-scope plumbing), prime imports the shared home — prime stays tools-free. Three new knobs in PrimeConfig + Settings with the defaults-equivalence test extended. Terminology: reporting relationship ≠ person.role job title. Known limits: single-manage
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-TC9 shipped as v0.23.0 (batching TC4): penalty-only seniority + agent-origin weighting on cognition_search — gate caught and fixed a cross-version seniority-vocab crash pre-merge
data.detail:
Vorpid implemented WP-TC9 per brief doc:acbf79422ad2 (branch wp-tc9-seniority-weighting: code 2cc6143, gate-finding fixup 98dcca4, bump 3647408; off main 7507ee1). Shipped: penalty-only multiplicative weighting (owner/senior 1.0, mid 0.95, junior 0.9, agent 0.85 — strict dominance: every agent multiplier below every human tier), weighted_score = raw score × multiplier with raw score never mutated, weight {multiplier, seniority, from_agent, basis} ALWAYS present per hit, basis vocabulary mirroring the dashboard's exact verified/unverified boundary (human:<tier> / human:unregistered / agent / exempt:<type> / unverified), constraints+incidents pinned 1.0 (reuse of _NEVER_EXCLUDED_SEARCH_TYPES), sorting inside the TC10 dedupe callback so the [:limit] slice takes the weighted top, THE safety-critical multi-project merge resort switched to weighted_score (fails-before-probed — raw-score resort silently no-ops weighting for project="*" only), per-call person-map memoization in _search_with_embedding's enclosing scope (fails-before-probed against per-round degradation), per-entry registries in fan-out, get_workflow weighting inheritance disclosed unconditionally.

GATE FINDING (Vince, fixed pre-merge as 98dcca4): _hit_weight indexed _SENIORITY_MULTIPLIERS[seniority] hard and _person_seniority_map used direct key access — write paths validate seniority but JOURNAL REPLAY does not, so a person node with an out-of-vocab tier (newer plugin version, hand-edited journal, cross-version team) crashed every cognition_search touching that author with KeyError. Violates the C1 cross-version doctrine (readers degrade on unknown vocab, never crash — same class as embedding-sync per-node unknown-type tolerance). Fix: .get(seniority, 1.0) neutral-on-unknown with the RAW tier string surfaced verbatim (visible, not hidden, never penalized), .get() chains in the map (missing key → unregistered-equivalent); 2 tests fails-before-verified against the exact KeyError. STANDING LESSON: every read path that indexes by a journal-carried vocabulary value needs the tolerance treatment — write-side validation is not protection against replay.

Independent gate runs: 930/1 skipped at 2cc6143; 931 passed + 1 chromadb cross-process failure at 98dcca4 that PASSED in isolation (documented C1 flake-watch class, subsystem untouched by the delta — accepted as flake); ruff clean both; 20 new tests total covering the full acceptance matrix incl. fallbacks a-f. Merged --no-ff 5435412 after flush c57ffa4. v0.23.0 = TC4 (claim-collision warnings) + TC9. Task f746c5f9361e closes with this episode. Gate D scenario 4 (seniority story arc with visible weights) now has its mechanism. Gate C2 measures weighting latency after Wave 3 completes (TC16 remaining).
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Gate C2 brief (APPROVED): Wave-3 stability audit — weighting latency + multi-user prime size/latency
data.detail: Approved Gate C2 brief (gate task 85b2b4de8aee, epic cedf4a8457e9), peer-reviewed APPROVE-WITH-CORRECTIONS with 3 HIGH pass-bar fixes applied. Part A (Vince runs, writes no code): A1 weighted-search latency sweeping BOTH total node count (1k/5k/10k) AND roster (0/10/100) — the real cost driver is get_nodes_by_type's whole-graph scan (O(total_nodes), not O(roster)); overhead bar ≤10ms p95 delta vs roster-0 at same node count, linear-in-nodes growth disclosed, NO cross-mode comparison (single-project times _search_cognition directly; project="*" must go through ctx dispatch — each judged against its own control). A2 prime: (i) like-for-like re-run of C1's exact B3 scenario at the C2 pin vs episode 3a1d1096b57a's ~11ms medians; (ii) new-topology absolute budget — 1/5/25 users, 3-level reports_to chain, manager with 10 reports, p95 ≤ 250ms + every section cap bounds output (prime_rollup_cap=5 per TC16 brief doc:01acff533612). A3 replay comparison via bench_wave1 --journal-dir vs C1 figures. A4 real-graph prime render sanity. A5 chromadb flake tally (escalate at ≥3 same-test flakes across epic gates). Part B harness (Vorpid WP, after TC16 gates — single-implementer pipeline makes earlier splitting pointless): extend bench_wave1.py or sibling — --persons/--nodes knobs, stamped/from_agent/claimed_by+backdated-stale fixture population, search + prime + multi-project benchmark modes, fixed-vector embedder injection, scan-count via monkeypatched _person_seniority_map counting wrapper (flagged fragility, scripts-only), JSON tail, seeded, no product code, no release (C1-harness precedent ed5d53a). Remediation substitution flagged for Colton: "blocks next wave" has no next wave after Wave 3 — C2 failure blocks the epic-close gates (B-final/D).
data.author: Vince

data.summary: WP-TC16 shipped: role-aware prime sections (manager rollup / subordinate view) — merged to main 14ded30, bump HELD for 0.24.0 batch
data.detail:
Vorpid implemented (b1fc0c3 + fixup 2313b94 on wp-tc16-role-prime, off main 5435412); Vince gated and merged (--no-ff, 14ded30). Closes task 9f563272da15 under epic cedf4a8457e9; brief doc:01acff533612.

Shipped: _derive_role (ONE person scan, reports_to_email; MANAGER iff direct reports, SUBORDINATE iff manager email, both may hold); '## Your Team' manager rollup (stale iff claim age STRICTLY > prime_stale_claim_days=7, null claimed_at never stale, cap prime_rollup_cap=5 stale→blocked→in-progress recency-desc, overflow line); '## Your Manager's Recent Decisions' (NO HEAD-filter, mirrors _format_decisions, cap prime_manager_decision_limit=3); pinned order Team Critical → Your Team → Your Manager's Recent Decisions → Your Recent Activity; byte-identical prime for role-less users; _task_claimed_at relocated to new stdlib-only cognition/task_meta.py with cognition_tools re-export (test_task.py import path unchanged) + mechanical dashboard/api.py import edit; 3 knobs in PrimeConfig+Settings+main() threading.

Gate: round 1 REJECT with 2 blocking findings (both review catches — suite was green 952 at b1fc0c3): (F1) _format_your_team compared claimed_by.email RAW against casefolded roster keys — claimed_by is a verbatim git-config stamp, never casefolded at write (resolve_git_identity returns verbatim; _update_task's casefolded copies are comparison-only locals), so a mixed-case git identity silently vanished from the manager rollup; fixed by read-time casefold per the in-file _task_matches_email/_node_email precedent. (F2) naive-but-valid ISO 'at' parses via fromisoformat then raises TypeError (not ValueError) on aware-naive subtraction — _humanize_claim_age had the subtraction OUTSIDE its try; the stale check caught only ValueError — crashing generate_prime (the SessionStart hook) for every user of the graph; same class as TC9's 98dcca4 replay-tolerance fixup; fixed via shared _parse_iso_datetime normalizing naive→UTC at both sites. Both with fails-before tests. Re-gate at 2313b94: 954 passed / ruff clean, pyright 21/21 parity, delta touched only prime.py/test file/CHANGELOG.

Vorpid's self-caught lesson (round 1, his report): his first null-claimed_at probe was a false negative — mutant (is_stale=True default) still passed because both rows landed in the same sort bucket and recency-desc alone preserved expected order; fixed by giving the wrongly-stale row a MORE recent timestamp so only correct bucketing produces the observed order.

TC16 closes Wave 3. Version bump HELD — TC16 batches with TC14/TC15 as the 0.24.0 candidate. Next: Gate C2 (Part B harness WP to Vorpid per doc:980816e8df8b; Vince runs Part A at pin 14ded30).

data.summary: Provenance stamp emails (claimed_by/created_by/recorded_by) are stored VERBATIM from git config — every read-side comparison must casefold at read; write-side casefolding only exists for person-node fields
data.detail: Recurred at TC16 gate (F1): _format_your_team compared claimed_by.email raw against casefolded person-roster keys, silently dropping any claimant whose git config carries a mixed-case email. The correct precedent already existed in the same file — _task_matches_email and _node_email both casefold the stamp at read "so it's correct called in isolation too" — but a NEW call site missed applying it. resolve_git_identity (git_identity.py:171) returns email verbatim; _update_task's casefolded emails (:1763/:1769) are comparison-only locals, the stored caller dict is raw. Person-node email/reports_to_email ARE casefolded at write (_casefold_email) — the asymmetry is the trap: roster keys are safe to compare raw against each other, stamps never are. Checklist rule: any new code comparing a stamp email to anything must casefold the stamp side at read. Vorpid's own framing: "know the pattern but still miss applying it to a new call site" — distinct failure class from not knowing the pattern; review should specifically enumerate NEW comparison sites against known normalization asymmetries. Companion to the TC9 98dcca4 lesson (journal-carried vocabulary tolerance): both are read-path defenses that write-side discipline does not provide.

data.detail:
# Scope: WP-TC14 — "Since You Were Gone" digest (per-email last-seen marker + prime section)

Status: APPROVED after corrections (peer-review rev 1 APPROVE-WITH-CORRECTIONS — 1 HIGH: RMW lock + real concurrency test; 4 MEDIUM: strict-> divergence framing, empty-graph stamp scope, never-silent-no-op scoping, clock-skew/null-entry acceptance; citation fixes — all applied). Vorpid implements; Vince gates. Epic cedf4a8457e9; task c11ce10ae9af (floating small, low severity). Ruling baked into the task: marker is machine-local, UNCOMMITTED, keyed PER EMAIL (manager + subordinate sharing a machine must not stomp each other); first-run/ephemeral-sandbox fallback = capped lookback window — never a full-history dump, never a silent no-op. Scope of that ruling (pinned here, not just in Design): "never a silent no-op" binds the MECHANISM — a missing marker must produce the fallback-window digest, never a skipped section; it does NOT bind the outcome — a genuinely empty delta renders nothing, like every other section. Prime section, journal-scan only, light-import.

## PROBLEM

A returning user has no "what changed while I was away" view: prime shows recent-N sections regardless of whether the user already saw those rows yesterday. Teams want the delta — teammates' new decisions/constraints/incidents since MY last session — which requires remembering when my last session was, per email, per machine.

## CURRENT MECHANICS (haiku-verified citations, at pin 14ded30)

- generate_prime is PURE READ-ONLY — zero file writes inside it. The only .cognition mutation in prime.py is `_consume_rehydrate_flag()`'s unlink (prime.py:693-725, `contextlib.suppress(OSError)`), called from `main()` at :779 — i.e. mutations live on the CLI/hook path, not in the library function. This invariant is load-bearing (dashboard/tests call generate_prime).
- Machine-local per-email state precedent: `.cognition/onboard-declined` (ONBOARD_DECLINE_FILENAME, prime.py:30) — plain text, one casefolded email per li

data.summary: Gate C2 PASSED: Wave-3 stability audit — weighted-search latency, multi-user prime size/latency, replay regression all green; Gate C complete
data.detail:
Run per brief doc:980816e8df8b at post-Wave-3 main (TC16 merge 14ded30; harness merge 761f407, scripts/bench_wave3.py by Vorpid at 3ad4772, gated 954/ruff clean). Vince ran all Part A items; scenario driver for A2-ii composed from the harness's exposed primitives in session scratchpad (never committed).

A1 single-project weighted search (nodes 1k/5k/10k × roster 0/10/100 × limit 10/50, 20 reps + 50-rep re-runs of every borderline combo): PASS. p50 roster deltas vs roster-0 at same node count ≤4.5ms everywhere (mostly <1ms); all six borderline 20-rep p95 deltas collapsed at 50 reps to ≤6.1ms (worst: 5k/roster-10/limit-50 +6.1ms, non-monotonic vs its roster-100 sibling +0.1ms — noise, not roster cost). Bar: ≤10ms p95 delta — met. Person-scan memo held in ALL runs (scans == calls, never per widening round). Roster-0 p50 growth 1k→5k→10k: 4.9→5.9→8.9ms (limit 10) — linear-in-nodes as disclosed, no super-linear signature.

A1 multi-project (project="*", two graphs, each vs OWN roster-0 control, never cross-mode): PASS. 50-rep p95: roster-0 16.0ms vs roster-100 19.5ms (+3.4ms). Disclosure: 20-rep runs showed a fat first-call warm-up tail (single max samples up to 1.4s from lazy init on the second graph) — excluded at 50 reps, medians unaffected (12-15ms throughout).

A2-i like-for-like C1 regression (exact B3 scenario re-run at C2 pin): PASS. B2 replay 9.2ms @ 1k (C1 range 9-17ms); prime medians 7.5-7.9ms vs C1's ~11ms — within noise, role derivation adds no measurable cost on a role-less graph. B4 embeds-per-op exactly 1 (all four ops); B5 convergence field-exact.

A2-ii role-topology absolute budget (1/5/25 stamped users, 3-level reports_to chain, manager with 10 reports carrying fresh/stale/blocked claims): PASS. Manager prime p95 28.2ms at 25 users (budget 250ms — 9x headroom); subordinate p95 17.8ms; Your Team capped at exactly prime_rollup_cap=5 rows + overflow line regardless of report count (cap proven binding with 20 claimed tasks). Disclosure: subordinate manager-decisions section empty-gated (no decisions stamped by that manager in fixture) — derivation exercised; rendering is TC16 test territory.

A3 real-journal replay at pin: PASS. 2,199 lines, 0 malformed, 0 warnings, expected/actual exact by set arithmetic (523 nodes/911 edges), 23.3ms (C1: 38ms at 2,151 lines). Growth since C1 (+19/+19) consistent.

A4 real-graph prime byte-size sanity (owner email): PASS. 4.2ms, 3,634 bytes; role sections absent + non-personalized view — EXPECTED (no roster, effectively one stamped identity; the role-less compat invariant passing on real data).

A5 chromadb flake tally across epic gates: 1 (test_chromadb_cross_process concurrent_open at TC9 gate, passed in isolation; zero flakes in TC10/TC1/TC4/TC16×2/C2-harness suite runs). Threshold ≥3 not met — no fix WP.

VERDICT: PASS, no remediation needed. Gate C (C1+C2) complete — task 85b2b4de8aee closes. Epic-close gates B-final and D are unblocked from C2's side. Remediation-substitution note (C2 failure would have blocked B-final/D) remains flagged for Colton but is now moot for this epic.

# Scope: WP-TC15 — Curation-containment observability (get_status surfaces out-of-curation edge writes)

Status: APPROVED after corrections (peer-review rev 1 APPROVE-WITH-CORRECTIONS — 1 HIGH: get_statistics return-type widening for pyright ratchet; 3 MINOR: docs citation, baseline narrative, bump-coordination mechanism — all applied). Load-bearing claim VERIFIED by reviewer against code: per-edge `source` survives replay into the MultiDiGraph edge_data that get_statistics's existing loop (storage.py:637 `for _, _, edge_data in self._graph.edges(data=True)`) already walks. Vorpid implements; Vince gates. Epic cedf4a8457e9; task 31f7a638ad2b (floating small, low severity). Ruling baked into the task: the containment rule (ONLY curate-orchestrator calls add_edge/add_edges_batch/mark_curated) stays docs-only; this WP adds the MINIMAL observability version — the server counts semantic-edge writes that occur outside a curation run and surfaces the count in get_status. NO token handshake / auth — PERMANENTLY REJECTED (adversarial threat model out of scope; local trust domain, accidental misuse only). Scope is EDGE writes per the task text — mark_curated misuse is out of scope (it surfaces indirectly via uncurated-count anomalies).

## PROBLEM

Containment is a documented convention with zero visibility: if a main-session agent hand-authors edges via cognition_add_edge/add_edges_batch (accidental misuse — the exact failure the constraint exists to prevent), nothing surfaces it. The graph's semantic-edge provenance quietly degrades and nobody notices until edge quality complaints appear.

## CURRENT MECHANICS (haiku-verified citations, at pin ~761f407)

- EVERY edge carries a `source` string, journaled and replayed faithfully: CognitionEdge.source (models.py:98), persisted in the add_edge journal entry (storage.py:270), replayed with legacy fallback to "curator" when absent (storage.py:1204), present in graph edge_data (storage.py:277).
- Complete producer enumeration (sur

data.detail:
Vorpid implemented (5a6a8a4 + fixup 99c3a8e on wp-tc14-digest, off main 761f407); Vince gated and merged (--no-ff, 9a09137). Closes task c11ce10ae9af under epic cedf4a8457e9; brief doc:de8ad0120603.

Shipped: .cognition/last-seen.json (machine-local, git-ignored via hygiene v4 glob entry last-seen.json*, casefolded email → aware-UTC ISO); _stamp_last_seen ONLY in prime main() after output (generate_prime pure-read invariant held; instructions.py compact-reinject proven non-stamping by test), lock-guarded RMW (git_hygiene._acquire_lock + bounded 3×20ms retry), atomic tmp+os.replace, suppress(OSError), stray-tmp unlink on entry; '## Since You Were Gone' section (personalized-only, placed after Your Manager's Recent Decisions): decisions/constraints/incidents strictly newer than marker via lexicographic ISO compare (no datetime parsing — TC16-F2 crash class structurally absent), own stamps excluded, unstamped included (awareness-of-content ruling), constraints HEAD-filtered / decisions+incidents bare, cap prime_digest_cap=5 + overflow, fallback window prime_digest_fallback_days=7 when marker missing/malformed; knobs in PrimeConfig+Settings.

Gate: round 1 REJECT with 1 finding — F1 stray-tmp leak: hygiene entry was the bare filename, not matching last-seen.json.tmp; a process killed between write_text and os.replace leaves an unignored .tmp that the journal-flush 'git add .cognition/' procedure would COMMIT (machine-local state syncing via git, the exact property the ruling protects). Fixed in 99c3a8e: glob entry last-seen.json* (v4 amended in place, unreleased), entry-unlink of strays, real-git-init check-ignore fails-before test. RULING RECORDED (do not relitigate): Vorpid's bounded lock-retry deviation from the brief's literal "skip on lock failure" text ACCEPTED — 3 attempts × 20ms, still skip-after-exhaust, hook never blocked, git_hygiene untouched; it strengthens the per-email no-lost-entry guarantee and makes the concurrency criterion deterministically testable.

Vorpid's self-caught test lesson (recorded verbatim class): his first entry-unlink fails-before was vacuous — write_text TRUNCATES, so a stray tmp is incidentally overwritten by any successful write, masking the unlink's effect; fixed by monkeypatching write_text to fail on the same call, isolating the unlink's own contribution. Same class as the TC16 same-bucket probe lesson: a probe must arrange state so ONLY the guarded behavior produces the observed outcome.

Suite: 994 passed / ruff clean (Vince independent, matches Vorpid). Concurrency tests genuinely concurrent: Barrier(2) dual-writer with pre-seeded third-entry survival; Event-based mutex proof. Bump HELD — standalone 0.24.0 bump branch after TC15 merges (coordination mechanism from TC15 brief peer review). Next: TC15 handoff (doc:c4ce636d9c2d).

data.summary: Pin pyright version (PYRIGHT_PYTHON_FORCE_VERSION) — resolved-version drift makes local pyright counts incomparable across environments
data.detail: Observed at the TC15 gate (2026-07-15): the same codebase measures wildly different pyright error counts per environment because pyright-python auto-downloads whatever version it resolves — main 9a09137 = 8 (CI committed baseline), 21 (Vorpid's env), 39 (Vince's env, which also printed the 'Please install the new version or set PYRIGHT_PYTHON_FORCE_VERSION to latest' nag). This makes the ratchet (.github/scripts/pyright_ratchet.py vs .github/pyright-baseline.txt) meaningful ONLY in CI, and forces gates to fall back to same-environment main-parity diffing (which did work: it isolated 3 real TC15 test-file errors). Fix: pin the pyright version explicitly — e.g. pin the `pyright` dev-dependency version in pyproject.toml (or set PYRIGHT_PYTHON_FORCE_VERSION in CI + document the same pin for local runs) so CI and every local environment resolve the identical checker. Acceptance: `uv run pyright --version` prints the same version on a fresh clone as CI uses; ratchet baseline re-validated (and re-counted if the pinned version differs from what produced baseline=8); brief note in CONTRIBUTING/README dev section. Small, standalone WP — do NOT fold into a feature branch.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.detail: Observed at the TC15 gate (2026-07-15). Two compounding causes, disentangled during the F1 fixup: (1) SCOPE — Vorpid's gate command was `pyright src/vibe_cognition` (src-only), while CI's ratchet (.github/scripts/pyright_ratchet.py) runs bare `pyright`, which scans the whole repo including tests/; his "21 errors, exact parity" reports structurally excluded the test file containing the 3 real TC15 errors (Vorpid has switched to bare `pyright`). (2) VERSION DRIFT — the residual gap: CI's committed baseline is 8 while both local environments measure main at 39 repo-wide under their auto-resolved pyright (Vince's env also printed the 'set PYRIGHT_PYTHON_FORCE_VERSION to latest' nag). pyright-python auto-downloads whatever version it resolves, so the ratchet baseline is meaningful ONLY in CI, and gates must fall back to same-environment main-parity diffing (which did work: it isolated the 3 TC15 test-file errors). Fix: pin the pyright version explicitly — pin the `pyright` dev-dependency in pyproject.toml (or set PYRIGHT_PYTHON_FORCE_VERSION in CI + document the same pin for local runs) so CI and every local environment resolve the identical checker; ALSO document that gate/local runs must be bare `pyright` (repo-wide), matching the ratchet. Acceptance: `uv run pyright --version` prints the same version on a fresh clone as CI uses; ratchet baseline re-validated (re-counted if the pinned version differs from what produced baseline=8); brief note in CONTRIBUTING/README dev section. Small, standalone WP — do NOT fold into a feature branch.

data.summary: WP-TC15 shipped: curation-containment observability — get_status surfaces edge_sources histogram + edges_outside_curation (merge 4d8b485)
data.detail: Vorpid implemented per peer-reviewed brief doc:c4ce636d9c2d; Vince gated and merged --no-ff at 4d8b485 (WP pin 3076d84 + F1 fixup 77df070, branch wp-tc15-containment-obs off 9a09137). FEATURE: derive-don't-track — get_statistics's existing edge loop (storage.py:637) now also builds an edge_sources histogram and edges_outside_curation count (counted: manual/batch/unknown; exempt frozenset _EDGE_SOURCE_CURATION_EXEMPT: deterministic, task-parent, curate-skill, curate-conflict, curate-cluster, curator). No write-path/journal/persistence changes; restart-proof and clone-identical by construction. Return type widened to dict[str, int | dict[str, int]] via sibling-dict at return boundary (pyright union-ripple contained). data.summary: chromadb cross-process concurrent-open flake: RustBindingsAPI 'bindings' AttributeError race fails test_chromadb_cross_process ~1-in-3 on Windows
data.detail: Surfaced during the DashV2 gate (2026-07-15), unrelated to that WP (reproduced identically on main 7537a6c). tests/test_chromadb_cross_process.py::test_cross_process_chromadb_concurrent_open_and_write_no_collision fails ~1-in-3 runs EVEN IN ISOLATION on Vince's machine (Windows 11, chromadb from current uv.lock): concurrent chromadb.PersistentClient opens race inside chromadb — get_tenant raises AttributeError "'RustBindingsAPI' object has no attribute 'bindings'", the recovery path (_validate_tenant_database → ValueError "Could not connect to tenant default_tenant") then crashes AGAIN in SharedSystemClient._release_system → rust.py stop() → del self.bindings (same missing attribute). Our _retry_chromadb_open (embeddings/storage.py:42) does NOT catch/retry this — it surfaces as a worker crash. The test's own failure message says this is a REAL finding about the primary concurrent-use feature, not noise — and it is: two sessions opening the same project's chroma store simultaneously can crash one of them at startup. Investigate: (a) does a newer chromadb fix the bindings race (check upstream issues); (b) should _retry_chromadb_open's retry predicate include AttributeError/ValueError from this race class, since the second open attempt typically succeeds; (c) is this Windows-specific (rust bindings DLL init). Note prior related territory: v0.15.3/v0.15.4 mid-session tool-freeze incidents were chroma-adjacent DLL-load wedges — different failure mode (hang vs crash) but same native-bindings init fragility. Flake tally discipline: track occurrences; if CI starts hitting it, priority escalates.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Marketplace pin-relay batching: one pin request per release-train boundary, sent only after the bump merge is final — never superseding-request chains
data.detail: Lesson from the v0.19.0–v0.24.0 consolidated pin (2026-07-15). While Loki's channel was down, Vince queued 5 superseding pin requests in ~10 hours (361e76a/461031e/ec5a7fd/3e07127/5435412, finally 7537a6c) as releases kept landing. Loki correctly treated the churn as a stale-relay warning sign (a pattern that has bitten this pipeline before), paused, and got an explicit go from Colton before pushing — adding a human round-trip that batching would have avoided. Colton has since confirmed unconditional pre-approval for Vince's pin requests, so approval is not the bottleneck; the practice still stands on its own: (1) send a pin request ONLY at a release-train boundary, after the bump merge is pushed and final; (2) if the relay target is offline and more releases land, EDIT/replace the queued request silently rather than sending escalating superseding messages — or simply wait, since the pin always points at latest main anyway; (3) the request should carry the verification recipe (SHA is HEAD of main, plugin.json version at that SHA, expected fast-forward from previous pin) so the relayer can verify mechanically — Loki's independent checks (git ls-remote, version-at-SHA, gh api compare ahead/behind) are the reference procedure. Applies to any human/agent relay step where requests supersede each other: churn at the relay boundary reads as instability downstream even when each individual request was correct.

data.summary: Edge direction conventions: SUPERSEDES is directional newer→older; CONTRADICTS is stored ONE-WAY with ARBITRARY direction — conflict-membership checks must be bidirectional for contradicts, incoming-only for supersedes
data.detail: Two graph-edge conventions that every consumer (UI, prime, API, tests) must respect, established across DashV2/DashV3 (relayed by Vorpid, who hit both traps): (1) SUPERSEDES points newer→older — "B replaces A" mints edge B→A (cognition_tools.py:3741/3824, prime.py:331). HEAD = no INCOMING supersedes; a node with an OUTGOING supersedes edge is the resolution, not itself stale. Trap: hand-constructed test edges built backwards pass bare-count assertions silently (bit twice in DashV2's new tests + once in the pre-existing pm_storage fixture). (2) CONTRADICTS is stored as ONE directed edge with ARBITRARY direction — cognition_add_edge's docstring says "either direction" and NO reciprocal edge is ever minted. Therefore any "is this node in conflict?" check MUST look at incoming AND outgoing contradicts edges; an incoming-only check silently reports only one side of every contradicts pair as conflicted while the other looks clean. This exact bug shipped in V1's drawer conflictBannerHTML (incoming-only) and was caught at the DashV3 brief peer review (BLOCKING) before propagating into the list-level conflicted flag; both were fixed in WP-DashV3 (_is_conflicted api.py + banner successor-scan, merge 1ee0c84). Canonical implementations to copy: _is_conflicted (dashboard/api.py) for membership; conflictBannerHTML (app.js) for direction-legible rendering ("contradicted by" incoming vs "contradicts" outgoing). Any future conflict-membership logic (prime sections, search filters, TC2 conflict pipeline consumers) starts from these two rules.
data.author: Vince

data.summary: Doc-fix WP (Gate B-final findings): README edge-authoring contradiction + tool-surface disclosure gaps
data.detail: Consolidated documentation fixes from the Gate B-final fresh-eyes audit (task 4f024867dbcb, run 2026-07-15 at pin b4476ed/1ee0c84). One doc-only WP; no behavior changes. (1) HIGHEST: README.md:282 says semantic edges "are the agent's job: after recording, run the /vibe-curate skill (or add them with cognition_add_edge)" — the parenthetical directly invites the exact violation the containment rule forbids and edges_outside_curation counts; delete/replace with the curate-only path. (2) cognition_search / cognition_get_history / cognition_get_neighbors docstrings are silent on supersession: disclose that results are NOT HEAD-filtered and a superseded node carries NO marker in the result shape (fresh-eyes agent called this the single biggest misuse risk — acting on a stale superseded decision surfaced by search; a result-shape superseded flag is a separate feature decision, note it as an option but don't build it in a doc WP). (3) get_status docstring: edges_outside_curation explains meaning but prescribes no next action — add remediation guidance (inspect edge_sources histogram; accidental manual/batch edges may be removed via cognition_remove_edge or left with the baseline documented; re-run /vibe-curate for missing legitimate edges). (4) SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS (instructions.py): session-start priming is never described as a feature — tools reference "the session-start view" obliquely; add a short paragraph naming what prime injects (constraints, open/your tasks, personalized sections) so newcomers don't have to infer it from fragments; also mention the TC15 observability keys exist (drift check found instructions.py has no mention). (5) cognition_add_edge/add_edges_batch/mark_curated docstrings: state explicitly that the curator-only restriction is a documented CONVENTION detected via edges_outside_curation, not a server-enforced ACL (fresh-eyes agent correctly could not tell). (6) Minor drift folds: readme.py's Since You Were Gone paragraph doesn't state constraints are HEAD-filtered there (README does); skills/vibe-cognition/SKILL.md claiming rules omit the claim_warning kind enum README documents. Dismissed as non-findings (recorded for the audit trail): SKILL.md files not describing dashboard views (skills are workflow guides, not feature docs); from_agent self-report spoofing (ruled out of scope, local trust domain, already disclosed in the surface); type-vs-node_type key split (already loudly disclosed in docstrings); drift claim that '## Your Recent Activity' is undefined (it exists, prime.py:287-303 — drift-agent miss).

data.summary: cognition_list_tasks rows lack claimed_by/claimed_at — no read-only way to see who holds a task claim from the tool surface
data.detail: Gate B-final fresh-eyes finding (verified against code 2026-07-15): _list_tasks rows (cognition_tools.py:1583-1595) carry created_by/owner/assigned_to/from_agent but NOT claimed_by or claimed_at — while the dashboard's _task_row (dashboard/api.py) exposes both. A cautious agent following the documented claim etiquette has NO read-only way to learn who currently holds a claim; the only path is attempting cognition_update_task(status="in_progress") and being rejected/warned — discovery by collision, the opposite of etiquette. Fix: add claimed_by (identity object) and claimed_at (ISO or null) to _list_tasks rows (derive claimed_at via the existing task_meta._task_claimed_at, same as the dashboard), document in the docstring, cover with a row-shape test. data.summary: Search results lack conflict/supersession provenance flags — stale or contradicted hits are indistinguishable from live facts (Gate D S2/S4)
data.detail: Gate D consumer-audit finding (both personas, 2026-07-15, isolated-graph scenarios 2 and 4 — the audit's sharpest catch). cognition_search result dicts carry NO marker for (a) a hit that has a contradicts edge (either direction) or (b) a hit that has been SUPERSEDED by a newer node — while the dashboard's list rows DO carry `conflicted` for the same nodes (inconsistent read surface). Measured live consequence: in scenario 4 the superseded junior finding OUTRANKED its senior correction (weighted 0.708 vs 0.688 — the junior 0.9 multiplier doesn't overcome a raw-relevance gap), so an agent trusting the top hit acts on the stale explanation with nothing to catch it; get_superseded_chain recovers the truth but only via an unprompted extra call. Proposed WP: each search result gains provenance flags derived at format time — `conflicted: bool` (reuse the _is_conflicted direction semantics: bidirectional contradicts, pattern 6ed494680fb3) and `superseded_by: node_id | null` (incoming SUPERSEDES source, i.e. the newer node's id — an actionable pointer, not just a bool). Cost: up to 3 edge lookups per formatted hit (10-50 per search), negligible. Consider ALSO whether a superseded hit should be down-weighted or annotated in weighted_score — design decision for the brief; the FLAGS are the non-negotiable part (visibility first, ranking second). Docstring must document both fields (tool-surface audit applies — return shape change). Complements doc task 6e54452b9735 item (2) (disclosure); this is the feature half. Blocks Gate D scenarios 2 (read half) and 4 → re-run both after ship.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Onboarding payoff gaps: auto-personalize ignores registered person nodes, and registration gets no prime acknowledgment (Gate D S5)
data.detail: Gate D consumer-audit finding (both personas, 2026-07-15, scenario 5). Two related gaps in the fresh-user experience. (1) _should_personalize's 'auto' mode (prime.py:193-205) personalizes only when the graph has >1 DISTINCT STAMPED WRITER email — deliberately, so a solo graph stays byte-identical to the pre-P13n digest. Consumer consequence measured live: a team's first onboarded member (three person nodes registered, but every node so far written by one person) gets ZERO personalized sections after registering — no manager-decisions section even though their manager authored decisions; the experience is indistinguishable from being unregistered except the New Here banner disappears. Proposed: widen the auto heuristic to `>1 distinct stamped email OR >1 registered person node` — multiple registered people is a STRONGER team signal than multiple writers, and the solo-graph byte-identity guarantee is preserved (a solo graph has ≤1 person node). The solo-identity regression fixture must keep passing. (2) No registration acknowledgment: after cognition_register_person, the next prime shows nothing confirming who the graph now thinks you are; both audit personas independently flagged the missing payoff. Proposed: when personalize is on and a person node matches the current email, prime opens the personalized block with a one-line identity header (e.g. 'You are registered as Jamie Junior — backend engineer (junior), reporting to Casey Lead') — one line, no new knobs, self-gates like other TC16 sections. NOT an acknowledgment/interceptor feature (those stay permanently rejected) — a passive display line. Blocks Gate D scenario 5 → re-run after ship.
data.author: Colton Dyck

data.summary: Write-time conflict advisory: cognition_record returns nothing when a new decision semantically collides with an existing one (Gate D S2, write half) — FLAGGED for Colton
data.detail: Gate D consumer-audit finding (2026-07-15, scenario 2 write side): the return from recording a decision that directly contradicts an existing settled decision is completely bare — no signal at the moment that matters most (before the second decision calcifies); the only trace is get_statistics' aggregate uncurated count, unattributed. Proposed direction: an ADVISORY-ONLY field in cognition_record's return for DECISION (and maybe CONSTRAINT) nodes — e.g. `similar_existing: [{id, summary, score}]` from a cheap embedding similarity check against same-type nodes above a threshold — mirroring the existing possible_duplicate_of precedent (episodes, reference-based, advisory, node still created). BOUNDARY (pin hard): the node is ALWAYS created; no blocking, no acknowledgment step, no confirmation round-trip — write-time interceptor/acknowledgment mechanics are PERMANENTLY REJECTED (M3 ruling) and this must not reinvent them; it is a returned FYI, exactly like possible_duplicate_of. Open design questions for the brief: threshold tuning (false-positive noise on every record?), embedding availability at record time (embeddings may still be loading — advisory must degrade to absent, never block), same-type-only vs cross-type. FLAGGED FOR COLTON'S ASYNC REVIEW before implementation: this is a behavior-surface addition adjacent to a permanently-rejected feature class; Vince judges it clearly on the allowed side (passive return field) but wants the human ruling recorded before spending implementation effort. Gate D scenario 2's write half stays open until either this ships or Colton rules the write-time half out of scope (in which case S2 passes on the read half via task cba864702fd7 alone — the scenario claim would be re-scoped).

data.detail: Ran the epic-close consumer audit (task bf6ebeb1e1b6) at pin 38bcb8b per the peer-reviewed plan: built an ISOLATED throwaway graph (scratchpad/gate-d-graph, own git repo, real nomic-embed sidecar embeddings), drove the testable cores (conftest build_lc/make_ctx pattern; contradicts/supersedes edges via CognitionStorage.add_edge on the throwaway instance ONLY — curator simulation, never the MCP tool, never prod), dumped each scenario's real user-facing artifacts, and had two independent sonnet persona evaluators (human team lead; LLM agent) judge them. MATRIX: S1 RELITIGATE — PASS both personas (settled decision with rejected-alternatives text = TOP search hit at 0.82 for the relitigating query AND unprompted in prime's Recent Decisions; strongest scenario). S2 CONFLICT — FAIL both: write-time return is completely bare (only unattributed uncurated count moves); read-time inconsistent (dashboard rows carry conflicted:True, but cognition_search returns the SAME two nodes with no marker). S3 CLAIM COLLISION — split verdict (human PASS/agent FAIL), reconciled PARTIAL: claim_warning returns are excellent (kind + named claimant + prose message), manager prime shows the holder, BUT cognition_list_tasks — the mandated pre-work check — shows owner:None/assigned_to:None for an actively-claimed task (= already-filed Gate B task 8c7bab562c37). S4 SENIORITY ARC — FAIL: weights ARE visible structured fields (junior 0.9 / senior 1.0 — that half passes), but the SUPERSEDED junior hit OUTRANKED its senior correction (0.708 vs 0.688) with no supersession marker in the result — an agent acts on stale info; narrative only via unprompted get_superseded_chain. S5 ONBOARDING — PARTIAL: the New Here prime banner is excellent (exact tool, params, decline path); but the post-registration payoff is invisible — ROOT-CAUSED live: _should_personalize 'auto' gates on >1 distinct stamped WRITER email (deliberate solo-graph byte-identity design), ignoring registered person nodes, so a fresh team's first onboardee gets the global digest; plus no identity-confirmation line exists. FIX TASKS: cba864702fd7 (search provenance flags: conflicted + superseded_by — fixes S2-read + S4, HIGH), 8a06e898d267 (auto-personalize widens to person-node count + one-line identity header, HIGH), f8fcac05f731 (write-time similar_existing advisory — FLAGGED for Colton, adjacent to permanently-rejected interceptor class, advisory-only boundary pinned), 8c7bab562c37 (pre-existing, covers S3). Cross-persona lesson recorded: signal that lives only in raw payload JSON reaches agents but not humans; signal that lives only in dashboards reaches humans but not agents; prime text and structured return fields with prose messages are the only surfaces BOTH consume. Gate stays in_progress; failing scenarios re-run after each fix ships. Throwaway graph retained until fixes land (re-runs need it), then deleted. Honesty notes: cores-not-wire coverage limit + evaluator model-family overlap disclosed in plan doc.
